title: 揭露：環保資料調查
description:
報名請到：[https://www.accupass.com/go/rladies_evironment](https://www.accupass.com/go/rladies_evironment)

**[Agenda]**
19:00 \~ 19:30 開放入場
19:30 \~ 19:40 開場
19:40 \~ 20:40 揭露：環保資料與行動
20:4 0\~ 自由交流時間

**[Talk Information]**
面對全球環境挑戰，環保工作不應僅依賴直覺或情感驅動，而是需要建立在紮實的數據基礎上，以有效推動環境保護的議題。在大數據時代，我們可以從政府開放數據、衛星影像、以及網路爬蟲等技術中獲取大量資料，並結合民眾參與和實地調查，取得寶貴的第一手資料。這些數據不僅能為環境倡議提供科學依據，還能使我們更精準地識別環境問題並提出有效的解決方案。
本次分享會將探討國際環保組織如何透過數據的力量推動環境倡議。我們將分享如何運用數據分析，揭示漁船過度捕撈對海洋的壓力，透過公民科學倡導塑膠污染源頭減量，以及將百年氣候數據轉化為吸引大眾關注的氣候變遷倡議。歡迎對環保議題或資料分析感興趣的朋友參加，一起來聽聽數據如何成為環保行動的力量來源。

**[Speaker Information]**
***簡妙蓉 研究員***
我是一個擁有GIS與自然科學背景的雜食性 programmer，擁有幾年的程式設計經驗，但很多時間都是在自個鬼打牆。我的起點始於R語言資料分析，因為好奇或身不由己而延伸到 Python 和網站開發。
目前在Greenpeace擔任研究員，但事實上在這裡當志工的時間比到職的時間還要長五倍。每天都體驗著被理想喚醒、被現實擊沉的循環。

**[Host Information]**

* Ben Chen：活動主持人
* Ning Chen：活動規劃
* Kristen Chan: 活動場地規劃

**[活動單位]**

* 主辦單位：[R-Ladies Taipei](https://www.facebook.com/groups/RLadiesTaipei)
* 場地贊助：台北大學統計系

**[活動地點]**

* 臺北市中山區民生東路三段67號 教學大樓 9樓
* 從捷運中山國中站出發，依照 [Google Map 路線](https://maps.app.goo.gl/ChUs67wDu8F9W9Pp9)前往臺北大學民生校區教學大樓。
* 進入教學大樓正門後，搭乘左側電梯至 **九樓**，再依照指示圖（橘線）找到活動場地。

**[線上活動連結]**
**[https://reurl.cc/gDma3b](https://reurl.cc/gDma3b)**

**[注意事項]**
基本上這是一個女性學習社群，因此若你不是女性，但出現在我們的實體聚會的話，你很可能被要求以下行為：

* 發言的機會請優先留給女生
* 座位請優先保留給女生
* 必須對 R-Ladies Taipei 有貢獻，例如：請勇敢舉手發問

title: R-Ladies Singapore: Kick Off Session!
description:
👩‍💻✨ Welcome to our first **R-Ladies Session**! We’re thrilled to have you join us for this journey into the world of R and data analysis.
**Who We Are**
R-Ladies is a global organization that promotes gender diversity in the R community, providing an inclusive, supportive space for women and gender minorities interested in data science, programming, and data-driven storytelling. Whether you’re a seasoned R user or just curious, you’re in the right place!
**What We’ll Cover Today**
We'll start with a quick intro to R, covering the basics so that you feel comfortable working in the environment. Then, we’ll dip our toes into **dplyr**, one of the most popular packages in R for data manipulation, using the classic `iris` dataset. Don’t worry if this is all new; by the end of today’s session, you'll have a toolkit to start exploring data with confidence.
**Session Outline**

1. **Introduction to R**: Setting up, basic commands, and understanding the console.
2. **The `iris` Dataset**: A quick look at our data, and why this is such a popular dataset for learning.
3. **Introduction to `dplyr`**: Learn how to filter, select, arrange, and summarize data using simple, readable code.

**What to Expect**
This session will be interactive! Feel free to ask questions, share your screen, and explore along with us. R is as much about experimentation as it is about coding—don’t be afraid to make mistakes.
Let’s dive in and have some fun! 🌈

title: R en Red: ARcenso, Oportunidades y Comunidad
description:
**¡R-Ladies Buenos Aires junto a R en Buenos Aires te invitan a su última actividad del año!** Cerramos el año celebrando lo logrado en comunidad con dos paneles espectaculares:

* **ARcenso:** [Andrea Gomez Vargas](https://soyandrea.netlify.app/) y Emanuel Ciardullo nos estarán presentando avances y primeros pasos en su experiencia desarrollando el paquete [ARcenso](https://soyandrea.github.io/arcenso/) para la gestión de datos de los censos nacionales de población como parte del programa de campeones y campeonas del software de rOpenSci.
* **Oportunidades y Comunidad:** [María Nanton](https://rladiesba.netlify.app/author/maria-nanton/), [Jesica Formoso](https://rladiesba.netlify.app/author/jesica-formoso/) y [Betsy Cohen](https://rladiesba.netlify.app/author/betsabe-cohen/) de R-Ladies BA nos van a presentar diferentes experiencias y oportunidades de becas, conferencias y experiencias de trabajo dentro de la comunidad de R a nivel local como internacional.

**Además estaremos sorteando algunos regalos y stickers ✨**

⚠️ **Importante**: Los espacios son limitados, por lo que si te anotaste pero no vas a poder asistir, **te pedimos que nos avises con tiempo** para que otra persona pueda participar. ¡Queremos asegurarnos de que todos puedan disfrutar de esta actividad! 🙌

title: EOY In-Person R-Ladies Meetup: Share, Learn, and Connect!
description:
Join us for our final R-Ladies event of the year! This in-person gathering is a fantastic opportunity to:

* Practice presenting your R-focused projects or work in a supportive environment.
* Gain valuable insights and feedback from a diverse group of peers.
* Network and celebrate the year with fellow R enthusiasts over dinner.

**Why present your work?**
Use this as your safe and welcoming space to hone your skills and learn from others. This is a great way to:
• **Increase your visibility** in the R community and showcase your work.
• **Enhance your public speaking skills** in a friendly and constructive setting.
• **Build confidence** in sharing your ideas and discussing your methods.
• Receive **constructive feedback** to refine your project or communication.
• Connect with like-minded individuals who share your interests in R programming.

After the presentations, we’ll head to King Street for a relaxed dinner and networking.

**How to Participate**:

1. **Present your work**: If you’d like to present, please let us know by answering our questions!
2. **Attend the event**: RSVP to secure your spot.

**Cost**:
Free to attend the event; dinner will be at your own expense.

See you there!

***R-Ladies Sydney organisers***

***Thank you Nous Group for supporting the RLadies!***
*Find us on X @rladiessydney and on [LinkedIn](https://www.linkedin.com/groups/14405806/) for more information on news, presentations and code!*

title: Božićna radionica o moderacijama u R-u
description:
Je li utjecaj poklona na blagdansko raspoloženje ovisan o satima uloženog truda u njihovo pakiranje? Ovisi li povezanost cijene kuhanog vina na adventu i broja kupljenih čaša o trenutnoj temperaturi zraka?

Odgovori na ova pitanja mogu se dobiti upravo provedbom moderacijskih analiza!

🎁 Što ćeš naučiti?

* Teorijske pojedinosti o moderacijskoj analizi
* Kako pripremiti podatke za provedbu moderacijske analize u R-u
* Testirati i interpretirati interakcijske efekte u R-u
* Kako provesti moderacijsku analizu u specifičnim scenarijima

📌 Kako se prijaviti?

Link na prijavu: http://bit.ly/4g2ZcW6

title: Lunch & Learn: An Introduction to Survival Analysis in R
description: This talk will provide a general introduction to survival analysis in R. After introducing the concept of and need for survival analysis methods, we will first discuss how to format a dataset properly to conduct a survival analysis, including working with dates, calculating date differences, and coding events and censoring. Then, we will learn how to perform basic survival analyses in R including plotting Kaplan-Meier curves and fitting Cox regression models. We will discuss the proper ways to interpret the results, and some of the common ways survival analysis results are mis-interpreted.

title: Graficando con R base
description:
**Detalles**

Alguna vez has querido mejorar el diseño de tus graficos unicamente con las paqueterias base de R?

Cuando queremos hacer un grafico para presumirlo en nuestra tesis y articulos lo primero que pensamos es en el sin fin de herramientas que nos brinda la libreria ggplot2. Sin embargo, cuando uno empieza a dar sus primeros pasos en R, los graficos base de R proporcionan una variedad de funciones que facilitan y resaltan el diseño de graficos coloridos y ordenados. Además una vez comprendido el uso de algunas funciones de graficos R base facilita el uso de otras librerias con mayor numero de funciones para el diseño de graficos.

La finalidad de este meetup es realizar cuatro tipos de graficos con R base y explorar algunas funciones basicas para mejorar el diseño de nuestros gráficos. R base maneja una variedad de graficos por lo que para este taller se explicará brevemente como hacer graficos de barra, histogramas, cajas de bigote y regresiones lineales.

**Imparte**
Katia Hernández Bolaños
María José Mondragón

**Requerimientos**

Tener instalado R >= 4.0
Tener instalado RStudio >= 1.4
Tener git instalado

**Código de conducta**
Recuerda leer y atender nuestro código de conducta disponible en https://rladiesmx.netlify.app/codigo_de_conducta/

title: Using GitHub Copilot (and others LLM) with R
description:
Join us for an exciting and insightful session with **Yanina Bellini Saibene**, as she explores the integration of **GitHub Copilot** (and other **Large Language Models**, LLMs) with **R**!

**In her own words**:
*“In this talk, I will demonstrate how to use GitHub Copilot within RStudio to code with R. I will also show other large language models (LLMs) and showcase examples of wrangling data and developing R packages. I will also highlight both opportunities and challenges when integrating LLMs into an educational context.”*

The integration of **LLMs** offers both **exciting opportunities** and **complex challenges**, especially within an **educational setting**. Yanina will share her insights into how these technologies can **enhance productivity** and **support learning**, while also discussing the potential risks and considerations when using AI in education.

This session is perfect for anyone curious about how AI-powered tools like GitHub Copilot can **enhance** your R programming experience, whether you’re a beginner or an advanced R user. 🚀

**Why You Should Attend**:
• **Hands-on demonstration** of how to use GitHub Copilot with RStudio.
• Learn how LLMs can assist with **data wrangling** and **R package development**.
• Discuss the **benefits and challenges** of integrating LLMs into the **educational space**.
• Explore ways to **optimize your workflow** and speed up coding tasks with AI support.

This event promises to be both **informative** and **thought-provoking**, and we are excited to have you join us for this session. Don’t miss out on this opportunity to explore the future of coding with R and AI!

title: Construindo seu primeiro dashboard interativo com o {Shiny}
description: O Shiny é um framework para criar aplicativos web de maneira fácil usando código R, sem precisar de qualquer conhecimento de HTML, CSS ou JavaScript. Por outro lado, o Shiny possui componentes de interface de usuário que podem ser facilmente personalizadas ou estendidas, e seu servidor usa programação reativa para permitir que você crie qualquer tipo de lógica de back-end que desejar. Atualmente, o Shiny é usado em quase tantos nichos e indústrias quanto o próprio R. Na academia é usado como um meio chamativo para exibir novos resultados, métodos ou modelos estatísticos e em empresas para configurar painéis de métricas em tempo real que incorporam análises avançadas. Este minicurso foi projetado para levá-lo de não saber nada sobre Shiny para ser capaz de construir dashboards, onde você será capaz de expor seus resultados de maneira interativa que ainda são fáceis de manter e de alto desempenho.

title: R in Research
description: R is a free, open-source statistical programming language used extensively in research and this talk discusses multiple publications of this type. The first relates to sonification of NASA data, the second is simulations necessary when undertaking innovative designs, and the last is a graphical user interface used by researchers to assure balance in cluster randomized trials.

title: ntbox y kuenm - usando R para evaluación de nicho
description:
¡¡¡ATENCION!!!!
Reprogramamos el meetuap en solidaridad con el PARO NACIONAL programado para este jueves 05/12, de modo que quienes iban a asistir puedan participar de las distintas acciones programadas para ese dia.
\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-

R-Ladies Bariloche te invita al último meetup del 2024!! Luego, unas merecidas vacaciones!

Estas empezando o tenes planeado iniciar con modelos de distribución de especies? Paula Quiroga nos cuenta su experiencia usando los paquetes de R ntobx (NicheToolBox) y kuenm. Estos paquetes de R permite llevar a cabo diversos pasos de procesamiento implicados en la modelización de nichos ecológicos, y te serán re útiles cuando arranques tu proyecto.

El taller será presencial y se realizará el jueves 5 de diciembre de 10 hs a 12 hs en el edificio de INIBIOMA, sala consejo (Pasaje Gutierrez 1400, Bariloche).

Para asistir al meetup, primero por favor lean nuestro [Código de conducta](https://github.com/rladies/.github/blob/master/CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md#spanish)

Les recomendamos tener conocimiento de R, qué es un script, cómo llamar paquetes y cargar un archivo de datos.

A quienes asistan, recuerden traer su taza/mate y si quieren, algo para compartir!!

¡Importante! Ni bien se inscriban en el meetup, entren a los ajustes de su perfil y verifiquen tener activas las notificaciones, no sólo del meetup en general, sino del grupo RLadies-BRC en particular (figuran abajo del todo en notificaciones por correo electrónico) para poder recibir notificaciones relacionadas al evento.

Por cualquier consulta bariloche@rladies.org.
Organización R-ladies BRC

title: 10° R-Ladies POA
description:
Olá, gurias!
Estamos preparando mais um encontro para fecharmos o ano!
Não deixe de se inscrever no evento e participar!

Interessada em compartilhar conhecimento? Entra em contato com a gente aqui pela plataforma, vamos adorar te ouvir!!

**Local: Sala de Convivência, DBServer, 5º andar**
**Tecnopuc**
**Av. Ipiranga, 6681 - Partenon, Porto Alegre - RS, 90619-900**
\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-
**Programação:**

**Shiny para emergências: um modelo de gestão de doações e recursos em emergências climáticas**
Resumo: Nos primeiros dias das enchentes de maio de 2024, uma força-tarefa formada por voluntários da comunidade PUCRS foi criada para gerenciar as doações recebidas. Esse esforço resultou na criação de um dashboard no Power BI. Contudo, devido à natureza paga do software, seu uso de forma expandida foi limitado. Para viabilizar o acesso a mais usuários e como forma de retribuição à comunidade, desenvolveu-se um aplicativo no Shiny, que em breve estará disponível para que outras instituições ou grupos possam adotar o modelo de gestão de doações, acompanhamento de recursos e outras funcionalidades.

*Iara Passos* \- Socióloga e Cientista de Dados\. Possui graduação e mestrado em Sociologia pela UFRGS e é graduanda em Estatística na mesma universidade\. Atualmente analista de dados na PUCRS\.

**R e Spark: uma introdução ao sparklyr**
Resumo: Lidar com volumes de dados (realmente) grandes é um desafio cada vez mais frequente no dia a dia de quem analisa dados. Conhecer ferramentas que auxiliam neste processo é essencial para tornar o trabalho mais fácil. Uma dessas ferramentas é o Apache Spark, um framework de processamento de big data de código aberto. Sua integração com o R acontece principalmente pelo pacote sparklyr, mantido pelo time do Posit, que permite uma série de integrações com funções de pacotes como dplyr e tidyr. Nesta introdução, conheceremos o funcionamento do Spark, as particularidades do seu uso, e algumas de suas limitações.

*Bruna Dalmoro* \- Cientista de Dados no Sicredi\, bacharela em Estatística pela UFRGS\, mestra em Ciência da Computação pela PUCRS\.

title: Creación de proyectos en R
description:
***Detalles***
*¿Te has olvidado dònde guardaste tus bases de datos y no sabes que path() darle a tu script en R?*
*¿Te has confundido de "version_final.csv", "version_final_fianal.csv", "version_esta_si_es.csv" ?*
**¡ Olvidate de eso!**
En este meetup aprenderemos a trabajar de forma ordenada en R y Rstudio con la finalidad de tener código reproducible y evitar perder nuestros archivos:

* Crear proyectos con Rstudio
* Organizar tus archivos y carpteas

**Imparte**
Anahí Canedo Téxon

**Requerimientos**

* Tener instalado R >= 4.0
* Tener instalado RStudio >= 1.4
* Crear una cuenta en Git: [https://github.com](https://github.com/)
* Instalar Git, descarga desde aquí: https://gitforwindows.org/
* Instalar paqueterías:
* install.packages("usethis", dependencies = TRUE)
* install.packages("here", dependencies = TRUE)
* install.packages("devtools", dependencies = TRUE)
* install.packages("swirl", dependencies = TRUE)

**Código de conducta**

title: [Meetup ONLINE] Análise de Dados Musicais com R
description:
**CONTEÚDO**
Convidamos você para um evento especial organizado pela **R-Ladies São Paulo**, onde combinaremos análise de dados e música para celebrar artistas negros brasileiros.

🎧 **O que você vai aprender?**
Faremos uma demonstração ao vivo e online utilizando pacotes de processamento de texto para explorar letras de músicas.

📢 **Quem pode participar?**
O evento é aberto para TODAS as pessoas interessadas e será transmitido através do Youtube da R-Ladies São Paulo: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=j3ovejIJmwE&ab_channel=RLadiesS%C3%A3oPaulo

A atividade será expositiva.

\*\*\*
Por favor leia todas as informações abaixo!
• LOCAL DO EVENTO:
Canal das R-Ladies São Paulo no Youtube
• CÓDIGO DE CONDUTA
Disponível aqui: [https://github.com/rladies/.github/blob/master/CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md#Portuguese](https://github.com/rladies/.github/blob/master/CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md#Portuguese)

Qualquer pessoa que violar ou houver previamente violado o código de conduta desta comunidade terá sua entrada proibida, será expulsa/o e/ou estará sujeita/o à ação que a pessoa organizadora achar mais apropriada, independente de inscrição no evento, ser membro do MeetUp ou qualquer outra condição.
\*\*\*
**• SOBRE AS R-LADIES**
R-Ladies é uma organização que promove a diversidade de gênero na comunidade da linguagem R. Nosso principal objetivo é promover a linguagem computacional estatística R compartilhando conhecimento, assim, quem tiver interesse na linguagem será bem-vinda, independente do nível de conhecimento. Nosso principal público-alvo são as pessoas que se identificam com o gênero feminino, portanto, mulheres cis, mulheres trans, bem como pessoas não-binárias e queer.

\*\*\*
O evento é gratuito, e organizado com muito carinho, ajude a espalhar a palavra, compartilhe com outras ladies :)

title: Transforma tus datos en historias visuales con Quarto
description:
**¡Aviso IMPORTANTE!**

Por cuestiones ajenas a RLadies Buenos Aires, **EL MEETUP SE REALIZARA DE MANERA ON LINE.**
Estaremos enviando el enlace a Zoom a todas las personas inscriptas a la brevedad.

\-\-\-
**Acerca de este Meetup**

En esta oportunidad, **Jesica Formoso** nos introducirá a **[Quarto](https://quarto.org/)**, un sistema de publicación de código abierto diseñado para crear documentos científicos y técnicos de manera dinámica y reproducible.
Aprenderemos cómo Quarto expande las capacidades de R Markdown, permitiéndonos generar informes, presentaciones y sitios web. A lo largo del taller, exploraremos ejemplos prácticos de su uso en informes reproducibles, abordando temas como la integración de código y texto, la personalización de formatos de salida y el uso de parámetros para crear variantes de documentos.
Este taller es **introductorio** está dirigida a personas con un **conocimiento básico de R** y **poca o ninguna experiencia previa con Quarto**.
**Requerimientos:**

* R: 4.4.1
* Quarto: v1.5 (descargar quarto CLI [aquí](https://quarto.org/docs/get-started/))
* RStudio: 2024.04.2+764 (descargar [aquí](https://posit.co/downloads/))
* Instalar el paquete **tidyverse**.

Para poder seguir el taller sin inconvenientes, te pedimos que traigas tu computadora preparada, con R y RStudio actualizados, y el quarto CLI y el paquete tidyverse instalados.

Finalmente, te invitamos a compartir el link al meetup para quien quiera inscribirse.
**¡Te esperamos!**

title: Exploring Azure AI Services and Certification Pathways
description:
報名請到：[https://www.accupass.com/go/rladies_azureai](https://www.accupass.com/go/rladies_azureai)

**[Agenda]**
19:00 \~ 19:30 開放入場
19:30 \~ 19:40 開場
19:40 \~ 20:40 Exploring Azure AI Services and Certification Pathways
20:40 \~ 自由交流時間

**[Talk Information]**

帶你一起了解 Azure AI 服務的世界

第一部分：Azure AI 服務網頁應用展示
了解如何利用 Azure AI 服務網頁應用，透過實際 demo 帶你一步步進入 Azure AI 服務的各種實驗和探索，包括 Azure AI 語言服務、Azure AI 語音服務、Azure AI 翻譯服務等。當天會有現場演示，學習如何將這些工具應用於你的專案中。
第二部分：認證與實用技能
對於 Azure AI 服務不熟悉的朋友，沒關係讓我們一起來了解如何通過認證途徑和實用技能來掌握 Azure AI 服務。我們將介紹關鍵認證、學習資源以及在 AI 領域取得卓越表現所需的實際技能。
不要錯過這次機會，通過 Azure 的強大工具和認證提升您的 AI 知識與技能。我們期待與您相見！

**[Speaker Information]**

***Vincent***
Microsoft Technical Trainer
專注於激勵、參與並培養學習者 Azure 雲服務技能的 Microsoft Technical Trainer。熱衷於教授與雲端運算相關的最創新技術，同時也是一位 Microsoft 雲平台的推廣者。Vincent 喜歡與新朋友見面，他的最愛座右銘是 "GET INSPIRED, MAKE GREAT THINGS HAPPEN!"
***Kristen***
Microsoft Technical Trainer
專注於資料與 AI 領域超過 10 年，同時也是 R-Ladies Taipei Co-Organizer。她曾是一名資料科學家，喜歡學習和分享有關機器學習與深度學習的主題。希望我們能夠一起進行愉快的討論！

**[Host Information]**

* Ben Chen：活動主持人
* Ning Chen：活動規劃
* Kristen Chan: 活動場地規劃

**[活動單位]**

[https://reurl.cc/gDma3b](https://reurl.cc/gDma3b)

title: Visualizing Data with R: An R-Ladies Introduction
description:
Join us for the R-Ladies New Delhi Chapter Meetup! This session is designed to introduce new members and attendees to the R programming language and the mission of R-Ladies, a global organization promoting gender diversity in the R community.
The event will feature:

1. **Introduction to R-Ladies and R Programming:** Learn about the community and how R can empower your data analysis skills.
2. **Hands-on Workshop:** Dive into data visualization using popular R libraries.

**What to Bring:**

1. A laptop (optional but encouraged for the hands-on session).
2. **Snacks:** Light snacks will be provided, courtesy of the [Cluster Innovation Centre](https://cic.du.ac.in/), University of Delhi.
This is a great opportunity to learn, network, and explore the potential of R. Beginners and experienced R users alike are welcome!
For any questions, contact Kritika Verma at **kritikaverma2707@gmail.com**.
We look forward to seeing you there!

venues.name: Cluster Innovation Centre
venues.address: Rugby Sevens Building, University Stadium, University of Delhi, G.C Narang Marg, Delhi, 110007

title: Introducción al uso de rgbif
description: En este taller se explora `rgbif`, una herramienta de R diseñada para acceder a la enorme base de datos de biodiversidad del *Global Biodiversity Information Facility* (GBIF). Los participantes aprenderán a descargar, manipular y analizar datos de especies, así como a realizar búsquedas avanzadas y filtrados por localidad, taxonomía y otros criterios de interés. Es ideal para aquellos que desean enriquecer sus estudios de biodiversidad y análisis ecológicos con datos abiertos y de fácil acceso.

title: ¿Qué hay en mi data frame?
description:
## Detalles

En este meetup revisaremos algunas fucniones de Rbase para extraer información de una dataframe, con la intención de practicar ejercicios cortos para ganar más habilidad en R:

* Función concatenar: c()
* Crear listas y acceder a sus elementos
* Cargar un dataframe y acceder a sus elementos
* Extraer información con funciones de Rbase: ncol(), str(), colMean(),max(), min()
* ¿Podemos graficar mi dataframe?

**Imparte**
Anahí Canedo Téxon
**Requerimientos**

* Tener instalado R >= 4.0
* Tener instalado RStudio >= 1.4

**Código de conducta**
Recuerda leer y atender nuestro código de conducta disponible en [https://rladiesmx.netlify.app/codigo_de_conducta/](https://rladiesmx.netlify.app/codigo_de_conducta/)

title: ¿Tenes que hacer un GAMM? Te cuento mi experiencia con series de crecimiento
description:
R-Ladies Bariloche te invita a otro meetup y no te lo podés perder!!

En esta oportunidad vamos a estar explorando un poco los GAMMs, o los Modelos Aditivos Generalizados Mixtos. El objetivo de este meetup es compartir una experiencia utilizando GAMM para anlizar series de crecimiento anual de árboles. Veremos como armar el modelo considerando factores fijos y aleatorios, predictores continuos y categoricos, y como graficar el efecto parcial de nuestro predictor de interes.

**IMPORTANTE**! Este Meetup es continuación de un excelente meetup de R-Ladies Buenos Aires en colaboración con R-Ladies Ushuaia a cargo de María Victoria Quiroga. Te recomendamos que lo veas antes de venir por que esta buenisimo! [https://youtu.be/BqKRkNaefzE?si=NCg0KNXaS0y6c4Q1 ](https://youtu.be/BqKRkNaefzE?si=NCg0KNXaS0y6c4Q1)

El taller será **presencial** y se realizará el jueves 21 de noviembre de 10 hs a 12 hs en el edificio de INIBIOMA (Pasaje Gutierrez 1415, Bariloche), en el segundo piso "sala consejo" .

Para asistir al meetup, primero por favor lean nuestro **[Código de conducta](https://github.com/rladies/.github/blob/master/CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md#spanish)**

Les recomendamos tener conocimiento de R, qué es un script, cómo llamar paquetes y cargar un archivo de datos.
A quienes asistan, recuerden traer su taza/mate y si quieren, algo para compartir!!
¡**Importante**! Ni bien se inscriban en el meetup, entren a los ajustes de su perfil y verifiquen tener activas las notificaciones, no sólo del meetup en general, sino del grupo RLadies-BRC en particular (figuran abajo del todo en notificaciones por correo electrónico) para poder recibir notificaciones relacionadas al evento.

Por cualquier consulta bariloche@rladies.org.
Organización R-ladies BRC

title: Leveraging the Power of Data in Sport
description:
R Ladies Melbourne is very excited to announce our next meetup event. We will be welcoming two data analytics experts, Elisa Koch and Lauren Boothby, who both work at the Australian Football League and will be joining us to discuss *Leveraging the Power of Data in Sport.*

Elisa has worked as a Data & Analytics leader in the US and Australia in industries spanning finance, FMCG, technology, and sport. Currently, she is the Head of Data and Analytics at the Australian Football League (AFL). She is passionate about leading businesses to be more data driven at all levels of the business. Elisa is a people-first leader passionate about building high-performing teams built on diverse thinking and the ability to bring our authentic selves to work. Elisa serves as an Advisory Board Member for the Institute of Analytic Professionals Australia and is on the board of Spectrum Migrant Resource Centre, a non-profit focused on enabling people with migrant and refugee backgrounds to feel at home in Australia.

Lauren is a Senior Data Analyst and joined the Data and Analytics team at the AFL in 2023 from North Melbourne where she spent over five years working in both the football department and strategy team as a data analyst. Previous to this, Lauren has experience working with the West Coast Fever Netball Team and the West Coast Eagles Football Club.

Please join us on Wednesday 20th November 2024 at Dius Offices Melbourne from 5.30pm. We will first have a casual networking catch-up with some light refreshments, and the presentation will start at 6pm. You will also be able to join this event online if you are not able to join us in person. Please register your attendance (both in-person and online) on Meetup by the 14th November for planning purposes.

We look forward to seeing you there!

R Ladies Melbourne

title: Openscapes: Forking as a Worldview
description:
**Forking as a Worldview: Scaling Solutions in Open Science with Environmental and Earth Science**

Join us for an enlightening session with **[Julia Stewart Lowndes](https://www.linkedin.com/in/julia-stewart-lowndes/)**, the Director of **[Openscapes](https://openscapes.org/)**, as she delves into the transformative concept of “forking” in the realm of open science. Originally rooted in software development, forking has evolved into a powerful approach for addressing collaborative challenges across government and academia.

Julia will share her insights on how forking goes beyond mere code adaptation; it embodies a mindset that promotes innovation, credit-sharing, and collective progress. Through her work with Openscapes and NASA, Julia has built communities that empower teams to transition their workflows to the cloud and beyond.

In her own words:

“*Forking is a concept from software development that has evolved with open source software and the internet. It means taking something that works and is made available, then adapting it for your own use while staying connected to the source. This networking allows for contributions back to the original work and ensures proper credit is given. While forking traditionally involves code, it can apply to virtually anything—it’s about taking what works to new places to solve problems more efficiently.*

*Since 2018, Openscapes has partnered with teams in government and academia to help them tackle significant collaborative challenges through open science. One of our primary focuses at NASA Openscapes has been upskilling users of NASA Earthdata as they transition their workflows to cloud computing.*

*We’ve fostered a mentor community across NASA Earth science data centers (DAACs) to co-create tutorials and develop teaching, mentoring, and facilitation skills. Along the way, we’ve forked numerous resources, including code, documentation, workshop materials, presentations, and even entire programs like NOAA Fisheries Openscapes and Pathways to Open Science.*

*Ultimately, we’ve discovered that forking represents a way of thinking—a worldview. Recognizing forking as an essential element of our collaborative innovation efforts in open science is crucial, and I am eager to discuss it with all of you!*”

**Don’t miss this unique opportunity to learn from Julia Stewart Lowndes, a thought leader in open science. Whether you’re interested in cloud-based workflows, open data, or fostering innovation through collaboration, this talk is sure to provide valuable insights and inspiration.**

title: R-Ladies NYC November Book Club!
description:
R-Ladies NYC will be hosting a book club in November! We will be reading **Atlas of AI: Power, Politics, and the Planetary Costs of Artificial Intelligence by Kate Crawford**. We will be meeting on Saturday, November 16th at 2pm at [Book Club Bar](https://maps.app.goo.gl/r4CMEfjHdFWJnpA68) in the East Village.

Please RSVP so we can get a rough headcount. See you there!

Hi all - I am here in a plaid blazer and brown backpack waiting for a space to open up. Come say hi if you’re here!

Link to the book at [Bookshop](https://bookshop.org/p/books/atlas-of-ai-power-politics-and-the-planetary-costs-of-artificial-intelligence-kate-crawford/17465404?ean=9780300264630&gad_source=1&gclid=Cj0KCQjwjY64BhCaARIsAIfc7YbJU-m0--_SxDnwT0hUXAwgdHlDlWMBpZ1V4W5P7xpYMwexcY1UNVAaAmFIEALw_wcB).

title: Visualising data with ggplot2 – Preparing figures for publication and outreach
description:
The visualisations we use to present our findings matter as much as the text, if not more. We will walk through the steps to create beautiful figures that can make posters, talks, and publications shine. If you can picture a cool way to visualize data, it can probably be achieved with ggplot2!

This workshop is presented by Ryssa. She is a postdoc in Social Brain Sciences at ETH Zurich. Her research focuses on how the brain and body mediate the development of intergenerational social bonds, and how this can reduce loneliness for people of all ages.

**Programm:**
18:30-20:15 workshop
20:15 - onwards socializing and drinks

**Location:**
University of Zurich (KOL-H-309)

We are looking forward to having a great evening together!

Seraphina, Céline, Luisa, Elena and Ryssa!

title: Centralized database management with AWS and R/Shiny
description:
Join us for our next meetup in November, to hear from **Ilaria Capelli** and **Mina Sohrabi** about their work at Raiffeisen Bank International. We will explore how their new R package simplifies querying their centralized database through a secure **AWS** setup and discover their intuitive **R/Shiny** interface for easy data access and updates. They will showcase features like Excel-like operations via **rhandsontable**, designed to enhance usability for both technical and non-technical users. Perfect for analysts and clients looking to smoothly interact with macroeconomic data!

**Abstract**:
As developers at a leading economic and financial markets research institution, centralizing our data is crucial for both internal (backtesting, sharing forecasts, model validations) and external (client access) use cases. Our contribution consists of two parts: first, the introduction of our R package, developed using the “Open API generator,” enabling seamless querying of our database via a RESTful API. Additionally, we show the integration of a robust authorization and authentication layer within AWS cloud infrastructure. Second, we have designed a user-friendly R/Shiny-based frontend to facilitate easy access for non-technical users. This interface displays forecasts and historic data for various variables and countries, allowing for data updates and downloads. To mimic familiar Excel operations such as row-wise pasting and data removal, we have successfully utilized the open-source R-wrapper rhandsontable of the JavaScript library handsontable. Both contributions enable our analysts and clients to smoothly interact with our database by providing a convenient, secure, and user-friendly environment, enhancing the accessibility of our macroeconomic data for all stakeholders.

title: ¿Qué onda con el R?
description:
En este meetup abordaremos temas para principiantes de R desde un enfoque amigable y relajado:

* Visibilizar la comunidad de RLadies a nivel mundial
* Lógica de la programación con Scratch (Imparte: María José Mondragón).
* Te contamos en 60 minutos todo lo que necesitas saber la primera vez que abres R: Creación de objetos, Instalación de paquetes que viven en CRAN, Establecer directorios de trabajo, Cargar un archivo csv.

**Imparte**
Anahí Canedo Téxon
**Requerimientos**

* Tener instalado R >= 4.0
* Tener instalado RStudio >= 1.4

**Código de conducta**
Recuerda leer y atender nuestro código de conducta disponible en [https://rladiesmx.netlify.app/codigo_de_conducta/](https://rladiesmx.netlify.app/codigo_de_conducta/)

title: ggplot2_v2
description:
Na tokratni delavnici bomo raziskovali, kako smiselno in učinkovito predstaviti podatke z uporabo paketa ggplot2 v R verzija 2.
Na delavnici bomo set podatkov v parih/trojicah poskušali čim bolj smiselno predstavit. Prvi bo pisal, drugi pa narekal in potem obratno! Učili se bomo drug od drugega skozi pisanje kode.
\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-
In this workshop, we will explore how to meaningfully and effectively present data using the ggplot2 package in R. One person will dictate another write the code, and then we switch. We will learn from each other through code writing.

**The workshop will take place exclusively in person!**

title: Tips and Tricks! (POSTPONED to Wednesday 11/13)
description:
**UPDATE 11/6, 3:15pm PST**

To our R-Ladies community --

While we were very much looking forward to sharing community-curated R tips & tricks with you all tonight, we have decided to postpone this event to **next Wednesday 11/13 (6:00pm PST/4:00pm HST).**

We are processing and grieving the outcome of this election, and recognize that many of our fellow R-Ladies are navigating complex emotions as well. We are so grateful for this community, and the support that each of our members brings to our events. We are looking forward to connecting and learning with you all next week.

Thank you for understanding, and please don’t hesitate to reach out if you need support or want to connect.

\-\-\-

Hello, R-Ladies Santa Barbara!

Do you have a tip, trick, or shortcut that makes your R experience better? We want to hear all about it! If you've been with us for a while, you might remember our successful "R Tips and Tricks" event a few years ago. We loved it, and learned so many helpful tidbits that we want to do it again - but this time, as a **collaboration with R-Ladies Honolulu!** Join us for a virtual meetup on **Wednesday, November 6 from 6-7:30 PT.**

**To make this event happen, we need your help! Please submit your tip or trick using this [form](https://docs.google.com/forms/d/11LELy3CqWj8cB8zDS-OLH4o1oRWb8__k7tgo9vI4O6c/edit?ts=66edadda), and we'll put together a slide.** During the event, you have the option to take a minute or two to present your slide, OR one of the R-Ladies co-organizers can present the slide if you prefer. Check out the [slide deck](https://docs.google.com/presentation/d/13ff2h-9JEd-ziRua8i5Akaypp0br8BOUXQzO6KtaZA8/edit#slide=id.g77e18ccd7e_0_0) from our last Tips and Tricks event for inspiration - tips can range from R packages, functions, or shortcuts to handy workflow tools that may not be R-specific.

**We will send out Zoom information to all registered attendees closer to the event.**
We hope to see you in November to hear about all the tips and tricks used by R-Ladies in Santa Barbara and Honolulu. Who knows - you might learn something new that you won't be able to code without!

Danielle, Camila, and Sam

title: The Art of the Plot: Applying Data Viz Principles in R to Maximize your Impact
description: In this session, we'll take an Excel default-style chart and transform it into a visually engaging and impactful plot using R. Together, we’ll walk through key data visualization principles, simplify cluttered visuals, and make data-driven insights stand out. Get ready to go from “meh” to memorable in just a few lines of code. Let's make data visualizations that are not only informative but impossible to forget!

title: R-Ladies Monthly Happy Hour
description:
Join R-Ladies Seattle for our monthly happy hour! Come and network with local R-Ladies to make new connections and discuss all things R. This is a casual event so you can stop by any time. You'll find us by our logo (and probably Kim's rainbow hair).

Chips and salsa provided!

Non-alcoholic and alcoholic beverages available. Attendees are responsible for their own tab.

title: Holiday special | Presenting your work to non-scientists
description:
We bring you a holiday special in collaboration with Project Bridge -- *how do you present your work to non-scientists over holiday dinners?*

The workshop will focus on communicating research with general audiences!

**MATERIALS**
[https://github.com/rladies-aurora/202411-scicomm](https://github.com/rladies-aurora/202411-scicomm)

**ZOOM LINK**
[https://ucdenver.zoom.us/j/93885644391?pwd=mFQcWKrnSkWqsOkeJGtJG8K7HfaGW0.1](https://ucdenver.zoom.us/j/93885644391?pwd=mFQcWKrnSkWqsOkeJGtJG8K7HfaGW0.1)

**SPONSOR**
Project Bridge Colorado

**More info:** The goal of the [Project Bridge](https://www.cuanschutz.edu/services/project-bridge) organization is to bridge the communication gap between scientists and their communities by creating and supporting opportunities to foster public interest in scientific research. This collaborative workshop, hosted by R Ladies and Project Bridge Colorado, will offer valuable tips and tricks for communicating science to non-scientific audiences. Many scientists struggle to explain their work without relying on technical jargon, as it often feels like a second language. In this workshop, we will explore effective strategies to present complex research in a way that resonates with the general public.

title: Aprende a crear tus primeras funciones usando R
description:
Se viene un nuevo meetup y no te lo podés perder. Si te la pasas copiando y pegando código muchas veces este taller es para vos.
El objetivo de este meetup es aprender desde cero cómo crear nuestras propias funciones que no están en ningún paquete de R.

El taller será presencial y se realizará el martes 12 de noviembre de 10 hs a 12 hs en el edificio de INIBIOMA, sala seminarios (Pasaje Gutierrez 1400, Bariloche).

Para asistir al meetup, primero por favor lean nuestro **[Código de conducta](https://github.com/rladies/.github/blob/master/CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md#spanish)**
Les recomendamos tener conocimiento de R, qué es un script, cómo llamar paquetes y cargar un archivo de datos.
A quienes asistan, recuerden traer su taza/mate y si quieren, algo para compartir!!
¡Importante! Ni bien se inscriban en el meetup, entren a los ajustes de su perfil y verifiquen tener activas las notificaciones, no sólo del meetup en general, sino del grupo RLadies-BRC en particular (figuran abajo del todo en notificaciones por correo electrónico) para poder recibir notificaciones relacionadas al evento.
Por cualquier consulta bariloche@rladies.org.
Organización R-ladies BRC

title: Iniciando com R: dos fundamentos da programação à manipulação de dados
description:
💜

**Sobre a oficina**
Esta oficina foi pensada para pessoas de gêneros minoritários que estão começando no mundo da programação e querem aprender os primeiros passos na linguagem R! Vamos explorar desde a instalação do R e do RStudio até conceitos básicos de programação, como variáveis, operadores, condicionais, laços, estruturas e manipulação de dados.

**Programação do evento**
14:00 Introdução ao R e Rstudio
14:15 Conceitos básicos de programação
15:00 Estrutura de controle simples
15:45 Introdução a funções
16:15 Estrutura de dados básica
17:00 Prática guiada e desafios

**Local do evento:**
Online: via Meet

**O que precisa para participar do evento?**
O evento é totalmente gratuito, mas para participar online, é necessário ter acesso a um notebook, uma conta google e acesso à internet.

**Código de conduta**
Para participar do evento, você deve concordar com o[ código de conduta da comunidade](https://github.com/rladies/.github/blob/master/CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md#portuguese).

**Sobre R-Ladies**
O R-Ladies é uma organização mundial, com a missão de promover a diversidade de gênero na comunidade R e no mercado da tecnologia como um todo. O foco principal do R-Ladies é nos incentivar a alcançarmos nosso potencial, construindo uma rede global colaborativa de líderes, mentoras, aprendizes e desenvolvedoras para facilitar o progresso individual e coletivo de mulheres (e pessoas de outros gêneros minoritários) em todo o mundo!

**Gostaria de ministrar uma oficina/palestra?**
Preencha [esse formulário](https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSdVXRNZGm4x7f71cPueBQrCDy8mwmtpoiDsgJvOVjgjiqh7pg/viewform).
Você também pode ajudar divulgando o evento e convidando mais pessoas a se juntarem à nós.

Siga nossas redes para ficar por dentro de todos os eventos:
Instagram: @rladies.floripa
Twitter: @rladiesfloripa
Site do Meetup: https://www.meetup.com/pt-BR/rladies-florianopolis/

E-mail: florianopolis@rladies.org

title: Introduction to Positron
description:
Join us for an interactive demo / overview of Positron, the new "polyglot" data science IDE from Posit, the makers of RStudio. This walkthrough will include coverage of relative benefits and drawbacks compared to RStudio, with an emphasis on how Positron might bring new customizability and convenience to your R data workflows. (Please note that Positron is still very firmly in beta! This talk is intended to introduce and enable confident exploration of this nascent IDE.)

Link will be provided closer to the event.

Want to learn more about Positron, check out these resources.

1\. https://www\.appsilon\.com/post/introducing\-positron
2\. https://drmowinckels\.io/blog/2024/positron/
3\. https://www\.andrewheiss\.com/blog/2024/07/08/fun\-with\-positron/
4\. https://github\.com/posit\-dev/positron/wiki\#is\-positron\-for\-me
5\. https://positron\.posit\.co/
6\. https://www\.superdatascience\.com/podcast/817
7\. https://www\.youtube\.com/watch?v=8uRcB34Hhsw

title: Positron: un entorno de programación integrado innovador para R y Python
description:
Únete a nosotros este miércoles 6 de noviembre a las 6:00 pm en una conferencia con **Brandon Martínez**, estadístico de la Universidad Nacional de Panamá y entusiasta del análisis de datos. Brandon nos hablará sobre **Positron**, un entorno integrado para R y Python, ideal para optimizar el flujo de trabajo en programación y análisis.
¡Una gran oportunidad para aprender sobre Positron y explorar nuevas herramientas en ciencia de datos!

**Detalles del evento**
📅 Fecha: Miércoles 6 de noviembre
⏰ Hora: 6:00 pm
🔗 Link de Zoom: [https://udea.zoom.us/j/91207732798](https://udea.zoom.us/j/91207732798)
¡Te esperamos para una sesión llena de inspiración y conocimiento!

title: Make your analysis shine with {gtsummary}
description:
Today, our RLadies Sydney co-organiser *Kristy* will tell us everything she knows about the gtsummary R package!

The ***{gtsummary}*** package provides an **elegant and flexible way to create publication-ready analytical and summary tables** using the **R** programming language. The {gtsummary} package summarizes data sets, regression models, and more, using sensible defaults with highly customizable capabilities.

We look forward to seeing you online or in-person :)

title: Book club: Happy Git and GitHub for the useR - kick-off session
description:
Interested in streamlining your workflow by connecting Git and R? Join our **book club** for “**[Happy Git and GitHub for the useR](https://happygitwithr.com/)”** by Jennifer (Jenny) Bryan! The book describes the use of the version control system Git and the hosting site GitHub for statistical and data scientific workflows in R.

After our engaging sessions on “Building Reproducible Analytical Pipelines with R” by R-Ladies Bergen and “Hands-On Machine Learning with R” by R-Ladies Den Bosch, we're excited to team up for a joint book club, together with R-Ladies Amsterdam!

Jenny Bryan herself will introduce the book and be available for questions during the kick-off session!

**When**: Kicking off on Wednesday, November 6, 2024, at 16:30 CET (Den Bosch/Bergen/Amsterdam time) – thereafter 17.00 CET every 2 weeks
**Where**: Zoom (link available for attendees)
**What to expect?**

* Book Club Format: Every two weeks, we’ll work through each chapter together. To make this a group effort, the idea is that all participants take turns in presenting chapters. Participants are encouraged to present chapter summaries, but listening in is equally welcome.
* Presentations summarizing key points from each chapter
* Open discussions for questions, insights, and collaborative learning

Feel free to join us at any session, even if you miss the first one. We’ll explore “**[Happy Git and GitHub for the useR](https://happygitwithr.com/)”** together – learning is better in good company! You do not have to prepare anything for the kick-off session.

**Code of Conduct**
R-Ladies is committed to a welcoming, harassment-free experience for everyone. We have a strict Code of Conduct and do not tolerate harassment in any form. See the [code of conduct](https://rladies.org/coc/)

**Cross Posting**
This event will be cross posted on the [R-Ladies Den Bosch](https://www.meetup.com/rladies-den-bosch/events/304310072) and [R-Ladies Amsterdam](https://www.meetup.com/rladies-amsterdam/events/304310352/) meet-up pages.

title: Let me GitHub Search that for you
description:
There's an amazing amount of R code on GitHub, including the source of all CRAN packages and the source of R itself. As a result, lots of intermediate-to-advanced questions about R programming can be at least partially answered via an appropriate code search on GitHub. I must do this multiple times per day and I am sure that I am not alone. I'll share some tips on how GitHub code search can be a very effective technique for self-help for an R programmer.

Yes, this event will be recorded and posted on the R-Ladies Philly YouTube channel.

title: Reprogramado: Club de lectura Fundamentos de ciencia de datos con R - Sesión 10
description:
¡Nos emociona invitarles a nuestro club de lectura! 📚 Para esta edición, utilizaremos el libro **Fundamentos de Ciencia de Datos con R**, disponible [aquí](https://cdr-book.github.io/index.html).
En la décima sesión del club de lectura, José Navarro, científico de datos, nos hablará sobre el capítulo 36,9.
Recuerda que al participar en nuestros eventos, aceptas nuestro [código de conducta](https://rladies.org/coc/), el cual nos ayuda a crear un espacio amable y seguro para aprender.

title: Shiny-Phyloseq comunidades microbianas
description:
Haremos ejemplos de la librería Shiny-Phyloseq

title: Open Source Software and The Government
description:
🔔 **Important Update for Our Attendees!** 🔔

The event will be at 6:30pm CET / 1:30pm EDT
\-\-\-\-

Are you interested in how open source software can transform government operations? Join us for an insightful event featuring **Dr. Gwynn Gebeyehu**, who will explore the pivotal role of open source solutions in the public sector.

In her own words:
*“Open-source software (OSS) is a critical part of machine learning, artificial intelligence, and big data. However, it comes with **ABSOLUTELY NO WARRANTY**, which poses challenges for risk-averse governments. Additionally, historical procurement practices related to acquisition, evaluation, and lobbying complicate matters. This talk discusses one approach to centering OSS within governmental institutions.”*

From enhancing transparency to fostering innovation, open source technology has the potential to reshape public service as we know it. This is a unique opportunity to discover how technology can positively impact the public sector. **Don’t miss out—reserve your spot now and prepare for an inspiring session!**

title: Random projection for regression analysis in R
description:
We hope you have all had a great summer, and are now ready to meet fellow R-Ladies and learn new things again! For our first event after the summer we will welcome our own **Laura Vana-Gür** who will talk about how we can use random projection for regression analysis in R using the SPAR package (https://github.com/RomanParzer/SPAR).

**Abstract**:
Random projection is a powerful and important tool for dimensionality reduction where a set of high-dimensional points is linearly mapped onto a lower dimension. Random projection matrices can be rapidly generated and are oblivious to the data distribution, maintain interpretability and are equipped with theoretical guarantees on preserving the geometry of the original space with a high probability. When employed in a supervised setting, they can provide a significant reduction in computational cost. However, they tend to overfit so it is desirable to first eliminate the unimportant predictors and then perform the random projection and estimate the model on the space of the reduced (i.e., projected) predictors. Moreover, to reduce the uncertainty from the random projection, ensembles can be built. In this work we propose an R package which implements a variety of random projection and screening tools for regression in high-dimensional settings. The functionality of the package is presented using simulated and real data examples.

title: Club de lectura Fundamentos de ciencia de datos con R - Minería de textos

¡Nos emociona invitarl@s a nuestro próximo meetup organizado por RLadies Barranquilla!

Linda Cabrera, analista de datos y coorganizadora de RLadies Guayaquil, nos guiará a través de este capítulo dedicado a la **minería de textos**. Aprenderemos sobre técnicas clave para procesar, analizar y visualizar información extraída de textos utilizando R.

Es una oportunidad ideal para profundizar en el procesamiento del lenguaje natural y la transformación de datos no estructurados en insights valiosos.

title: R-Ladies of HorroR: A Spooky Data Night
description:
**Hello Ghoulish R Ladies of Honolulu!**

**We’ve got not one, but TWO fang-tastically exciting events coming your way! ✨**

**First up, as the fall chills creep in and spooky season takes hold, we’re conjuring a special Halloween-themed event! For our third gathering, we’ll be working with a horror movie dataset from TidyTuesday’s archives - guaranteed to send shivers down your spine! Get ready to dive into the eerie world of data, conjuring up spooking visualizations and exploring frightful insights. It’s a great opportunity to hone your skills by asking questions, exploring the dataset, and casting your ggplot magic to create some hauntingly good graphs! 📊🎃**

**Whether you’re brewing up your first plot or you’re a seasoned witch casting data spells, ALL levels are welcome! Beginners can learn the basics of data exploration and visualization, while our data wizards can show off their scariest plots. 🧙‍♀️✨**

**🔮 RSVP by October 22st - and don’t hesitate to reach out with any questions!**

**🕸️When: October 23rd, 5:30 pm - 7:00 pm HST**
**🕷️ Where: Entrepreneur’s Sandbox**

**But wait, there’s more!**

**Our second event is a virtual meetup in collaboration with R-Ladies Santa Barbara! 🎉 On Wednesday, November 6th, from 4:00-5:30 PM HST, we’re hosting a Tips & Tricks session where you can share your magical R wisdom! Do you have a clever shortcut, a useful function, or a package that you can’t code without? We want to hear about it!**

**Help us make this event fun by submitting your tip or trick using our [form](https://docs.google.com/forms/d/11LELy3CqWj8cB8zDS-OLH4o1oRWb8__k7tgo9vI4O6c/viewform?ts=66edadda&edit_requested=true). During the event, you’ll have the chance to present your slide (just 1-2 minutes), or if you prefer, one of our R-Ladies co-organizers can present for you!**

**🗓️When: November 6th, 4:00 - 5:30 pm HST**
**💻Where: Virtual (Zoom link will be shared with RSVP’d guests before hand)**

**Need inspiration? Check out the slides from R-Ladies SB’s last [Tips and Tricks](https://docs.google.com/presentation/d/13ff2h-9JEd-ziRua8i5Akaypp0br8BOUXQzO6KtaZA8/edit#slide=id.g77e18ccd7e_0_0) event - anything from R packages and functions to handy workflow tools is fair game!**

**We’re looking forward to seeing you in October and November - whether you’re creating spooky visuals or brewing up R tips, it’s going to be fun! Who knows you might just learn something good that you won’t be able to code without!**

**All the best,**
**Jayslen, Maddie, and Juliette**

title: posit::conf(2024) Watch Party
description:
**2024 Posit Conference Watch Party with R-Ladies Ottawa**

Posit, the company that makes many of the tools you know and love (RStudio, Shiny, the tidyverse, and many more!) hosts an annual conference. This year, the conference took place in Seattle from August 12th-14th. Did you miss the conference, but still want to know what’s new in the world of data science? Look no further – R-Ladies Ottawa is hosting a **posit::conf(2024) Watch Party**!

This event will take place on October 23rd, from 5:30-7:30pm, at the Ottawa Public Library (Sunnyside branch - Program room 1B). Attendance is **FREE** and there will be door prizes available to win (see details below)! We’ll watch a few of the most popular talks from this year’s conference and have opportunities to discuss and network. Afterwards, we’ll be heading to a restaurant in the Glebe for anyone who would like to join!

**Please note that the mission of R-Ladies is to increase gender diversity in the R community.** While our Meetup Group is open to anyone to join, our events are intended to provide a safe space for women and gender minorities. Male allies may attend our in-person events **if they are invited by and accompanied by a woman or gender minority.**

**Door prize giveaway**

By attending this event, you’ll have a chance to win a copy of David Keyes’ new book, R for the Rest of Us: A Statistics-Free Introduction, courtesy of NoStarch Press.

Description of the book: “Learn how to use R for everything from workload automation and creating online reports, to interpreting data, map making, and more.

Written by the founder of a very popular online training platform for the R programming language!

The R programming language is a remarkably powerful tool for data analysis and visualization, but its steep learning curve can be intimidating for some. If you just want to automate repetitive tasks or visualize your data, without the need for complex math, R for the Rest of Us is for you.”

**Free stickers!**

Posit has generously donated hex stickers for this event! Everyone who attends will be able to take some home.

**Event schedule**

5:30pm - 7:00pm - Watch party and discussion (see talk descriptions below!)
7:00pm - Networking and announcement of door prize winner
7:30pm - We’ll be heading to a restaurant in the Glebe, for anyone who would like to join!

**The talks we’ll watch:**

1\. Introducing Positron \(Julia Silge\) and Exploratory Data Analysis in Python with Positron \(Isabelle Zimmerman\)

Positron is a next generation data science IDE that is newly available to the community for early beta testing. This new IDE is an extensible tool built to facilitate exploratory data analysis, reproducible authoring, and publishing data artifacts. Positron currently supports these data workflows in either or both Python and/or R, and is designed with a forward-looking architecture that can support other data science languages in the future. In this session, learn from the team building Positron about how and why it is designed the way it is, what will feel familiar or new coming from other IDEs, and whether it might be a good fit for your own work.

2\. Closeread: bringing Scrollytelling to Quarto \- Andrew Bray

Scrollytelling is a style of web design that transitions graphics and text as a user scrolls, allowing stories to progress naturally. Despite its power, scrollytelling typically requires specialist web dev skills beyond the reach of many data scientists. Closeread is a Quarto extension that makes a wide range of scrollytelling techniques available to authors without traditional web dev experience, with support for cross-fading plots, graphics and other chunk output alongside narrative content. You can zoom in on poems, prose and images, as well as highlighting important phrases of text. Finally, Closeread allows authors with experience in Observable JS to write their own animated graphics that update smoothly as scrolling progresses.

3\. GitHub: How To Tell Your Professional Story \- Abigail Haddad

GitHub is more than just a version control tool, it's a way of explaining your professional identity to prospective employers and collaborators – and you can build your profile now, before you're looking for new opportunities. This talk is about how to think of GitHub as an opportunity, not a chore, and how to represent yourself well without making developing your GitHub profile into a part-time job. I'll talk about why GitHub adds value beyond a personal website, what kinds of projects are helpful to share, and some good development practices to get in the habit of, regardless of your project specifics.

venues.name: Ottawa Public Library - (Sunnyside Branch in The Glebe)

title: Version Control and RStudio
description:
**WORKSHOP OVERVIEW**

Sick of having twenty versions of the same file? Can’t remember why you changed that line of code? Was it 6 weeks or 2 years ago? Did I add that bit or was it my colleague? In this introductory workshop, we’ll learn about using version control via Git and GitHub, two powerful tools for keeping track of file changes and facilitating collaborative coding.

We’ll also learn how to integrate version control into our RStudio workflow to keep our R code organised! There will be some basic interactive exercises to practice using Git & GitHub (creating a repository; cloning a repository; collaborating using forks). If you’d like to follow along with the exercises, please make sure to do the below set-up/prep in advance.

**PREPARATION AND RESOURCES**

1. (Install/)Update R and RStudio to the newest versions available to you.
2. If you don’t already have one, create a free GitHub account at https://github.com

* Register with an academic email if you have one (more perks)
* Think carefully about your username: avoid spaces & uncommon characters; a shorter username is better than longer; usually your real name (or related to it) is best

3. Download GitHub Desktop from https://desktop.github.com
4. Install Git

* It is possible/likely you already have it
* There are thorough instructions for explaining how to check and what to do if you don't have it at: https://happygitwithr.com/install-git.html

**ONLINE PROGRAMME**
The event will be held online and will start at 16:00 (GMT).
Please join us via link:
https://teams.microsoft.com/l/meetup-join/19%3ameeting_N2VlOGVhZjktMTMwYi00MDI4LTg1ZTEtZDcwMTNjZmFiM2Ew%40thread.v2/0?context=%7b%22Tid%22%3a%224ffa3bc4-ecfc-48c0-9080-f5e43ff90e5f%22%2c%22Oid%22%3a%2213d75a48-cddd-4e0a-b31a-9aa858d18c90%22%7d

Please take a minute to read our code of conduct - we are committed to creating a safe and harassment-free environment:
https://github.com/rladies/.github/blob/master/CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md
R-Ladies events are always free and open to anyone who wants to participate as long as they follow the code of conduct. We are pro-actively inclusive of queer, trans, and all minority identities, with additional sensitivity to intersectional identities. Our priority is to provide a safe community space for anyone identifying as a minority gender who is interested in and/or working with R.

title: Watch Party Francophone pour la posit::conf(2024)
description:
Nous sommes ravis d’organiser une #Watch #Party virtuelle pour la conférence organisée par Posit qui a eu lieu du 12 au 24 août 2024 à #Seattle 🔗 ​​[https://posit.co/conference/](https://posit.co/conference/).

🎉 Durant cette Watch Party, nous allons regarder les #replays de ces deux présentations sélectionnées de la conférence, le lundi 21 octobre 2024 à partir de 19h00 CEST :

1. **Introducing Positron, a new data science IDE, présenté par Julia Silge, Engineering Manager et Tom Mock, Product Manager, Posit, PBC.**
“Positron is a next generation data science IDE that is newly available to the community for early beta testing. This new IDE is an extensible tool built to facilitate exploratory data analysis, reproducible authoring, and publishing data artifacts. Positron currently supports these data workflows in either or both Python and/or R, and is designed with a forward-looking architecture that can support other data science languages in the future. In this session, learn from the team building Positron about how and why it is designed the way it is, what will feel familiar or new coming from other IDEs, and whether it might be a good fit for your own work.”
2. **Quarto, AI, and the Art of Getting Your Life Back, présenté par Tyler Morgan-Wall, Research Staff Member, Institute for Defense Analyses.**
“Tired of endless server issues and maintenance headaches? Want to reclaim your time for coding, writing, and creating? Join me as I share my journey of switching from the server-based headaches of Wordpress to Quarto, with a little help from AI. In this talk, I’ll describe the simple trick I used to convert an existing Wordpress blog—complete with custom scripts, styles, and beautiful 3D dataviz content—into a slick Quarto site. I'll then demonstrate some lesser-known features of Quarto to automate deploying a website entirely from a Quarto project file. Finally, I’ll show you how I used AI to customize and style my new Quarto site, and provide several useful strategies to employ if you decide to get some help from AI on your own Quarto journey.”

Chaima Boughanmi partagera également son expérience personnelle de la conférence ainsi que ses impressions. Après chaque présentation, une discussion ouverte sera organisée.

​​L'inscription est désormais ouverte via ce lien 🔗 [https://us06web.zoom.us/meeting/register/tZIoduqqrjovHN2Y2jSZoS2uDxLGKMziwWUw](https://us06web.zoom.us/meeting/register/tZIoduqqrjovHN2Y2jSZoS2uDxLGKMziwWUw).

N'hésitez pas à partager ce meetup avec toute personne qui pourrait être intéressée.

Ne manquez pas l'opportunité d'être parmi les premiers à accéder à des replays de très haute qualité.

On vous attend nombreux pour cette nouvelle Watch Party francophone ! ✨

title: Transforma tus datos en historias visuales con Quarto
description:
Debido al **paro universitario** que afectara al personal no dicente de la **UNTREF**, hemos decidido suspender la realización del taller planificado para el próximo lunes. Acompañamos el reclamos en defensa de la universidad pública 💜

**En breve estaremos comunicando la próxima fecha.**

\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-
\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-

**¡Se viene un nuevo Meetup PRESENCIAL!**

En esta oportunidad, **Jesica Formoso** nos introducirá a **[Quarto](https://quarto.org/)**, un sistema de publicación de código abierto diseñado para crear documentos científicos y técnicos de manera dinámica y reproducible.

Aprenderemos cómo Quarto expande las capacidades de R Markdown, permitiéndonos generar informes, presentaciones y sitios web. A lo largo del taller, exploraremos ejemplos prácticos de su uso en informes reproducibles, abordando temas como la integración de código y texto, la personalización de formatos de salida y el uso de parámetros para crear variantes de documentos.

Este taller es **introductorio** está dirigida a personas con un **conocimiento básico de R** y **poca o ninguna experiencia previa con Quarto**.

**Requerimientos:**

* R: 4.4.1
* Quarto: v1.5 (descargar quarto CLI [aquí](https://quarto.org/docs/get-started/))
* RStudio: 2024.04.2+764 (descargar [aquí](https://posit.co/downloads/))
* Instalar el paquete **tidyverse**.

Para poder seguir el taller sin inconvenientes, te pedimos que traigas tu computadora preparada, con R y RStudio actualizados, y el quarto CLI y el paquete tidyverse instalados.

Finalmente, te invitamos a compartir el link al meetup para quien quiera inscribirse.

**¡Te esperamos!**

title: R-Ladies NYC Lightning Talks!
description:
We are excited to host an **online** version of one our most popular events - R-Ladies NYC Lightning Talks.

***\*\*Update\*\****
We've decided to delay event by 2 weeks to encourage more participants to sign up to give a talk. The new date for the lightning talks is **Sunday, October 20th at 4pm EST**. We hope this extended timeline will allow more members to get involved and contribute. Looking forward to seeing everyone there!

Please fill out the very brief form to submit your 5-minute talk idea! [https://forms.gle/3ahdFtx6eLDHvxeE9](https://forms.gle/3ahdFtx6eLDHvxeE9)

***What are lightning talks?***
Lighting talks are five minute talks. You can talk about anything R, Python, Tech, and/or Data Science related. Looking for ideas? How about talking about a cool package you found, how you use R/Python in your company, a favorite learning resource, your journey into Data Science, or a side project you've been working on?

Prior lightning talk topics include: 1-Automating Reports with Gmailr; 2-Using Shiny to Avoid Boring Tasks at Work; 3-How to Do VLOOKUP in R; 4-Balancing Business and Technical Requirements; 5-Pie Charts: Friend or Foe?

Interested in proposing a talk but want to brainstorm ideas, get feedback, or some encouragement? We're here to help! You can attend one of our Prep & Brainstorm Workshops, join #lightningtalks channel in our slack channel, or email [nyc@rladies.org](mailto:nyc@rladies.org). We would be happy to set up a time to talk with you!

Are men welcome to give a lightning talk? Yes, men are welcome. Our mission is to encourage more women and non-binary individuals to get involved in the R community. Please help us by spreading the word about R-Ladies, and by being respectful of the fact that this event is primarily for women/non-binary/female-identifying individuals. We will prioritize women and non-binary individuals for the talks, but encourage all to apply!

We would especially love to hear from people who have not presented at a meetup or conference before. A lightning talk can be a great way to start out speaking, as it's quick, low-prep, and fun!

***Extension Dates:***
\- Proposal due: Friday 10/11 [https://forms.gle/3ahdFtx6eLDHvxeE9](https://forms.gle/3ahdFtx6eLDHvxeE9)
\- Notification of acceptance: Sunday 10/13
\- Speakers to Confirm Participation: Thursday 10/17
\- Submit slides to R\-Ladies: Friday 10/18
**\- Lightning Talk Event: Sunday 10/20**

title: Construindo seu primeiro dashboard interativo com o {Shiny}
description:
Vagas Limitadas!

O Shiny é um framework para criar aplicativos web de maneira fácil usando código R, sem precisar de qualquer conhecimento de HTML, CSS ou JavaScript. Por outro lado, o Shiny possui componentes de interface de usuário que podem ser facilmente personalizadas ou estendidas, e seu servidor usa programação reativa para permitir que você crie qualquer tipo de lógica de back-end que desejar. Atualmente, o Shiny é usado em quase tantos nichos e indústrias quanto o próprio R. Na academia é usado como um meio chamativo para exibir novos resultados, métodos ou modelos estatísticos e em empresas para configurar painéis de métricas em tempo real que incorporam análises avançadas. Este minicurso foi projetado para levá-lo de não saber nada sobre Shiny para ser capaz de construir dashboards, onde você será capaz de expor seus resultados de maneira interativa que ainda são fáceis de manter e de alto desempenho.

Assim, o objetivo do minicurso será oferecer formas de trabalhar com uma base de dados, no que diz respeito a conhecer a natureza do dado e realizar ajustes caso necessário, avaliar presença de dados faltantes, realizar cálculos referentes à medidas descritivas, construir gráficos, tabelas e exportar dados. title: Lunch&Learn: Code like a pro using AI with GitHub Copilot
description:
Ever wished there was a version of ChatGPT to help you write R code right inside your favorite IDE, like RStudio or VS Code? Good news—there is, and it’s called GitHub Copilot!

In this talk, Rebecca will demonstrate Copilot's powerful AI tools, including advanced code completion (it can write entire multi-line functions from a single comment!) and a chatbot that can "see" your whole code file. Copilot can save you time and brain juice, even if you're already an R pro. While GitHub Copilot is proprietary software owned by Microsoft, meaning it comes with a cost and is only fully available in VS Code (which is also owned by Microsoft), a "light" version with more limited functionality is supported in RStudio.

To get started, you’ll need a GitHub account, you should have VS Code and RStudio installed, and have an active GitHub Copilot subscription. During the live demo, Rebecca will also provide resources to help you kick off your Copilot journey.

title: NASA Space Apps Challenge 2024
description:
R-Ladies Community welcomes you to the NASA Space Apps Challenge Switzerland!

We are thrilled to bring this exciting event to Switzerland for the first time, inviting you to join us for a weekend of innovation, collaboration, and creativity. This unique hackathon brings together brilliant minds from diverse backgrounds to tackle real-world problems using NASA's vast collection of open data.

**About NASA Space Apps Challenge**
The NASA Space Apps Challenge is a global hackathon that engages communities around the world in addressing real-world problems, fostering innovation, and promoting the spirit of exploration. Established in 2012, this event has seen tremendous growth, with over 29,000 participants from more than 200 cities in 80+ countries. And now, for the very first time, Switzerland will join this global community of innovators!

**What to Expect**
**Collaborate:** Work with fellow participants from various fields, including science, technology, engineering, design, and entrepreneurship.
**Innovate:** Use NASA's open data to create solutions for real-world challenges related to Earth, space exploration, and beyond.
**Create:** Develop exciting applications, tools, and projects that can make a difference in the world and beyond.

**Who Can Participate?**
Anyone with a passion for solving problems and a desire to make an impact is welcome to join. Whether you're a seasoned coder, a creative designer, a passionate scientist, or a project manager, your skills are valuable, and your contributions can make a difference.

**Why Participate?**
**Global Recognition:** Join a prestigious global community of innovators and showcase your talents on an international stage.
**Networking:** Meet and collaborate with like-minded individuals, industry experts, and potential mentors.
**Prizes**: Stand a chance to win exciting prizes and gain opportunities for further development and exposure of your projects.
**Impact**: Use your skills to create solutions that can help address some of the most pressing challenges faced by humanity.

**How to Join?**

1. Create a profile at https://www.spaceappschallenge.org/
2. Chose location: Switzerland -> **[Zurich](https://www.spaceappschallenge.org/nasa-space-apps-2024/2024-local-events/zurich/)** and press "join"
3. Press "going" at[ local even](https://www.linkedin.com/events/7219723567330934785/comments/)t and share with your friends!

For more information, visit our [website](https://www.spaceappschallenge.org/) and follow us on [Linkedin](https://www.linkedin.com/company/18940634/)
Check out our[ local page](https://techlegion.ch/SpaceApps.html) as well.

Don't forget to chose your location - onsite and virtual :
https://www.spaceappschallenge.org/nasa-space-apps-2024/2024-local-events/zurich/

**Let's Innovate Together!**
We can't wait to see the incredible ideas and projects that will come out of the first-ever NASA Space Apps Challenge in Switzerland. Join us for this groundbreaking event and be part of a global movement to use open data for the betterment of our world and beyond.

For more information, visit our websites: https://www.spaceappschallenge.org/
https://www.linkedin.com/company/space-apps-ch/
https://www.linkedin.com/events/7219723567330934785/comments/

Let's make history together at the NASA Space Apps Challenge Switzerland!

title: AI 工程師— Pre-Trained By TSMC
description:
報名請到：[https://www.accupass.com/event/2408181420559068361750](https://www.accupass.com/event/2408181420559068361750)

**[Agenda]**
19:00 \~ 19:15 開放入場
19:15 \~ 19:25 開場
19:25 \~ 20:25 AI 工程師— pre-trained by TSMC
20:25 \~ 自由交流時間

**[Talk Information]**
「欸 ! 你在台積喔 ? 薪水很多吧 ? 」，「哇 ! 你兒子在台積喔 ? 很好的工作耶 !」，「裡面很常加班吧 ? 但拿到錢時就很值得了吧 ?」這些事一年前身為前公司的員工在買早餐、家族聚會、朋友聚會，甚至是無時無刻的聊天起手式。「你們這個部門在幹嘛阿 ?」 「如何在製造業為部門帶入資料科學的技術 ?」而這些是其他部門的同事對品質管理部門的無限疑惑。對一間世界級的大公司裡神秘的化學分析部門，大家總有無限的聽說與想法，不如，坐下來聽聽這次的分享，由小工程師的角度看大台積電，由製造業擁抱 AI 浪潮。
「蛤 ! 你真的有想清楚了嗎? 」，「你真的知道你要甚麼嗎?」，「你知道你走了會損失多少嗎? 以後就回不來囉 !」這些一年後從台積下山的我，哪怕去樓下的麵攤吃麵都會被阿婆提醒的，碗裡少了一顆滷蛋，我怎麼覺悟的? 我考慮了些什麼? 有後悔嗎? 實不相瞞，我甚至比任何一個人更想知道答案。
這次的分享，我會由小工程師的角度出發，希望讓你知道台積電的好與壞、人知與也許不為人知的台積電。另一方面，也會分享在不違反保密條約的前提下，怎麼使用 attention-based/ pre-train model。最後，經歷一遍離職的評估、掙扎，為此，我甚至走訪心理輔導，只為了找到什麼才是我想要的。相信我，這會很精彩、也很特別，同時很有收穫的一次分享。

**[Speaker Information]**
***邱威翔 Wilson***
生成式 AI 工程師 @ NeuroWatt
從做 AI 晶片的公司到寫 AI 的菜鳥工程師，從半導體晶片特用化學品質管理到人工智慧程式設計，從碩士到就業都在跨領域嘗試的科學家。喜歡運動、喜歡音樂、喜歡高科技、喜歡物理化學，我甚至喜歡經濟與法律，我想我就是對所有新事物充滿想像的那種人，願意在畫布上嘗試大膽的形狀，希望在彩虹上添加第八種顏色。而對於喜歡的事物也非三分鐘熱度，願意犧牲看劇的時間了解一個最新的 AI 模型，選擇詳細研究日本經濟貨幣政策而非社群軟體。夢想是走遍世界的每一個城市，聆聽屬於每個人的故事，並回報自己無限的能量。

**[Host Information]**

* Ben Chen：活動主持人
* Ning Chen：活動規劃
* Kristen Chan: 活動場地規劃

**[活動單位]**

* 主辦單位：[R-Ladies Taipei](https://www.facebook.com/groups/RLadiesTaipei)
* 場地贊助：[言文字](https://www.instagram.com/emoji0701/)

title: Data Wrangling Practice with R
description:
Join us for an engaging session on “***Data Wrangling with R***” hosted by ***R-Ladies Rome***! This interactive workshop is specifically designed to introduce participants to the essential techniques and tools necessary for effective data wrangling using the R programming language.

In today’s data-driven world, the ability to manipulate and prepare data is crucial for analysis and decision-making. This workshop aims to equip attendees with the skills needed to clean, transform, and organize datasets efficiently. Participants will learn how to utilize tidyverse R packages, which are fundamental for performing operations like filtering, sorting, and summarizing data.

Throughout the session, we will provide hands-on exercises to reinforce learning, allowing participants to practice their skills in real-time. Whether you are a beginner looking to gain confidence in data manipulation or an intermediate user aiming to refine your techniques, this workshop will cater to all levels of experience.

By the end of the workshop, participants will have a solid understanding of data wrangling concepts and be able to apply these techniques to their own projects. Additionally, attendees will benefit from networking opportunities with fellow data enthusiasts and experts in the field.

Don’t miss out on this opportunity to enhance your data wrangling skills and become a more proficient user of R. We look forward to seeing you there!

title: satuRday with R-Ladies: R for beginneRs
description:
Join us for an in-person Meetup to learn the basics of managing data with tidyverse and creating basic plots with ggplot2.

Download materials to participate from the [R-Ladies St. Louis GitHub](https://github.com/rladiesstl/meetup-september-2024).

Here is the schedule:\`

9am–10:30am Introduction to data management with tidyverse
10:30–10:45 BREAK
10:45–12:15 More tidyverse and ggplot2 for graphs
12:15–12:45 lunch\* and networking

\* We will provide a light lunch at the end of the workshop and the room will be open to network with each other and to chat with the R-Ladies team about your ideas for future Meetups. Recommend bringing a water bottle or cup to fill if needed. If you have dietary restrictions please bring your own refreshments.

DIRECTIONS:
We will meet in Goldfarb Hall Room 132 on the Washington University campus.
\* Goldfarb Hall is on Forsyth about 1.5 blocks west of Skinker
\* Park in free parking garage by turning from Forsyth onto Wrighton Way about a block west of Skinker and accessing the garage. It is free on the weekends but will make you take a ticket to get in; when you leave you'll need the ticket to get out but there will be no charge.
\* Leave the parking garage on the southwest corner (signs near the stairs say Brown School)
\* The big round glass building in front of you is Hillman Hall, Goldfarb is the brick building right next to it to the west

Actualmente realiza una estancia postdoctoral en el Departamento de Medicina Personalizada de la Icahn School of Medicine at Mount Sinai, en la ciudad de Nueva York. Se encuentra involucrada en el desarrollo del paquete Giotto para la visualización de datos espaciales de single cell en R.**
