title: Grow Your Career In Tech: #IamRemarkable Workshop
description:
Are you battling with imposter syndrome at work? Struggling to find the confidence to speak up and acknowledge your worth? Join the **#IamRemarkable workshop** on Thursday, **September 26th 2024**.

#IAmRemarkable is a global movement founded by Google that empowers everyone to celebrate their achievements in the workplace and beyond.
Over 600k people joined the initiative and 82% of them reported a confidence boost thanks to the insights and the activities shared in the workshop.

In this experience, you will be guided by **trained facilitator Clelia Calabrò**, who is passionate about adaptation to change and transformation.

Find more info on the [#IamRemarkable](https://www.rmrkblty.org/iamremarkable) initiative.

Ready to grow your career in tech with #IamRemarkable? Save your seat now, we canʼt wait to meeting you soon!

This event to open to people of all gender identities.

Céline, Elena, Luisa, Seraphina - and Clelia!

title: Club de lectura Fundamentos de ciencia de datos con R - Sesión 8
description:
¡Nos emociona invitarles a nuestro club de lectura! 📚 Para esta edición, utilizaremos el libro **Fundamentos de Ciencia de Datos con R**, disponible [aquí](https://cdr-book.github.io/index.html).
En la octava sesión del club de lectura, Daniel Bolivar, psicólogo y candidato a magister en ciencia de datos, nos hablará sobre los capítulos 30 y 31 del libro.
Recuerda que al participar en nuestros eventos, aceptas nuestro [código de conducta](https://rladies.org/coc/), el cual nos ayuda a crear un espacio amable y seguro para aprender.
**📅Fecha:** 25 de septiembre de 2024
**⏰Hora:** 18:00 - 20:00 (UTC-5, Colombia y Ecuador Continental)

Te sugerimos tener a la mano tu computadora con R y RStudio instalados. Puedes descargar e instalar el software desde [este enlace](https://cran.rstudio.com/).
El link de meet se encuentra publicado en este evento. No te olvides de agendarlo en tu calendario.
¡Te invitamos a participar! ¡No te pierdas esta gran oportunidad!

title: Creación de paquetes en R
description:
¡Marca en tu agenda la conferencia de septiembre!

En esta ocasión, tendremos la oportunidad de contar con Freddy Hernández Barajas, profesor del Departamento de Estadística de la Universidad Nacional de Colombia. El profesor Freddy es reconocido por sus contribuciones en la creación de paquetes en R para modelamiento estadístico y análisis de datos, además de ser el creador del canal de YouTube "EstadísticaUtil". En su presentación, nos enseñará a crear un paquete en R y ofrecerá recursos valiosos para facilitar esta tarea.

¡No te lo pierdas!

title: posit::conf(2024) watch party!
description:
Join us on Wednesday, September 25th from 5:30-7:00pm for a special posit::conf(2024) \**sneak peek* watch party, where we’ll dive into a few exciting talks that focus on documentation, reproducibility, and collaboration. You can expect some networking and discussion, popcorn, and these great speakers:

1. **Why’d you load that package for? - Luis D. Verde Arregoitia:** (lightning talk)
2. **Context is King - Shannon Pileggi:** (Abstract) The quality of data science insights is predicated on the practitioner’s understanding of the data. Data documentation is the key to unlocking this understanding; with minimal effort, this documentation can be natively embedded in R data frames via variable labels. Variable labels seamlessly provide valuable data context that reduces human error, fosters collaboration, and ultimately elevates the overall data analysis experience. As an avid, daily user of variable labels, I am excited to help you discover new workflows to create and leverage variable labels in R!
3. **“Please Let Me Merge Before I Start Crying”: And Other Things I’ve Said at the Git Terminal - Meghan Harris:** (Abstract) This talk is geared towards those who may feel comfortable working independently with Git but need some confidence when working collaboratively. Just like novice drivers can learn to confidently (and safely!) merge onto (seemingly) intimidating highways, those new to collaborating with Git can also conquer Git merges with some exposure and preparation. This talk will go over: different ways R users can interact with Git, what Git merges and Git merge conflicts are, real-life examples of Git merges, advice on resolving Git merges, and suggestions for cleaner workflows to promote better Git merges

\**While you won’t yet find the public links to this year’s talks, we are still able to stream recordings from our virtual attendee profile!*

We’ll supply some light refreshments, but please feel free to bring something to share. See you there!

Your R-Ladies SB co-organizers,
Sam, Danielle, Camila

title: [ONLINE] Importação de dados - Clube do Livro R for Data Science
description:
• CONTEÚDO
Esse encontro faz parte do clube do livro R for Data Science (2 edição)!

Esse livro foi escrito por Hadley Wickham (criador do ggplot2), Mine Çetinkaya-Rundel, e Garrett Grolemund.Iremos usar a versão que está sendo traduzida para português, disponível gratuitamente e online em https://cienciadedatos.github.io/pt-r4ds/ .Clube do livro organizado por R-Ladies São Paulo, liderado por Beatriz Milz e Luana Antunes.Saiba mais em: https://github.com/R-Ladies-Sao-Paulo/2024-clube-do-livro-r4ds

O evento é aberto para TODAS as pessoas interessadas e será transmitido através do Youtube: [https://www.youtube.com/c/RLadiesS%C3%A3oPaulo/streams](https://www.youtube.com/c/RLadiesS%C3%A3oPaulo/streams)

• LEITURAS DA SEMANA

Importação de dados: [https://cienciadedatos.github.io/pt-r4ds/data-import.html](https://cienciadedatos.github.io/pt-r4ds/data-import.html)

***Por favor leia todas as informações abaixo!***

**• LOCAL DO EVENTO: Canal das R-Ladies São Paulo no Youtube**

**• CÓDIGO DE CONDUTA**

**Disponível aqui: [https://rladies-sp.org/coc.html](https://rladies-sp.org/coc.html)**

**Qualquer pessoa que violar ou houver previamente violado o código de conduta desta comunidade terá sua entrada proibida, será expulsa/o e/ou estará sujeita/o à ação que a pessoa organizadora achar mais apropriada, independente de inscrição no evento, ser membro do MeetUp ou qualquer outra condição.**

• SOBRE AS R-LADIES

R-Ladies é uma organização que promove a diversidade de gênero na comunidade da linguagem R. Nosso principal objetivo é promover a linguagem computacional estatística R compartilhando conhecimento, assim, quem tiver interesse na linguagem será bem-vinda, independente do nível de conhecimento. Nosso principal público-alvo são as pessoas que se identificam com o gênero feminino, portanto, mulheres cis, mulheres trans, bem como pessoas não-binárias e queer.\*\*\*O evento é gratuito, e organizado com muito carinho, ajude a espalhar a palavra, compartilhe com outras ladies :)

title: R-Ladies NYC Lightning Talks (Prep & Brainstorm Workshop) (2 of 2)
description:
We are excited to host an online version of one our most popular events this fall - R-Ladies NYC Lightning Talks! We are holding this workshop to provide guidance and support for interested speakers. If you are interested in proposing a talk but want to brainstorm ideas, get feedback, or some encouragement, join us!

***What are lightning talks?***
Lighting talks are five minute talks. You can talk about anything R, Python, Tech, and/or Data Science related. Looking for ideas? How about talking about a cool package you found, how you use R/Python in your company, a favorite learning resource, your journey into Data Science, or a side project you've been working on?

Prior lightning talk topics include: 1-Automating Reports with Gmailr; 2-Using Shiny to Avoid Boring Tasks at Work; 3-How to Do VLOOKUP in R; 4-Balancing Business and Technical Requirements; 5-Pie Charts: Friend or Foe?

Are men welcome to give a lightning talk? Yes, men are welcome. Our mission is to encourage more women and non-binary individuals to get involved in the R community. Please help us by spreading the word about R-Ladies, and by being respectful of the fact that this event is primarily for women/non-binary/female-identifying individuals. We will prioritize women and non-binary individuals for the talks, but encourage all to apply!

We would especially love to hear from people who have not presented at a meetup or conference before. A lightning talk can be a great way to start out speaking, as it's quick, low-prep, and fun!

***Important Dates***
**\- Prep & Brainstorm Workshops \(optional\): Thursday 9/12 or 9/19**
\- Submissions due: Friday 9/20 [https://forms.gle/3ahdFtx6eLDHvxeE9](https://forms.gle/3ahdFtx6eLDHvxeE9)
\- Notification of acceptance: Wednesday 9/25
\- Speakers to Confirm Participation: Friday 9/27
\- Submit slides to R\-Ladies: Friday 10/04
\- Lightning Talk Event: Sunday 10/06

title: Swashbucklin' Data Science: Navigatin' R fer Talk Like a Pirate Day!
description:
[Workshop GitHub](https://github.com/MLWorkshops/swashbuckling-r)

🏴‍☠️ Ahoy, useRRRRRs! 🏴‍☠️

Join Seattle R-Ladies and Seattle AI Workshop Meetups for a swashbuckling evening of data exploration at our R Pirate Day event. Whether ye be a landlubber new to R or a seasoned data buccaneeR, there’s something for everyone! Set sail with us on September 18 from 5-8pm at the Microsoft Redmond Reactor for an evening filled with deep dives into data science, networkin’, grub, jolly fun and plenty of pirate puns.

**Speakers and Workshops**:

* **Micheleen Harris**: Get Hooked on ggplot: craftin’ stunning graphics on the high seas
* **Kim Dill-McFarland**: Rough tides: How to improve your big data visualizations in R
* **Simran Saxena**: Aye-Eye NLP: Digging deep for emotional treasure

**Event Details:**

* **Location**: Microsoft Redmond Reactor (3709 157th Ave NE, Redmond, WA 98052)
* **Date & Time**: September 18, 2024, 5:00 PM - 8:00 PM ⏰
* **Food**: Pizza, salad, and drinks courtesy of our sponsor, Microsoft Reactor
* **Prerequisites**: Beginner knowledge of R and RStudio including loading and manipulating data frames, base plots, and saving outputs. Completely new to R? Check the resources below.

**Workshop Resources:**

* Check out our GitHub organization for workshop materials:[ MLWorkshops GitHub](https://github.com/MLWorkshops)
* Set up your RStudio server with this Docker setup:[ ](https://github.com/dataqueso/docker-rstudio-server-pop-genetics)[coming soon]
* Prepare in advance with Introduction to R from Kim:[ Workshop Resources](https://bigslu.github.io/workshops/)

Don’t miss this opportunity to enhance your R skills and network with fellow data enthusiasts. Pirate attire is optional but highly encouraged! Arrr you ready?

title: Primer encuentro de R-ladies Tepic
description:
Nos encontramos muy entusiastas en anunciar el primer evento PRESENCIAL de R-ladies Tepic. En esta ocasión, tendremos un taller básico de R y RStudio. Hablaremos también sobre ¿Qué es R-ladies? y el objetivo de la creación de R-ladies Tepic, un nuevo capítulo.
Buscamos iniciar la integración de una comunidad interesada en aprender sobre R y todas las posibilidades que tiene este lenguaje de programación para incrementar nuestras habilidades en la toma de decisiones mediante el análisis y visualización de datos.

title: Intro to Datviz
description:
WHAT TO EXPECT
This workshop will provide a practical introductory overview (with hands-on practice) with making data visualizations in R using ggplot2 ([https://ggplot2.org/](https://ggplot2.org/)).
Material: [github.com/rladies-aurora/202409-dataviz_intro](https://github.com/rladies-aurora/202409-dataviz_intro)

WHO is this for?

* If you are new to R & are short-budgeted on time!
* If you'd like to start working with data in R w/o learning all the programming details first!!
* If you are getting started with base R and want to learn more!
* If you're interested in learning DataViz basics w/ R.
* If any of this is true, this workshop might be for you!

TOPICS

* why everyone talks about data visualization!
* how to pick the right visualization for your data
* overview of visualization packages: ggplot2, ggvis, leaflet, plotly
* why use ggplot2 in R for visualization
* the basics of 'aesthetics'
* the basics of 'geoms'
* other ways to customize the layout of your visualization

AGENDA
5:00–5:10 Meet & Greet \| 🍕
5:10–5:15 Future Meetup planning + Set-up
5:15–6:15 Workshop: Intro to Data Visualization w/ R
6:20–6:30 Q&A

SET-UP

* To benefit most from this workshop, please bring your laptop with pre-installed RStudio (https://www.rstudio.com/products/rstudio/) and R.
* Please ping us on Discord (https://discord.gg/hyZKcbGSWw) or email us (eastlansing@rladies.org) with questions, if any.

💰SPONSOR

* JRaviLab \(PI: Janani Ravi\) \| [https://jravilab.github.io](https://jravilab.github.io)

title: R-Ladies NYC Lightning Talks (Prep & Brainstorm Workshop) (1 of 2)
description:
We are excited to host an online version of one our most popular events this fall - R-Ladies NYC Lightning Talks! We are holding this workshop to provide guidance and support for interested speakers. If you are interested in proposing a talk but want to brainstorm ideas, get feedback, or some encouragement, join us!

***What are lightning talks?***
Lighting talks are five minute talks. You can talk about anything R, Python, Tech, and/or Data Science related. Looking for ideas? How about talking about a cool package you found, how you use R/Python in your company, a favorite learning resource, your journey into Data Science, or a side project you've been working on?

Prior lightning talk topics include: 1-Automating Reports with Gmailr; 2-Using Shiny to Avoid Boring Tasks at Work; 3-How to Do VLOOKUP in R; 4-Balancing Business and Technical Requirements; 5-Pie Charts: Friend or Foe?

Are men welcome to give a lightning talk? Yes, men are welcome. Our mission is to encourage more women and non-binary individuals to get involved in the R community. Please help us by spreading the word about R-Ladies, and by being respectful of the fact that this event is primarily for women/non-binary/female-identifying individuals. We will prioritize women and non-binary individuals for the talks, but encourage all to apply!

We would especially love to hear from people who have not presented at a meetup or conference before. A lightning talk can be a great way to start out speaking, as it's quick, low-prep, and fun!

***Important Dates***
**\- Prep & Brainstorm Workshops \(optional\): Thursday 9/12 or 9/19**
\- Submissions due: Friday 9/20 [https://forms.gle/3ahdFtx6eLDHvxeE9](https://forms.gle/3ahdFtx6eLDHvxeE9)
\- Notification of acceptance: Wednesday 9/25
\- Speakers to Confirm Participation: Friday 9/27
\- Submit slides to R\-Ladies: Friday 10/04
\- Lightning Talk Event: Sunday 10/06

title: What is “production” anyway? MLOps for the curious
description:
Join RLadies Sydney online for a Special Event with Julia Silge!
Please register on the Rladies Sydney Meetup page to attend the event online: https://meetu.ps/e/NjB8n/yxNGr/i

RLadies Sydney are thrilled to welcome Julia Silge, a data scientist and engineering manager at Posit PBC (formerly RStudio), where she works on open source modeling and MLOps tools.
She is a tool builder, an author, an international keynote speaker, and a real-world practitioner focusing on data analysis and machine learning. Julia loves text analysis, making beautiful charts, and communicating about technical topics with diverse audiences. You can find her online at her blog and YouTube.

Topic:

While data scientists are often taught about training a machine learning model, building an MLOps strategy to deploy and maintain that model can be daunting. In this talk, learn about MLOps concepts like model deployment and model drift, the different ways the word “performance” is used with models, what you can monitor about a model, how feedback loops impact models, and how you can set yourself up for success with model maintenance. This talk will help practitioners who are already deploying models, but this is also useful knowledge for data science practitioners earlier in their MLOps journey; decisions made along the way can make the difference between resilient models that are easier to maintain and disappointing or misleading models.

title: R-Ladies Singapore: Getting to know you ☺️
description:
Hi! Welcome to R-Ladies Singapore (RLSG). ☺️
RLSG is a new addition to the R-Ladies community ([http://rladies.org](http://rladies.org/)) aiming to promote R's usage and exchange knowledge in R, especially for women and gender minorities. That said, **everyone is welcome to join our Meetups**, just that **priority will be given to women & gender minorities**.

Anyone interested in R regardless of proficiency may come join us.
So, please let us know what you hope to see in this local chapter so we may best tailor them to your interests and availabilities by filling up this survey ([https://forms.gle/bcWUpdc17mu2Gqhh8](https://forms.gle/bcWUpdc17mu2Gqhh8)) by 30 August.

Our first event will likely be in **Q4 2024**. We will keep you updated.

For any questions, please email: singapore@rladies.org

title: La gramática de ggplot
description:
**Descripción del evento:**
En nuestro Meetup online, aprenderás a utilizar la librería `ggplot2`, una herramienta poderosa para la visualización de datos en R. Con `ggplot2`, podrás generar una amplia variedad de gráficos con un estilo coherente, incluyendo gráficos de dispersión, burbuja, línea, series de tiempo, mapas de calor, treemap, gráficos de barras, redes, y más. En este taller nos enfocaremos principalmente en la grámatica de gráficos que inspira el paquete, la cual propone que cualquier gráfico puede ser creado a partir de 3 componentes: *Set de datos*, *Sistema de coordenadas* y *Geometrías.*

**Plataforma:** El taller se realizará online. Te enviaremos el enlace de acceso por correo electrónico antes del evento.

Para el taller, es importante que tengas tu computador listo y que hayas descargado previamente R Project desde este link ->: [https://dirichlet.mat.puc.cl/](https://dirichlet.mat.puc.cl/) y la versión gratuita de R Studio desde este link ->: [https://www.rstudio.com/products/rstudio/download/](https://www.rstudio.com/products/rstudio/download/)
Si es la primera vez que asistes a uno de nuestros eventos, recuerda revisar nuestro código de conducta. Es requisito para asistir estar de acuerdo con él: [https://github.com/rladies/starter-kit/wiki/Code-of-Conduct#spanish](https://github.com/rladies/starter-kit/wiki/Code-of-Conduct#spanish)
ggplot es parte del universo de paquetes de Tidyverse, por lo que recomendamos revisar el material de nuestro taller pasado en el siguiente enlace: https://github.com/Rladies-Concepcion/Taller-Tidyverse-2024

¡No dudes en consultarnos cualquier duda, te esperamos!

title: Meta-análises para diferenças de médias e proporções usando R.
description:
**Estamos de volta!** **=D**
Vem participar desse encontro para reunirmos novamente a comunidade nessa cidade coza max querida.

**Sobre a oficina**
A meta-análise é uma técnica estatística poderosa que combina resultados de vários estudos em um único resultado, permitindo conclusões mais precisas e confiáveis. Na oficina, você aprenderá como realizar uma meta-análise programando em R. Venha descobrir como integrar dados de diferentes estudos e gerar insights significativos!

**Programação do evento**
18:30 Apresentação do R\-Ladies Floripa \| Tamires Martins
19:00 Teórica: Introdução às meta\-análises: o que são\, onde são usadas\, tipos de meta\-análises\, pacotes utilizados \| Juliana Bolzan
19:30 Prática: Uso dos pacotes 'metafor' e 'meta' na condução de meta\-análises com dados clínicos e não clínicos \| Juliana Bolzan
20:30-21:00 Momento para explorar seus próprios dados e fazer networking.

**Local do evento:** Híbrido
\- Presencial: Laboratório de informática do CCS\-UFSC
\- Online: via Meet

**O que precisa para participar do evento?**
O evento é totalmente gratuito e teremos computadores disponíveis para quem for participar presencialmente. Mas, se você for participar online, é necessário ter acesso a um notebook e uma conta google. Antes do evento, crie uma conta na [posit.cloud](https://posit.cloud/) (é rapidinho), pois é por lá que vamos programar em R. [Link](https://posit.cloud/content/8573312) para o projeto na posit.cloud.

**Código de conduta**
Para participar do evento, você deve concordar com o[ código de conduta da comunidade](https://github.com/rladies/.github/blob/master/CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md#portuguese).

**Sobre R-Ladies**
O R-Ladies é uma organização mundial, com a missão de promover a diversidade de gênero na comunidade R e no mercado da tecnologia como um todo. O foco principal do R-Ladies é nos incentivar a alcançarmos nosso potencial, construindo uma rede global colaborativa de líderes, mentoras, aprendizes e desenvolvedoras para facilitar o progresso individual e coletivo de mulheres (e pessoas de outros gêneros minoritários) em todo o mundo!

**Gostaria de ministrar uma oficina/palestra?**
Preencha [esse formulário](https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSdVXRNZGm4x7f71cPueBQrCDy8mwmtpoiDsgJvOVjgjiqh7pg/viewform).
Você também pode ajudar divulgando o evento e convidando mais pessoas a se juntarem à nós.

Siga nossas redes para ficar por dentro de todos os eventos:
Instagram: @rladies.floripa
Twitter: @rladiesfloripa
Site do Meetup: https://www.meetup.com/pt-BR/rladies-florianopolis/

E-mail: florianopolis@rladies.org

title: 《報導者》的AI初體驗：我們如何建置立委發言監測儀表板？
description:
請至以下連結報名：[https://www.accupass.com/go/RLadies_Reporter](https://www.accupass.com/go/RLadies_Reporter)

**[Agenda]**
19:00 \~ 19:15 開放入場
19:15 \~ 19:25 開場
19:25 \~ 20:25 《報導者》的AI初體驗：我們如何建置立委發言監測儀表板？
20:25 \~ 自由交流時間

**[Talk Information]**
從今年年初的選舉開始，我們一直想從紛亂又龐雜的文字堆裡提煉出對選民、讀者有用的資訊，於是展開一系列我學習文字探勘和AI運作邏輯的旅程。從傳統的斷詞分析、LDA主題模型到串接生成式AI的API、RAG，我會分享我們如何洞察組織、讀者的需求，積極向外尋求資源與專家協助，最後完成《報導者》第一個AI產品的思考過程。
無論你有沒有數據處理、文字探勘或AI應用經驗，都可以聽懂。

**[Speaker Information]**

***簡毅慧***
報導者數據記者，誤闖資料科學和機器學習叢林的新聞系文組女，雖說是左手寫Python、右手寫報導，但ChatGPT問世後幾乎要變成左撇子，相信所有學到的技術，能帶大家看到更多角度的社會議題。作品獲得2024年Sigma Awards國際數據新聞獎肯定。

**[Host Information]**

* Ben Chen：活動主持人
* Ning Chen：活動規劃
* Kristen Chan: 活動場地規劃

**[活動單位]**
主辦單位：

* R-Ladies Taipei

1\. 言文字的規定：低消一杯飲品，沒有服務費，自助取餐，自助回收

title: posit::conf(2024) Community Mixer
description:
[posit::conf(2024)](https://posit.co/conference/) is an open-source data science conference happening in Seattle on August 12 - 14! From Posit, formerly RStudio, we have talk tracks, community events, updates on product enhancements, all-day workshops, and keynotes from your favorite data scientists.

Stick around after the final keynote of posit::conf(2024) for a chance to connect with community members from R-Ladies and PyLadies. This event is open to the public, attendees of posit::conf, and community members who are not registered for posit::conf may attend.

title: K-Means en R
description:
**DETALLES**
En este meetup, exploraremos las bases teóricas y prácticas del algoritmo K-means, una herramienta esencial para la agrupación de datos masivos. Los participantes aprenderán a implementar este algoritmo en R, interpretar los resultados y aplicar técnicas para optimizar el proceso de clustering.

**AGENDA**
-Introducción
-Algoritmo K-Means
-K-Means
-Variantes de K-Means
-K-Means dinámico
-Ejercicios con benchmark
-Comentarios finales

**FECHA**
Jueves 8 de agosto de 2024, 5 p.m. (CDMX)

**DESCARGA LOS MATERIALES EN:**
https://github.com/RLadiesCuerna/meetup_agosto_2024

**REQUERIMIENTOS DE SOFTWARE**
\- R y RStudio
\- Librerias de R animation y ggplot2

Puedes instalarlas con las siguientes instrucciones:
install.packages("ggplot2")
install.packages("animation")

**CÓDIGO DE CONDUCTA**
Recuerda leer y atender nuestro código de conducta disponible en https://rladies.org/code-of-conduct/ o https://rladiesmx.netlify.app/codigo_de_conducta/

title: Shiny 101
description:
Join us for a special event hosted by RLadies, featuring a talk by **Margaret Colville**, one of our dedicated members!

Have you heard about the Shiny package for building websites in R and wondered how to use it? In this talk Margaret will cover the basics of how it works and experiment with a simple example or two.

Whether you’re looking to create interactive dashboards or full-fledged web applications, this session will provide you with the foundational knowledge to get started.

*No prior experience with Shiny is required, but a basic understanding of R will be helpful.*

Join us and take advantage of Margaret’s commitment to empowering the community with her time and expertise!

***R-Ladies Sydney organisers***
***Thank you Nous Group for supporting the RLadies!***
*Find us on Twitter @rladiessydney and on [LinkedIn](https://www.linkedin.com/groups/14405806/) for more information on news, presentations and code!*
\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*
***Photos, Films and all other media/recordings***\*: Photographs and/or video/other media will be taken at events held by this community. By taking part in an R-Ladies Sydney event you grant the community organisers full rights to use the images resulting from the photography/video filming/media, and any reproductions or adaptations of the images for publicity, fund-raising or other purposes to help achieve the community’s aims. This might include (but is not limited to), the right to use them in their printed and online publicity, social media, press releases and funding applications. If you do not wish to be recorded in these media please inform a community organiser.\*
***Code of Conduct***\*: R-Ladies Sydney is dedicated to providing a welcoming and harassment-free event experience for everyone, regardless of gender, gender identity and expression, age, sexual orientation, disability, physical appearance, body size, race, ethnicity, or religion (or lack thereof). We do not tolerate harassment of event participants in any form, for any event venue, including talks, workshops, parties, Twitter and other online media.\*
***Community Policies***\*: Please note, cis men are welcome to join our meetups when they come as a guest of a woman participant or they bring a woman with them. We highly encourage speakers and teachers at R-Ladies Sydney events to be of minority genders.\*

title: Networking and 5 à 7
description:
Join R-Ladies Ottawa for an in-person networking event at Beyond the Pale! This is a great opportunity to meet other women and gender minorities from the Ottawa area who are interested in open-source while enjoying some delicious food and/or drinks!

**Please also note that the mission of R-Ladies is to increase gender diversity in the R community.** Our events are intended to provide a safe space **for women and gender minorities.**

title: Cómo preparar tu propuesta de presentación para LatinR 2024
description:
¡Hola!

¡Ya está abierto el envío de trabajos para [LatinR 2024](https://latinr.org/)! Este año la conferencia latinoamericana sobre R se llevará a cabo en línea, entre el 18 y el 22 de noviembre. Esta modalidad la hace una excelente oportunidad para compartir lo que has estado haciendo con R con el resto de la comunidad, sin tener que moverte de tu casa.
Pero ¿cómo preparar una propuesta de presentación?
¿Qué debiese incluir el abstract?
¿La idea que tengo es suficiente como para proponer algo?
¿Qué pasa si recién estoy empezando a usar R?
¿Es lo mismo escribir un abstract para una conferencia de R que para una conferencia académica de mi disciplina?
¿Qué tengo que tener en cuenta si quiero presentar sobre un paquete o una Shiny app que desarrollé?
Para responder estas y otras preguntas nos reuniremos el próximo jueves 1 de agosto. Durante la sesión, compartiremos recomendaciones para diseñar una propuesta y revisaremos algunos ejemplos de años anteriores para que te hagas una idea de la variedad de temas que es posible presentar.

**¿Y si todavía no tengo una idea concreta para una presentación?**
No importa. Durante la sesión hablaremos también sobre cómo generar ideas. Si usas R, seguro que tienes algo para compartir con la comunidad.

**Preparación**
Si aún no tienes una cuenta en OpenReview (la plataforma que usa LatinR para envíos) te sugerimos hacerte una cuenta ahora, incluso si todavía no tienes claro si vas a enviar una propuesta. OpenReview a veces se demora hasta dos semana en aprobar las cuentas nuevas que se crean con correos personales, así que es mejor no dejar este paso para última hora.

**Acceso**
Media hora antes del inicio publicaremos la contraseña de acceso a Zoom como comentario acá en Meetup. El enlace te aparecerá a la derecha, debajo de la fecha del evento, cuando te registres.

**Código de conducta**
Queremos que este sea un espacio de aprendizaje amable y seguro. Al inscribirte en este evento estás aceptando nuestro código de conducta: https://guide.rladies.org/about/coc/

¡Te esperamos!

R-Ladies

title: [Rescheduled] - Debugging in R
description:
\

So you have just started your R journey. The examples are well defined and everything is sunshine and rainbows. 🌈 what the workshops and tutorials didn’t prepare you for is the real world error messages and how to overcome them. There just wasn’t enough time! This workshop is intended to help you decide these messages and strategies on how to tackle your code problems with a minimal reproducible example.

Please submit your own error messages and code ahead of time using the form here: https://forms.gle/tAzRJ5QmUfSHyUBU7. This will help us tailor the workshop to your needs!

This workshop is intended for anyone who has some prior coding experience in R or other languages.

See you there!

Yuka, Jasmine and Cici

**IMPORTANT**\*\*

1. Have your computer and a steady internet connection ready
2. If this is your first R-Ladies event, please take a moment to review our R-Ladies Global code of conduct: https://github.com/rladies/.github/blob/master/CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md
3. I don't identify as one of the "ladies", but can I attend? Of course! Everyone is welcome to join us at our events and to be a part of our mission to empower gender minorities and underrepresented people in data science with the skills and knowledge to code in one of the top programming languages in data science, which is R.

To be a part of our community, we ask participants to be actively respectful, encouraging, and supportive of each other.

**Note to access the zoom you will need to enter with a password we will email out the password in an email before the event**

title: Club de lectura Fundamentos de ciencia de datos con R - Sesión 5
description:
¡Nos emociona invitarles a nuestro club de lectura! 📚 Para esta edición, utilizaremos el libro **Fundamentos de Ciencia de Datos con R**, disponible [aquí](https://cdr-book.github.io/index.html).
En la quinta sesión del club de lectura, Viviana Flórez y José Cuenca, científicos de datos, nos hablará sobre los capítulos 6 y7 del libro. Recuerda que al participar en nuestros eventos, aceptas nuestro código de conducta, el cual nos ayuda a crear un espacio amable y seguro para aprender.
**📅Fecha:** 31 de julio de 2024
**⏰Hora:** 18:00 - 20:00 (UTC-5, Colombia y Ecuador Continental)

Te sugerimos tener a la mano tu computadora con R y RStudio instalados. Puedes descargar e instalar el software desde [este enlace](https://cran.rstudio.com/).

El link de zoom se encuentra publicado en este evento. No te olvides de agendarlo en tu calendario.
¡Te invitamos a participar! ¡No te pierdas esta gran oportunidad!
**R-Ladies Barranquilla** 💜👩‍💻

title: Choosing the right chart for your data
description:
Join us for an engaging meetup where we'll dive into the art and science of data visualization with our topic: "Choosing the Right Chart for Your Data." Whether you're a seasoned data analyst, a beginner just getting started, or a professional from any field working with data, this event will equip you with the knowledge to effectively communicate your data insights.
**What to Expect:**

* **Introduction to Data Visualization Principles:** Understand the key principles of effective data visualization and why choosing the right chart is crucial.
* **Chart Types and Their Uses:** This section provides a comprehensive overview of various chart types, including bar charts, line charts, pie charts, scatter plots, histograms, and more. Learn about their strengths, weaknesses, and best scenarios for their use.
* **Practical Examples:** Dive into real-world examples and case studies to see how different charts can transform raw data into compelling stories.
* **Interactive Q&A Session:** Have data visualization expert, Yan answer your questions and share your experiences with chart selection.

**Who Should Attend:**

* Data analysts and scientists looking to enhance their visualization skills.
* Business professionals who need to present data insights clearly and effectively.
* Students and educators in data-related fields.
* Anyone interested in improving their ability to communicate data.

title: R-Ladies NYC Social: A night at Talea Brewery!
description:
Join R-Ladies NYC for a summer social at Talea Brewery!

For the first hour, we will take data from Open Brewery DB, free and open source brewery data, and demonstrate how to make graphs and visuals of the data using R! New to R and/or have never used R before? No worries! **You do not need any prior experience with R to follow along** \- just bring your laptop and positive attitude\! After the demonstration\, we will have the rest of the time to network\, socialize\, and share some of our graphics\. Food and drinks \(and free swag\) are on us\! The only thing you need to bring to this event is your laptop\.

This event will be held at Talea Brewery in Williamsburg:
87 RICHARDSON STREET
BROOKLYN, NY 11211

Please RSVP here on Meetup so we have an idea for attendance. Make sure to bring your laptop to this event!

To learn more about R-Laides NYC: [https://www.rladiesnyc.org/](https://www.rladiesnyc.org/). R-Ladies is dedicated to providing a harassment-free experience for everyone. We do not tolerate harassment of participants in any form.

Are men welcome to attend R-Ladies events? Yes, men are welcome. Our mission is to encourage more women and non-binary individuals to get involved in the R community. Please help us by spreading the word about R-Ladies, and by being respectful of the fact that this event is primarily for women/non-binary/female-identifying individuals.

title: Building Reproducible Pipelines with R, Docker and Nix
description:
🕵️ Discover essential tools and best practices for creating reproducible workflows. 🧞‍♂️🔥

In this talk, **[Dr. Bruno Rodrigues](https://www.linkedin.com/in/brodriguesco/)** will present some ideas and tools needed to build **reproducible pipelines with R, Docker an Nix**. Bruno will discuss best coding practices (moving away from "script-based" workflows), Docker to containerize analyses and Nix, a powerful package manager that can be used within, or outside of Docker.

After the talk, participants will have enough knowledge to start using these tools for their analyses! ⛳️

⏰ Don’t miss out on this insightful session empower your with practical knowledge! 🤩

description:
**報名請至**：[https://www.accupass.com/go/rladiestaipei_mvp](https://www.accupass.com/go/rladiestaipei_mvp)
線上會議連結：[https://reurl.cc/gDma3b](https://reurl.cc/gDma3b)
**[Agenda]**
19:00 \~ 19:30 開放入場
19:30 \~ 19:40 開場
19:40 \~ 20:40 主議程：

* 向量資料庫與 Qdrant 的介紹 (Ko Ko)
* 以 Memory Kernel 實作地端版 RAG (Ian Chen)
* 解放大型語言模型 - Google Gemini 安全設定實驗 (Ryan Chung)

20:40 \~ 自由交流時間

**[Talk Information]**

***向量資料庫與 Qdrant 的介紹 （Ko Ko）***
簡介：向量資料庫可以說已經成為當代 AI 應用的基礎設施，本次會分享向量資料庫的基礎知識，並且使用目前最火紅的向量資料庫之一：Qdrant，來做範例。並且在最後教大家如何使用 LLMs 結合向量資料庫來做應用。

***以 Memory Kernel 實作地端版 RAG （Ian Chen）***
簡介：從AI API到AI框架的應用，講述一個從天上到地下的RAG實作經驗

***解放大型語言模型 - Google Gemini 安全設定實驗 （Ryan Chung）***
簡介：大部分的大型語言模型，都有著立意良善的設計、不可談論色情、暴力、仇恨、歧視等敏感議題。然而在Google Gemini中，使用者可以選擇完全開放，不阻擋。真的可以跟大型語言模型百無禁忌地聊天嗎？讓我們一起來做一些實驗。

**[Speaker Information]**
Ko Ko、Ryan Chung、Ian Chen均是Microsoft AI MVP，從2023年合著《駕馭 ChatGPT 4: 探索 Azure OpenAI 與 Cognitive Service for Language 開發實踐 (使用. NET 與 Node. Js)》，再到2024最新合著《極速 ChatGPT 開發者兵器指南：跨界整合 Prompt Flow、LangChain 與 Semantic Kernel 框架》。

**[Host Information]**

* Ben Chen：活動主持人、活動場地規劃
* Kristen Chan：活動主持人
* Ning Chen：活動規劃

**[活動單位]**

* 主辦單位：R-Ladies Taipei

title: Quarto Dashboards
description:
Join us for an insightful session on “Quarto Dashboards” led by expert Isabella Velasquez!

In this workshop, you’ll dive into the world of Quarto, exploring how to create dynamic and interactive dashboards for data visualization. Whether you’re a beginner or seasoned developer, this session promises to enhance your skills and empower you with practical knowledge.

Don’t miss out on this opportunity to learn from the best in the field and take your data presentations to the next level. See you there!

title: Debugging in R
description:
So you have just started your R journey. The examples are well defined and everything is sunshine and rainbows. 🌈 what the workshops and tutorials didn’t prepare you for is the real world error messages and how to overcome them. There just wasn’t enough time! This workshop is intended to help you decide these messages and strategies on how to tackle your code problems with a minimal reproducible example.

Please submit your own error messages and code ahead of time using the form here: https://forms.gle/tAzRJ5QmUfSHyUBU7. This will help us tailor the workshop to your needs!

This workshop is intended for anyone who has some prior coding experience in R or other languages.

See you there!

Yuka, Jasmine and Cici

**IMPORTANT**\*\*

1. Have your computer and a steady internet connection ready
2. If this is your first R-Ladies event, please take a moment to review our R-Ladies Global code of conduct: https://github.com/rladies/.github/blob/master/CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md
3. I don't identify as one of the "ladies", but can I attend? Of course! Everyone is welcome to join us at our events and to be a part of our mission to empower gender minorities and underrepresented people in data science with the skills and knowledge to code in one of the top programming languages in data science, which is R.

To be a part of our community, we ask participants to be actively respectful, encouraging, and supportive of each other.

title: Intro R and RStudio Workshop (hybrid)
description:
Join the Seattle R-Ladies in this 1.5-hour workshop where we introduce R and RStudio at the beginner level. This workshop is open to R-Ladies members representing minority genders including but not limited to cis women, trans women, trans men, non-binary, genderqueer, and agender individuals. You do not have to be in the Seattle area to attend virtually.

Content

In this workshop, we cover:

R and RStudio including projects, scripts, and packages
The help function
Reading in data as a data frame and RData
Data types

Pre-requisites

You do not need any prior experience in R or coding!

Format

This is a hybrid workshop. In person attendees, please find information on the event location at UW South Lake Union on this page. Virtual attendees will receive a Zoom link the day of the workshop.

Materials

https://bigslu.github.io/workshops/introR.workshop/introR.html

title: Developing Software Together Using GIt and RStudio
description:
Never save report_fertig_fertig_letzte-version.Rmd file again. In this tutorial you will learn how to use Git to track your work and collaborate with others and your future self.

Materials: https://paocorrales.github.io/git-developing-software-together/
Preparation: https://paocorrales.github.io/git-developing-software-together/preparation.html

About the Speaker:
Elio Campitelli has a Ph.D. from Universidad de Buenos Aires in atmospheric sciences, where they studied the large-scale circulation of the Southern Hemisphere and now studies tropical influences on Antarctic sea ice at Monash University. They also taught Introduction to Programming, and Visualization of Information at Universidad Nacional Guillermo Brown and is a The Carpentries certified instructor. They are an active member of the R community, and maintains several open-source R packages (e.g., ggnewscale; metR).

title: Understanding Mixed-Effects Models through Simulation
description:
**WORKSHOP OVERVIEW:**
In this talk, we will delve into the increasingly popular mixed-effects models, frequently employed in experimental fields such as psycholinguistics where data are nested within participants and items. These models can often appear opaque, making it challenging to grasp what specific parameters signify and how they are reflected in the data.
Through the power of simulation, we aim to demystify these models by constructing and manipulating their parameters from scratch. This approach not only aids in a deeper comprehension of the models but also serves as an invaluable tool for conducting power analysis and refining experimental predictions.
Key areas we will explore include:

* Simulating data and see whether the model estimates mirror our expectations.
* Understanding random effects and how to simulate them.
* Exploring how simulation techniques can be used for power analysis.

This session is designed to be useful for a wide audience but of course familiarity with the lme4 package and R will help. If you would like to familiarize yourself with data simulation, you can have a look at my blog posts about this topic ([https://julianquandt.com/categories/power-analysis/](https://julianquandt.com/categories/power-analysis/)).

**ONLINE PROGRAMME**
The workshop will be held online, starting on 12th July at 12:00 pm (UK Summer time). Please join us via this Zoom link:
[https://bham-ac-uk.zoom.us/j/84906376457?pwd=alZGSFY4SmJzVjRHb2FJMm9zS0NuQT09](https://bham-ac-uk.zoom.us/j/84906376457?pwd=alZGSFY4SmJzVjRHb2FJMm9zS0NuQT09)
Meeting ID: 849 0637 6457
Passcode: 163231

**SPEAKER:**
Julian Quandt, currently a postdoctoral researcher at WU Vienna University of Economics and Business, completed his PhD studies in Psychology at Radboud University, Nijmegen, focusing on metacognition in value-based decision making. During his doctoral studies, faced with the absence of a standard tool for power analysis for Mixed-Effects Models, he used data simulation instead. Through this process, he discovered the power of data simulation as a tool for learning and enhancing his own understanding of statistical models. Julian authored four blog posts on data simulation, covering both simple and complex statistical models. His intention was to share his intuition that simulation can serve as a valuable tool for comprehending these models, in addition to enabling us to conduct power analysis for (nearly) any statistical model.

**CODE OF CONDUCT**
Please take a minute to read our code of conduct - we are committed to creating a safe and harassment-free environment:
https://github.com/rladies/.github/blob/master/CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md
R-Ladies events are always free and open to anyone who wants to participate as long as they follow the code of conduct. We are pro-actively inclusive of queer, trans, and all minority identities, with additional sensitivity to intersectional identities. Our priority is to provide a safe community space for anyone identifying as a minority gender who is interested in and/or working with R.

title: Bringing R to Serverless Cloud Computing
description: Over the past several years, two technologies have undergirded many of the most innovative research projects and tools developed by the Urban Institute: the R programming language and Amazon Web Services (AWS) Lambda. Despite their respective prevalence, R and Lambda have largely existed in isolation because Lambda does not natively support R. The presentation will describe efforts to address this barrier. The framework for running R code using AWS Lambda and examples of projects will be discussed.

title: Spatial Workflows in R
description:
Join us on **Thursday, July 11 from 5:30-7pm PDT** to learn the ins and outs of spatial workflows!

Dealing with spatial data through coding can be challenging, but is a useful skill across many different fields of expertise. In this session, we will cover the basics of working with spatial data in R, giving you the tools you need to handle spatial data confidently and accurately. We will demonstrate many parts of the spatial data workflow, from data import and manipulation, to basic and advanced data visualization using a suite of different R packages. Finally, we will take it a step further and give an example of how we can use spatial data for decision-making by providing a basic workflow for spatial prioritization in a conservation context.

This event will be **virtual**! A zoom link will be provided directly to folks who RSVP.

If you'd like to code along during the workshop, there are several options for accessing the workshop material:

1. If you're a git and GitHub user, you can [fork and clone the GitHub repository](https://github.com/SnBuenafe/RLadies_SpatPrio_Workshop) ***(recommended)***. For guidelines on how to do so, you can find some instructions [here](https://github.com/samanthacsik/cute-cats-dogs/wiki/Forking-&-Cloning-a-GitHub-respository) \- noting that you will be using Tin and Sandra's repository\, instead of the repository in the example\!
2. If you're not a GitHub user, you can [download the necessary materials via Google Drive](https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1tUuCMVxkKt79k-frYXyA_GVjU6SEf9NO?usp=sharing).

There are several R packages that will be installed and loaded as we work through the workshop material. Some may take some time to install, so if you'd like to install packages ahead of time, you can follow the code in load_packages.R.

title: R-Ladies Monthly Happy Hour
description:
Join R-Ladies Seattle for our monthly happy hour! Come and network with local R-Ladies to make new connections and discuss all things R. This is a casual event so you can stop by any time. You'll find us by our logo (and probably Kim's rainbow hair).

Chips and salsa provided!

Non-alcoholic and alcoholic beverages available. Attendees are responsible for their own tab.

title: R-Ladies Meetup Dinner
description:
In the beginning of July this year, the largest user conference for the statistical software environment R – useR! 2024 (https://events.linuxfoundation.org/user/) – will take place. For the first time in almost 20 years, this conference will be held again in Austria, specifically in Salzburg. As part of the social program for R-Ladies, we would like to host a welcoming event. Join us during a nice informal dinner in a traditional Salburger location. Engage in informal conversation with R-Ladies from all over the world and get to know the speakers of the useR conference.

Cost: Entrance and participation are FREE. Participation is NOT limited to conference attendees! Food and drinks are to be paid separately (registered participants will be informed about the options).

Registration is required.

R-Ladies is an organisation that promotes gender diversity in the R community worldwide. We are pro-actively inclusive of queer, trans, and all minority identities, with additional sensitivity to intersectional identities.

Code of Conduct
R-Ladies is dedicated to providing a harassment-free experience for everyone. We do not tolerate harassment of participants in any form.
This code of conduct applies to all R-Ladies spaces, including meetups, Twitter, Slack, mailing lists, both online and offline. Anyone who violates this code of conduct may be sanctioned or expelled from these spaces at the discretion of the Global Leadership Team.
Participants are responsible for knowing and abiding by these rules.
Harassment includes:

* Offensive comments related to gender, gender identity and expression, sexual orientation, disability, mental illness, neuro(a)typicality, physical appearance, body size, age, race, or religion.
* Unwelcome comments regarding a person’s lifestyle choices and practices, including those related to food, health, parenting, drugs, and employment.
* Deliberate misgendering or use of ‘dead’ or rejected names.
* Gratuitous or off-topic sexual images or behaviour in spaces where they’re not appropriate.
* Physical contact and simulated physical contact (eg, textual descriptions like “*hug*” or “*backrub*”) without consent or after a request to stop.
* Threats of violence.
* Incitement of violence towards any individual, including encouraging a person to commit suicide or to engage in self-harm.
* Deliberate intimidation.
* Stalking or following.
* Harassing photography or recording, including logging online activity for harassment purposes.
* Sustained disruption of discussion.
* Unwelcome sexual attention.
* Pattern of inappropriate social contact, such as requesting/assuming inappropriate levels of intimacy with others.
* Continued one-on-one communication after requests to cease.
* Deliberate “outing” of any aspect of a person’s identity without their consent except as necessary to protect vulnerable people from intentional abuse.
* Publication of non-harassing private communication.

R-Ladies prioritizes marginalized people’s safety over privileged people’s comfort. The Global Leadership Team will not act on complaints regarding:

* ‘Reverse’ -isms, including ‘reverse racism,’ ‘reverse sexism,’ and ‘cisphobia’.
* Reasonable communication of boundaries, such as “leave me alone,” “go away,” or “I’m not discussing this with you.”
* Communicating in a ’tone’ you don’t find congenial.
* Criticizing racist, sexist, cissexist, or otherwise oppressive behavior or assumptions.

title: Women in Technology: Web Development Workshop | Building a Website in R
description:
Join **R-Ladies Rome & R-Ladies NYC** for a **hands-on workshop** focused on Web Development specifically tailored for women in technology.

The event will provide a platform for professional networking, skill-building, and empowerment for women interested in expanding their knowledge in Web Technology and Open Source tools. Through practical sessions and discussions, attendees will have the opportunity to **explore the essentials of Quarto**, from setting up your development environment to customizing your site's design and functionality.

By the end of the tutorial, you'll have a **fully functional, personalized website** where to showcase your projects and achievements.

Don't miss this chance to enhance your skills and be part of a supportive community of **women in technology**!

\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-Hands\-on Session Setup\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-

* Please have the latest version of RStudio installed or sign up for Posit Cloud ([https://posit.cloud/plans](https://posit.cloud/plans)). If you are using another tool, please ensure Quarto CLI is installed on your computer ([https://quarto.org/docs/get-started/](https://quarto.org/docs/get-started/)).
* Throughout the tutorial, we will be referencing the Quarto guide for RStudio ([https://quarto.org/docs/get-started/hello/rstudio.html](https://quarto.org/docs/get-started/hello/rstudio.html)) as well as W3Schools CSS tutorials ([https://www.w3schools.com/css/default.asp](https://www.w3schools.com/css/default.asp)).
* The tutorial will also go over how to use Git and GitHub for version control, but this is optional.
* You can set up version control with GitHub at a later point. That said, if you wish to follow along with the tutorial, we suggest you to create a GitHub account and connect it to RStudio ([https://happygitwithr.com/rstudio-git-github](https://happygitwithr.com/rstudio-git-github)) before the meetup.
* Lastly, we will be using Quarto Pub ([https://quartopub.com/](https://quartopub.com/)) to publish the website. Again, if you wish to follow along during the tutorial, please create a an account ahead of time. The Quarto website also has information about publishing with other platforms ([https://quarto.org/docs/publishing/](https://quarto.org/docs/publishing/)).
\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-

title: Building a Website in R
description:
Join us for an interactive hands-on tutorial on web development with R, where you'll learn to build your own website/portfolio using Quarto.

This event is designed for both beginners and experienced R users who want to expand their skills into web development. We'll cover the essentials of Quarto, from setting up your development environment to customizing your site's design and functionality. By the end of the tutorial, you'll have a fully functional, personalized website where to showcase your projects and achievements.

Dive into the world of web development with R and Quarto, and transform your data and analysis into a stunning online presence!

title: R pour le Data Journalisme : De la Donnée à l'Information Quotidienne
description:
R-Ladies Paris vous donne rendez-vous le mercredi 26 juin à partir de 19H en direct sur Zoom. Notre invité Victor Alexandre, data journaliste chez le Parisien, partagera son expérience sur l’utilisation de R pour analyser, visualiser et raconter des histoires à partir des données au quotidien.

Découvrez comment exploiter la puissance de R pour transformer des données brutes en informations percutantes, essentielles pour le journalisme moderne.

Veuillez vous inscrire à notre événement à travers ce lien 🔗 https://us06web.zoom.us/meeting/register/tZUocOiqrz0rGdEZnCwsEXTyAYt85nIdEVxH

Vous trouverez plus de détails sur notre intervenant ci-dessous : https://www.linkedin.com/in/victoralexandre/

Biographie : Victor Alexandre a rejoint l'équipe data-innovation du Parisien-Aujourd'hui en France en 2015, en tant que data journaliste. Après un master 2 en journalisme à l’Institut d’Etudes Politiques de Lille, il s’est formé aux statistiques en sciences sociales à l’Ecole Normale Supérieure / Ecole des Hautes Etudes en Sciences Sociales / ENSAE. Au quotidien, il propose ou accompagne des sujets en s'appuyant sur des calculs. Il utilise R au quotidien depuis 10 ans.

Nous vous attendons nombreux ! 💫

title: [ONLINE] #tidytuesday Work Group (on a Wednesday)
description:
Join fellow R-Ladies in exploring a #tidytuesday data set! For those craving human connection and some practice flexing your data science skills, this is the event for you! We will hold our event via Zoom, where you can meet other R-ladies, improve your R skills, and build your data science portfolio. This event is for people at all levels of R (beginner through advanced).

At this event, we'll revisit one of the Tidy Tuesday datasets from October 2023 on Taylor Swift album and song lyrics via the taylor R package (https://taylor.wjakethompson.com/). You can read more about it here: https://github.com/rfordatascience/tidytuesday/blob/master/data/2023/2023-10-17/readme.md and see some previous analysis of the data here: https://rpubs.com/salvatoremancino/1065547.

Agenda (all times in ET):
6:30 - 6:45 pm Meet & Greet
6:45 - 7:45 pm #tidytuesday
7:45 - 8:00 pm Discussion

What is #tidytuesday?
#TidyTuesday is a weekly social data project created by the R4DS online learning community that is focused on using tidyverse packages. A new data set and accompanying article featuring an interesting plot is released on Github every Monday. The goal is to generate a copy of the original plot or create your own original approach!

For more information on #tidytuesday, check out [this blog post](https://bit.ly/2HqEsdt) and [the main repository page](https://github.com/rfordatascience/tidytuesday).

Who is this for?
New and experienced R users alike! Let's learn together! All are welcome, including R-Ladies RTP members and R-Ladies from around the world.

What should I bring?
You'll need a computer with videoconferencing capabilities and R. Serve yourself a beverage and a snack to fuel your evening!

Where will it take place?
**Please join us online via Zoom.** A link to join will be provided when you RSVP.

All events are intended for women as leaders, mentors, members, and attendees. We are emphatically queer and trans friendly. Men are welcomed to attend *as guests* and must be accompanied by the R-Lady who invited them. This group was created so those who identify as women have a comfortable place to learn. If you would like to attend and feel excluded, please introduce yourself to an organizer.

title: Democratizing Methods for Causal Inference
description:
Join R-Ladies NYC for a talk by Jennifer Hill.

The past few decades have seen an explosion in the development of freely available software to implement statistical methods and algorithms to help us explore and analyze data. However researchers tend to assume that once they have made their software available through CRAN or on GitHub that their job is done. Typically, very little attention is paid to ensuring that the software is easy to use or unlikely to be used incorrectly. Even less attention is paid to helping researchers new to a method to understand the underlying assumption or how to appropriate interpret the output.

In this talk, I will describe a new software tool for causal inference that works to scaffold the user experience to maximize the probability that researchers can use the tool appropriately and understand the foundational ideas. Moreover, I'll describe a randomized experiment we performed to understand whether this tool actually accomplishes these goals relative to traditional software.

[RSVP HERE](https://forms.gle/Au7xXdmu4ePscT7c8) \| The passcode to login to the event will be emailed to registrants on the morning of June 25\.

**R-Ladies is dedicated to providing a harassment-free experience for everyone. We do not tolerate harassment of participants in any form. Please take a moment to review the R-Ladies Global code of conduct (particularly if this is your first time attending): [https://rladies.org/code-of-conduct](https://rladies.org/code-of-conduct).**

**For questions regarding accessibility, please email nyc@[rladies.org](http://rladies.org/).**

title: Taller Forecasting con R
description:
Este evento se realiza en colaboración de R-Ladies Milagro y R-Ladies Guayaquil.

Para el taller de Forecasting con R, solo necesitas traer entusiasmo por aprender. Dentro de las instalaciones se te facilitará el equipo necesario para que puedas seguir el taller junto a nuestra increíble ponente. Recuerda que es imprescindible realizar la inscripción para garantizar una logística adecuada.

Nuestros instructora experimentados los guiará a través de ejercicios interactivos para ayudarlo a comprender conceptos importantes relacionados con el forecasting.

No pierda esta oportunidad de establecer contactos con otros entusiastas de la tecnología, profundizar en el mundo del forecastig y obtener información valiosa sobre el análisis de datos. ¡Adopte el mundo de la tecnología y regístrese ahora para disfrutar de una experiencia de aprendizaje enriquecedora!

title: 用 Cypress 讓資料跑起來
description:
報名請至：[https://www.accupass.com/go/cypress](https://www.accupass.com/go/cypress)
**[Agenda]**
19:00 \~ 19:30 開放入場
19:30 \~ 19:40 開場
19:40 \~ 20:40 主議程：用Cypress 讓資料跑起來～遊戲公司測試超輕鬆～
20:40 \~ 自由交流時間

**[Talk Information]**
在當前 AI 技術快速發展的背景下，各種生成式產品與應用層出不窮，對於開發者來說，加速了產品的迭代和資料更新速度。遊戲公司在這種快速變遷中，如何確保產品的穩定性和高品質，成為了重要課題。
在本次講座中，我將介紹如何使用 Cypress 這一強大的自動化測試工具，來幫助遊戲公司進行測試和資料收集。我們將探討如何通過自動化測試，提升開發效率，確保產品的穩定性，同時發現潛在的漏洞。我作為公司的內部黑客，不斷挑戰系統的極限，找出各種潛在的問題，確保我們的遊戲產品在任何情況下都能穩定運行。
我們將深入了解：

* Cypress 如何進行自動化測試
* 使用 Cypress 進行資料收集的方法
* 實際應用案例，展示 Cypress 在遊戲公司中的強大效能
* 通過自動化測試，提升產品質量，發現並修補漏洞

讓我們一起探索如何運用 Cypress，為遊戲公司帶來高效、可靠的測試解決方案，確保每一款遊戲產品的成功。

**[Speaker Information]**
***Eason Tseng***

* ONElab Software Engineer
* 精誠資訊 軟體工程師
* 中華網龍 遊戲工程師
* 軒昂有限公司 前端工程師
* 瑞嘉軟體科技 測試工程師
* Udemy Egret 遊戲引擎講師

**[Host Information]**

* Ben Chen：活動主持人、活動場地規劃
* Kristen Chan：活動主持人
* Ning Chen：活動規劃

**[活動單位]**

* 主辦單位：R-Ladies Taipei
* 協辦單位：ONElab

**線上會議連結：[https://reurl.cc/gDma3b](https://reurl.cc/gDma3b)**

description:

Nosso principal objetivo é promover a linguagem computacional estatística R compartilhando conhecimento, assim, quem tiver interesse na linguagem será bem-vinda, independente do nível de conhecimento. Nosso principal público-alvo são as pessoas que se identificam com o gênero feminino, portanto, mulheres cis, mulheres trans, bem como pessoas não-binárias e queer.***O evento é gratuito, e organizado com muito carinho, ajude a espalhar a palavra, compartilhe com outras ladies :)

title: R-Ladies Social after Cascadia R Conference
description:
Join us for a social after Cascadia R Conference! This event is open to all Cascadia R Conf attendees as well as R-Ladies and useR! members even if you did not attend the conf.

Cascadia R Conference is an R conference serving the Pacific Northwest (OR/WA/BC). This event is a place to come together around R and get together with like-minded people across the region. Interested? [Learn more on their website and register today](https://cascadiarconf.com/)!

The conference concludes at 6 pm on June 22. Afterwards, we invite R-Ladies and other attendees to join us at a social gathering. Come even if you didn't attend the conference. It's a great time to meet and chat with other R enthusiasts from the area!

We will share the location closer to the event. Please note, each member will be responsible for covering their own food expenses. We appreciate your understanding and hope to see you there.

title: Reproducible pipelines book club: Q&A with the author Bruno Rodrigues
description:
We are happy to announce an extra special meeting in our book club where we will host Bruno Rodrigues, the author of [“Building Reproducible Analytical Pipelines with R”](https://raps-with-r.dev/).

During this meeting, Bruno will present an additional reproducibility tip, not included in the book. Afterward, we will hold a Q&A session where everyone is invited to ask a question.

What topic or burning question do you have for the author? It's a great opportunity, so feel free to ask! We will send a link to a shared document where you can write your questions, concerns, or compliments.

Thanks to all for joining us on this joint book club venture with R-Ladies Cologne and R-Ladies Bergen. And a special thanks to those of you who volunteered to present the chapters. We really enjoyed our sessions together and wish everyone much reproducible progress to come.

description:

description:

title: Data Science Salon NYC: JUNE 18TH FINANCE & TECHNOLOGY
description:
R-Ladies NYC are excited to be community partners with Data Science Salon NYC to promote their upcoming one-day event. **Please note: this event is not free. You must purchase tickets at [www.datascience.salon/newyork/](https://www.datascience.salon/newyork/)**

[Data Science Salon NYC on June 18th, 2024](https://www.datascience.salon/newyork) is an annual one-day 150 person conference focused on AI and machine learning applications in finance & technology. The intimate event takes place at [S&P Global Ratings in Manhattan, New York, NY](https://www.datascience.salon/newyork/#venue) and brings local industry leaders and specialists face-to-face to educate each other on innovative solutions in artificial intelligence, machine learning, predictive analytics and acceptance around best practices.

Featuring speakers from **S&P Global, JP Morgan Chase, CIti, Capital One, Nubank, Federal Reserve Bank of New York, Finder, T. Rowe Price and many more!**

You will see a mix of use-cases, technical talks and panel conversations, and you will walk away with actionable insights from those working on the frontlines of machine learning in finance & technology. All sessions are recorded and are available on-demand for two weeks.

The fifth annual Data Science Salon NYC gathers the most diverse community in the data science field, bringing together executives, senior data science practitioners, data science managers, analysts, and engineering professionals. The format includes an optional expo and lots of time for networking in a casual environment with entertainment.

**Topics Include:**

* Enterprise AI Use Cases in the Finance Sector
* AI Governance
* Natural Language Processing & Deep Learning
* Scalability and LLMOps
* Data Science Teams: Managing, Building, Collaboration
* LLMs in Production
* Generative AI in the Enterprise
* Multilingual LLMs
* Open Source Tools into your workflows
* Designing ML pipelines efficiently
* and much more!

**Why attend the DSS NYC experience?**

* Meet and connect with the most diverse senior technical community in the space
* Improve your skills in 20+ engaging sessions, including use-cases, technical deep dives, and panel conversations
* Learn from practitioners, technical experts and executives what AI and machine learning mean to finance & technology
* Ask expert speakers questions in live Q&A sessions
* Access all sessions on-demand until two weeks after the event

**Grab your spot** at [www.datascience.salon/newyork/](https://www.datascience.salon/newyork/)
DSS NYC also offers discounted rates for groups, nonprofits and more.

title: Intro to R and R Studio
description:
**Hello R Ladies of Honolulu!**

**We are excited to announce our second event, which will occur on June 13th, 2024 at the Entrepreneur's Sandbox and at Honolulu Beerworks. Dr. Lansing Perng, a recent PhD recipient from University of Hawai’i Manoa, will be test-running an intro to R course she has been working on for a college-level course.**

**Don't forget to bring your laptops, this course will include follow-along coding.**

\*\*Any and all levels are encouraged to attend - this is a great opportunity for R beginners to learn the basics, and a chance for seasoned R practitioners to reflect on teaching methods that have been most effective for them over the years. If there are others who are working on R-related curriculum, we would love to hear about it and work that into the general discussion as well! Make sure to bring your laptops! \*\*

**After, we’ll walk over to Honolulu Beer Co for drinks and some post-work socializing.**

**Please RSVP by June 12th, and please let us know if you have any questions!**

**When: 6:30 pm - 8:00 pm**
**Where: Entrepreneur’s Sandbox & Honolulu Beerworks**

**All the best,**
**Jayslen, Maddie, Juliette and Emily**

title: R-Ladies Monthly Happy Hour
description:
Join R-Ladies Seattle for our monthly happy hour! Come and network with local R-Ladies to make new connections and discuss all things R. This is a casual event so you can stop by any time. You'll find us by our logo (and probably Kim's rainbow hair).

Chips and salsa provided!

Non-alcoholic and alcoholic beverages available. Attendees are responsible for their own tab.

title: R-Ladies Coffee and Code Hangout
description:
Come join R-Ladies NY for another evening of coding and coffee. This will be an informal, hands-on working session where we'll work together on our own projects or tutorials.

Come in curious about a subject or looking to make progress on a project you're working on!

BYOLaptop :) All experience levels welcome!

Some possible things we'll be working on:
\- Posit Table Contest \(the deadline is June 15th\!\)
\- TidyTuesday
\- web scrapping with rvest
\- making your website/portfolio with R
\- package development
\- Anything and everything\! Bring a project or idea you want to work on\!

Find us at the tables in the cafe!

description:

¡Les esperamos el lunes 10 de junio a las 18.30 hs!

title: Introducción al Tidyverse
description:
El próximo sábado 8 de junio volveremos a reunirnos para aprender sobre el Tidyverse, el universo de paquetes diseñados para hacer ciencia de datos con R. Inscribete para recibir el link de la reunión!

REQUERIMIENTOS
Para la sesión solo necesitas tener instalado el Tidyverse: > install.packages("tidyverse")

CÓDIGO DE CONDUCTA
Queremos que este sea un espacio de aprendizaje amable y seguro. Al inscribirte en este evento estás aceptando nuestro código de conducta: https://guide.rladies.org/about/coc/#spanish ¡Te esperamos!

title: Personal R Administration
description:
Does the release of a new R version fill you with dread?
Are there passwords in your R code?
Do you look at the output of a failed package installation and think to yourself, “WTF?!”
If you said yes to any of those question, then you need Personal R Administration. You'll come away with tips, tricks, tweaks, and some hacks for building data science dev environments that you won't be afraid to come back to in a year.

title: [ONLINE] Personal R Administration Workshop with Shannon Pileggi & David Aja
description:
Come join your fellow R users from R-Ladies RTP and R-Ladies Gaborone in a joint virtual meet up.

Does the release of a new R version fill you with dread? Are there passwords in your R code? Do you look at the output of a failed package installation and think to yourself, “WTF?!” If you said yes to any of those question, then you need Personal R Administration. You'll come away with tips, tricks, tweaks, and some hacks for building data science R development environments that you won't be afraid to come back to in a year.

This is a half-day long hands-on event based on the "What They Forgot To Teach You About R" workshop that Shannon and David co-teach every year at posit::conf. For more on that workshop, visit: https://rstats.wtf/.

**Who is this for?**
New and experienced R users alike! Let's learn together! All are welcome, including R-Ladies RTP members and R-Ladies from around the world. This meetup is organized in collaboration with R-Ladies Gaborone.

**What should I bring?**
You'll need a computer with videoconferencing capabilities, R, and RStudio. If you have difficulties installing R and/or RStudio, please reach out to the organize at least 24 hours before the event.

**Where will it take place?**
Please join us online via Zoom. A link to join will be provided when you RSVP.

All events are intended for minoritized genders as leaders, mentors, members, and attendees. We are emphatically queer and trans friendly. Men are welcomed to attend as guests and must be accompanied by the member who invited them. This group was created so that women, non-binary, gender non-conforming, and minoritized folks have a comfortable place to learn. If you would like to attend and feel excluded, please introduce yourself to an organizer.

title: Club de lectura Fundamentos de ciencia de datos con R.
description:
¡Nos emociona invitarles a nuestro club de lectura! 📚 Para esta edición, utilizaremos el libro **Fundamentos de Ciencia de Datos con R**, disponible [aquí](https://cdr-book.github.io/index.html).
En la primera sesión del club de lectura, Isabel Vásquez y Viviana Florez, integrantes del capítulo de R-Ladies Barranquilla, nos hablarán sobre los capítulos 1, 2 y 3 del libro. Recuerda que al participar en nuestros eventos, aceptas nuestro código de conducta, el cual nos ayuda a crear un espacio amable y seguro para aprender.
**📅Fecha:** 6 de junio de 2024
**⏰Hora:** 18:00 - 20:00 (UTC-5, Colombia y Ecuador Continental)

Te sugerimos tener a la mano tu computadora con R y RStudio instalados. Puedes descargar e instalar el software desde [este enlace](https://cran.rstudio.com/).

Compartiremos el enlace de Zoom unas horas antes de la sesión.
¡Te invitamos a participar! ¡No te pierdas esta gran oportunidad!
**R-Ladies Barranquilla** 💜👩‍💻

title: Launch of BaR Chat Networking Series
description:

title: RYouWithMe? MarkyMark!
description:
Come join us for a **hands-on session on the basics of R and Rstudio**!

This month we finish with the *"MarkyMark”*" modules of ***RYouWithMe,*** a series of online learning resources for using R - designed by us, for us.

The series is oriented towards R beginners and aims to provide a foundation for future RLadies Sydney activities.

You can start going through the Modules on your own and join us for questions, help and troubleshooting during the meetup.

Find it here: [https://rladiessydney.org/courses/ryouwithme/](https://rladiessydney.org/courses/ryouwithme/)

**How to get involved!**

As an R-Lady learner based in Sydney:

1. Join the R-Ladies Sydney Slack workspace ***[here](https://join.slack.com/t/rladies-sydney/shared_invite/zt-2czd4i8jq-qKuml1FeCHaJPuaYkRDXHQ)***;
2. Introduce yourself in the *#ryouwithme_intros_2024 channel*;
3. Keep an eye out in the *#ryouwithme_general_2024* channel for announcements;
4. Share your successes, ask questions, help out your peers in the *#ryouwithme_help_2024*.
5.

Bring your laptop!

title: HOW TO: present results
description:
Na tokratni delavnici bomo raziskovali, kako smiselno in učinkovito predstaviti podatke z uporabo paketa ggplot2 v R. Vabljeni, da prinesete tudi svoje rezultate, za katere niste prepričani, kako jih uspešno vizualizirati.
Poleg tega bomo spoznali paket SRAdb, ki omogoča neposreden prenos podatkov iz podatkovne baze SRA v okolje R.

**Delavnica bo potekala izključno v živo!**
\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-
In this workshop, we will explore how to meaningfully and effectively present data using the ggplot2 package in R. We encourage you to bring your own results, especially if you're unsure how to visualize them successfully.
Additionally, we will introduce the SRAdv package, which allows direct retrieval of data from the SRA database into the R environment.

**The workshop will take place exclusively in person!**

title: Creative coding: make generative art with R
description:
Location: Room 37 Level B of Building 7, University of Canberra.

R is a programming language used widely for data analysis, but in this workshop, we will explore ways to make art! Building on the popular ggplot2 package, Dr. Emi Tanaka (ANU) will demonstrate how to draw Truchet tiles and other generative art where time permits. All codes demonstrated will be shared with the participants. Come join the workshop to meet fellow Rtists, potentially learn more about the ggplot2 system and showcase your inner creativity!
Please note that the registrants are expected to be proficient in R and some knowledge of tidyverse or ggplot2 as we do not have time to cover the basics in this workshop. Participants are expected also to bring their own laptops with R installed.

title: XI Meet-up R-ladies Niterói
description:
Este encontro está inserido na agenda do VIII Seminário Internacional de Estatística com R (www.ser.uff.br), no qual o grupo R-ladies Niterói participa com a oferta de um minicurso sobre programação com R. Será ministrado pela profa. Jéssica do departamento de Estatística da UFF. É uma atividade voltada para pessoas com pouca experiência na linguagem R. O minicurso será online com número limitado de vagas.
Minicurso: Uma introdução à programação no R.
Resumo: Neste minicurso, o R será apresentado como uma linguagem de programação. A ideia é entender os comandos, as sintaxes e os tipos de objetos dentro do R a partir de exemplos e atividades práticas.

title: Hackaton 2024: R-Ladies Morelia y R-Ladies Querétaro
description:
## Detalles

**Día 1**
**Tema 1 : Funciones y programación defensiva**
Instructora: Driselda Sanchez Aguirre

* [Funciones](https://swcarpentry.github.io/r-novice-gapminder-es/10-functions.html)

title: Taller de familiarización con R/RStudio
description:
¡Hola!

## Detalles

Este taller está dirigido a personas que han oído sobre R y que aún no se han adentrado de forma autónoma a explorarlo. Durante el taller nos familiarizaremos con esta herramienta para que puedas iniciar tu camino en el trabajo con este lenguaje de programación.
Asumiremos cierta familiaridad con otros software de análisis estadístico, aunque este no será en lo absoluto un taller orientado a la ejecución de análisis, sino de demostración de las posibilidades y forma de trabajo del programa.

**Preparación**
No es necesario que traigas nada previamente instalado. Durante la sesión mostraremos cómo preparar tu computadora para utilizar R y RStudio.

**Acceso**
Media hora antes del inicio publicaremos la contraseña de acceso a Zoom como comentario acá en Meetup.

**Código de conducta**
Queremos que este sea un espacio de aprendizaje amable y seguro. Al inscribirte en este evento estás aceptando nuestro código de conducta: https://guide.rladies.org/about/coc/

¡Te esperamos!

R-Ladies

title: 2024 Du Bois Challenge using R
description: The 2024 10 week Du Bois Visualisation challenge ended in April with participants contributing their recreations of W.E.B. Du Bois's 1900 Paris Exposition charts. The challenge's goal is to recreate Du Bois's charts using modern tools like Tableau, PowerBi and D3 by Observable. This talk will look at how Simisani Ndaba used R to recreate this year's challenge's charts.

description:
• CONTEÚDO
Esse é o quarto encontro do clube do livro **R for Data Science** (2 edição)!
Esse livro foi escrito por Hadley Wickham (criador do ggplot2), Mine Çetinkaya-Rundel, e Garrett Grolemund.
Iremos usar a versão que está sendo traduzida para português, disponível **gratuitamente** e **online** em [https://cienciadedatos.github.io/pt-r4ds/](https://cienciadedatos.github.io/pt-r4ds/) .
Clube do livro organizado por R-Ladies São Paulo, liderado por Beatriz Milz e Luana Antunes.
Saiba mais em: [https://github.com/R-Ladies-Sao-Paulo/2024-clube-do-livro-r4ds](https://github.com/R-Ladies-Sao-Paulo/2024-clube-do-livro-r4ds)

O evento é aberto para TODAS as pessoas interessadas e será transmitido através do Youtube: [https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=RSQMMG2pPWU](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=RSQMMG2pPWU)

Canal das R-Ladies São Paulo no Youtube
