Disponível aqui: [https://github.com/rladies/.github/blob/master/CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md#Portuguese](https://github.com/rladies/.github/blob/master/CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md#Portuguese)
Qualquer pessoa que violar ou houver previamente violado o código de conduta desta comunidade terá sua entrada proibida, será expulsa/o e/ou estará sujeita/o à ação que a pessoa organizadora achar mais apropriada, independente de inscrição no evento, ser membro do MeetUp ou qualquer outra condição.
\*\*\*
• SOBRE AS R-LADIES
R-Ladies é uma organização que promove a diversidade de gênero na comunidade da linguagem R. Nosso principal objetivo é promover a linguagem computacional estatística R compartilhando conhecimento, assim, quem tiver interesse na linguagem será bem-vinda, independente do nível de conhecimento. Nosso principal público-alvo são as pessoas que se identificam com o gênero feminino, portanto, mulheres cis, mulheres trans, bem como pessoas não-binárias e queer.
\*\*\*
O evento é gratuito, e organizado com muito carinho, ajude a espalhar a palavra, compartilhe com outras ladies :)

title: How groups manage conflict: Sequence Analysis using TraMineR
description:
We are happy to welcome **Ayşegül Engin** to present how she is using R and in particular the TraMineR package in her research on model-driven decision support. She will present ***How groups manage conflict when using model-driven decision support: Sequence Analysis using TraMineR***, shared work with L. Alberto Franco and Etiënne A.J.A. Rouwette.

Following the presentation we are happy to invite you to a small **buffet** and some **drinks**, sponsored by the University of Vienna.

**Abstract**:
*Within and across organisations, groups are formed to tackle situations that require a decision or recommendation to be made, whether the situation is simple or complex, routine or one-off, short or long-term. However, inconsistencies between group members’ definitions of the situation often gives rise to conflict. Within the management and system sciences, various model-driven support approaches have been developed to assist groups in revealing disagreements and managing conflict. In this study, we investigate whether the preference for deliberate and deep information processing, also known as epistemic motivation, affects an important feature of model-driven group decision support. It serves as an aid to surface and manage cognitive conflict within the group. We report on an experimental study that adopts a process-oriented methodology to examine conflict management behaviors and trajectories of groups with different levels of epistemic motivation. Using TraMineR: Sequence Analysis Toolkit, we show how conflict management trajectories are influenced by epistemic motivation of groups. The results indicate that three dimensions of epistemic motivation; discomfort with ambiguity, decisiveness, preference for order and structure are significant co-variates of variance in groups’ conflict management trajectories.*

title: Taller de R-Ladies: csv,conf pre-conference workshop
description:

title: R 與 GPT 的魔法旅程：從入坑開始與 LLM 接軌
description:
**[報名連結]**
請移駕至此報名：[https://www.accupass.com/go/rladiestaipei-GPT](https://www.accupass.com/go/rladiestaipei-GPT)

**R-Ladies Taipei x Generative AI Week 衛星活動**
Generative AI Week 即將到來，R-Ladies Taipei 也參與了今年的盛事，將此次活動成為 Generative AI Week 的一環，一起共襄盛舉，為 AI 的浪潮盡一點綿薄之力！Generative AI 年會相關活動請參考這個連結：[https://2024.gaiconf.com/zh](https://2024.gaiconf.com/zh)。

**[Agenda]**
19:00 \~ 19:15 開放入場
19:15 \~ 19:25 開場
19:25 \~ 19:55 我從零開始學 R 的歷程與如何推別人入坑
19:55 \~ 20:25 當 R 遇見 GPT：資料分析師的新魔法
20:25 \~ 自由交流時間

**[Talk Information]**
**我從零開始學 R 的歷程與如何推別人入坑**
**講者：*李又如***
2018 年開始做資料新聞之後，發現需要新技能才能存活，但從零開始無論是看書還是線上課程都只感受到「麻瓜」的挫折感，最後是從需求出發、像是越級打怪的學習方式，才慢慢學起來。
但發現這種方式意外地適合新手。我將從自己的學習和教學經驗（新的同事、資料新聞的學生），分享麻瓜的困境（我們可能連變數是什麼都找不出來）跟可能的學習方法。
去年開始的 chatGPT 風潮也馬上成為我寫 code 的小幫手，本來以為也會是新手的好助教，結果發現不是人人都能很順暢地與 chatGPT 互動。我將分享失敗經驗跟比較好的互動方式。

**當 R 遇見 GPT：資料分析師的新魔法**
**講者：*Kristen Chan***
在資料的世界裡，R 語言一直是資料分析師手中的魔法杖。如今，隨著生成式預訓練變換器（GPT）技術的興起，一個新的法術即將被解鎖。本次演講將帶領大家探索如何將這兩大強力工具結合，從基本的 R 語言應用拓展至利用 GPT 開闢新的可能性。
我們將從 R 語言的基礎出發，快速過渡到如何利用它與 GPT 相結合，不僅增強數據分析的深度，也拓寬了應用的範疇。無論是進行自然語言處理、自動化內容創建，還是構建更智能的預測模型，R 語言和 GPT 的結合將為數據分析帶來前所未有的靈活性和力量。
精簡而充滿啟發性的本次演講，不僅適合 R 語言愛好者，也歡迎所有對於最新 AI 技術感興趣的朋友。

**[Speaker Information]**
**李又如**
曾擔任週刊記者，沒有特定新聞路線的雜食系，2017 年進到一個工程師超過半數的團隊以後開始慢慢長理科腦。喜歡資料新聞不設限的特性，覺得每天都在學習新事物。作品曾入圍國內外各大新聞獎，曾獲亞洲卓越新聞獎（SOPA Awards）、全球華文永續報導獎。

**Kristen Chan**
台灣微軟 Microsoft Technical Trainer / R-Ladies Taipei 現任負責人
喜歡學任何新鮮有趣的東西，期望能貢獻自己的能力運用資料解決問題。

**[Host Information]**
Ben Chen：活動主持人
Ning Chen：活動規劃
Summit Suen: 活動場地規劃

**[活動單位]**
主辦單位：

* R-Ladies Taipei

場地贊助：

* [言文字](https://www.instagram.com/emoji0701/)

**[注意事項]**
1\. 言文字的規定：低消一杯飲品，沒有服務費，自助取餐，自助回收

* 店家資訊：https://www.instagram.com/emoji0701/

* 發言的機會請優先留給女生
* 座位請優先保留給女生
* 必須對 R-Ladies Taipei 有貢獻，例如：請勇敢舉手發問

title: R in Production: An Evening with Hadley Wickham
description:
Join us in partnership with [R-Ladies NYC](https://www.meetup.com/rladies-newyork/) for an enlightening evening with [Hadley Wickham](https://hadley.nz/) as we explore the realm of **"R in Production."**

**Agenda:**

* **6:00 PM CET / 12:00 PM EDT Networking with R-Ladies Rome & NYC**
* **7:00 PM CET / 1:00 PM EDT Dr. Hadley Wickham's Talk + Q&A**

"This talk will discuss what it means to put R *"in production"*. This isn't something you'll necessarily encounter early in your data science journey, but it's useful to have a strong mental model in place for when you inevitably face it. I'll discuss the three principles that I've found useful for distinguishing production for regular analyses:

**Not just once:** Successful data science projects are not a one-off, but will be run repeatedly for months or years. I'll discuss some of the challenges for creating R scripts and applications that run repeatedly, handle new data seamlessly, and adapt to evolving analytical requirements without constant manual intervention.

**Not just my computer:** the transition from development on your laptop (usually windows or mac) to a production environment (usually linux) introduces a number of challenges. Here, I'll discuss some strategies for making R code portable, how you can minimise pain when something inevitably goes wrong, and few unresolved auth challenges that we're currently working on.

**Not just me:** R is not just a tool for individual analysts but a platform for collaboration. I'll cover some of the best practices for writing readable, understandable code, and how you might go about sharing that code with your colleagues." - *[Hadley Wickham](https://hadley.nz/)*

In this engaging event, Hadley, renowned Chief Scientist at Positi PBC (former RStudio), will guide us into the practical applications and best practices for deploying R solutions in real-world production environments. From effective code structuring to ensuring scalability and reliability, Hadley will share invaluable insights and actionable tips for harnessing the full potential of R in production settings.

Whether you're a seasoned data scientist or just beginning your journey with R, this event promises to equip you with the knowledge and tools needed to successfully deploy R solutions and drive impactful outcomes in your organization.

title: let‘s GIT to it
description:
What if you could turn back time? With git, an open source software for version control, you can do that - at least for your code, should the need arise.

In our next workshop, Dominique and Nicolas from ipt (https://ipt.ch/) will teach you how to use git to version control your code, and to effectively collaborate with others on that code.

**Workshop Preparation:**
No previous experience with git required. Please bring a (charged) laptop with the following components installed:

* Git ([Download Git](https://git-scm.com/downloads)): Download and install Git, making a note of where on your computer you install it.
* R ([Download R](https://cloud.r-project.org/)): Download and Install R (if not already installed).
* RStudio ([Download RStudio Desktop](https://www.rstudio.com/products/RStudio/#Desktop)): Download and Install RStudio (if not already installed)
* GitHub account: On [GitHub](https://github.com/) create yourself a free GitHub account.

(Source:[https://www.geo.uzh.ch](https://www.geo.uzh.ch/microsite/reproducible_research/post/rr-rstudio-git/))

If you would like some help installing, use the time slot from 17:30 - 18:00. We are happy to help you out!

During the workshop, you will be asked to work with the following repository: https://github.com/iptch/2023-03-git-techbier

**Program:**
17:30 - 18:00 possibility to get support for installing RStudio and git
18:00 - 18:10 welcome and ice-breaker questions
18:15 - 20:15 workshop
20:15 onwards: socializing & sponsored apéro

**Location:**
Raum SI 122
KV Business School Zürich AG
Bildungszentrum Sihlpost
Sihlpostgasse 2
8004 Zürich

We are very excited to be able to host this workshop for you together with ipt, and we look forward to chatting to you after the event at the apéro!
Seraphina, Céline, Luisa

description:
Join R-Ladies Seattle for our monthly happy hour! Come and network with local R-Ladies to make new connections and discuss all things R. This is a casual event so you can stop by any time. You'll find us by our logo (and probably Kim's rainbow hair).

Chips and salsa provided!

Non-alcoholic and alcoholic beverages available. Attendees are responsible for their own tab.

title: Reproducible book club #11
description:
This is the 11th meeting of our book club where we are working through, chapter by chapter, the wonderful book by Bruno Rodrigues, [“Building reproducible analytical pipelines with R”](https://raps-with-r.dev/).
In this meeting, Angelica Becerra, Data Scientist at Cynkra, will guide us through [Chapter 15](https://raps-with-r.dev/ci_cd.html). We'll learn about CI/CD and how you can leverage GitHub Actions for it.
This will be an online event where you are welcome to code along and discuss any questions or problems that might arise. We are all learning this together!

The meeting will not be recorded.

title: Women in Mathematics Day at ANU: market day stall needs staff
description: Women in Mathematics Day ([May12](https://aus01.safelinks.protection.outlook.com/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fmay12.womeninmaths.org%2F&data=05%7C02%7Calice.richardson%40anu.edu.au%7C2592de32c4d94b1b211908dc6cc5b8a8%7Ce37d725cab5c46249ae5f0533e486437%7C0%7C0%7C638504843672203376%7CUnknown%7CTWFpbGZsb3d8eyJWIjoiMC4wLjAwMDAiLCJQIjoiV2luMzIiLCJBTiI6Ik1haWwiLCJXVCI6Mn0%3D%7C0%7C%7C%7C&sdata=GROKWD19ZNxhEBC75SlbhGyydsuCBRG1ORO9dwGPcH8%3D&reserved=0)) is celebrated on May 12 each year, but this year at ANU the main events will take place on Monday 13 May (: [https://maths.anu.edu.au/news-events/events/may12-celebrating-women-mathematics](https://maths.anu.edu.au/news-events/events/may12-celebrating-women-mathematics)). R-Ladies Canberra has taken up the offer of a stall at the market day session in the afternoon. We are looking for a couple of ANU-based R-Ladies to come along for all or part of that time to meet the participants in a market and explain what a great organisation R-Ladies is! We did send out an email about this but now that time is short, we've created this event to bring it back to your attention. Please RSVP if you are able to help out, even for a short time. Hope to see you there!

title: Back to basics: chart makeover
description: Sarah will present three charts and discuss how they can be improved to tell a stronger story by changing chart type, axes, and labels, and dodging common information design pitfalls.

title: Kratka ali slatka uvodna R radionica
description:
Jesi li uvijek bila znatiželjna u vezi R-a, ali te programiranje pomalo plaši? Pridruži nam se 10.05. u 12 sati!

Na ovoj kratkoj, ali slatkoj R-adionici, pokrit ćemo osnove R-a, tidyversa i vizualizacije podataka. Radionica je osmišljena kako bi novim korisnicima pružila samopouzdanje u osnovno korištenje R-a i motivaciju za nastavak samostalnog učenja. Radionica ne pretpostavlja prethodno iskustvo s R-om ili programiranjem.

description:
Estamos felices de anunciar nuestro primer encuentro del año.

En esta oportunidad tendremos tres invitadas de lujo que nos presentarán su trabajo en R en temas variados. No se lo pierdan!

title: Actions to increase diversity in the R-Ladies community -  São Paulo
description:
Hello friends! We are excited to announce our first international speaker of the year: Beatriz Milz from R-Ladies São Paulo chapter in Brazil. She will be sharing the R-Ladies São Paulo stories and their journey to increase diversity in their community.

This presentation aims to share the diversity issues in the R-Ladies São Paulo community and the actions being taken to increase diversity. The idea is to present some important concepts, such as intersectionality, and discuss the importance of understanding the local context. Also, I drew some reflections that can be useful for thinking about what actions we can take in different R-Ladies chapters to seek to improve diversity.

Based in this post: https://rladies.org/blog/2023-06-05-increasing-diversity-sao-paulo/

title: Introduction to R
description:
Do you want to work with R but have no idea where to start? Are you intimidated by the idea of learning a programming language? Have you always been interested in R but found the amount of online resources overwhelming?

R-ladies Amsterdam is hosting a beginner's event to give you a gentle introduction to the R programming language. This event will be an in-person workshop at the University of Amsterdam. We will explain some basic concepts of R and run code together. You get the opportunity to ask questions and work together with other people who are learning R. We will also provide some snacks :)

Itinerary:
18.30 - 18.45 Welcome
18.45 - 20.30 Workshop
20.30 - 21.00 Networking

We kindly ask you to bring your own laptop and install R and RStudio before you arrive! You can find instructions on how to do so here: [https://posit.co/download/rstudio-desktop/](https://posit.co/download/rstudio-desktop/)

title: RYouWithMe?
description:
Come join us for a **hands-on session on the basics of R and Rstudio**!

This month we keep going with the *"*VizWhiz”*"* modules of ***RYouWithMe,*** a series of online learning resources for using R - designed by us, for us.

The series is oriented towards R beginners and aims to provide a foundation for future RLadies Sydney activities.

You can start going through the Modules on your own and join us for questions, help and troubleshooting during the meetup.

Find it here: [https://rladiessydney.org/courses/ryouwithme/](https://rladiessydney.org/courses/ryouwithme/)

**How to get involved!**

As an R-Lady learner based in Sydney:

1. Join the R-Ladies Sydney Slack workspace ***[here](https://join.slack.com/t/rladies-sydney/shared_invite/zt-2czd4i8jq-qKuml1FeCHaJPuaYkRDXHQ)***;
2. Introduce yourself in the *#ryouwithme_intros_2024 channel*;
3. Keep an eye out in the *#ryouwithme_general_2024* channel for announcements;
4. Share your successes, ask questions, help out your peers in the *#ryouwithme_help_2024*.
5.

Bring your laptop!

title: Modelos Aditivos Generalizados
description:
**Se viene un nuevo Meetup VIRTUAL! Organizado por R-Ladies Buenos Aires y R-Ladies Ushuaia. En esta oportunidad María Victoria Quiroga nos acompaña para contarte todo lo que necesitas saber sobre Modelos Aditivos Generalizados (GAM’s), una solución ideal para modelar datos que se generan mediante la suma de diferentes componentes (como descomponer series temporales, descomponer la variabilidad espacial en diferentes componentes, diferentes componentes que representan diferentes fuentes de variabilidad, entre otros).**
**Para sacarle provecho al meetup es conveniente tener un conocimiento básico de R y necesitarás los siguientes paquetes: mgcv, ggplot2, itsadug, data.table, car, grid y lubridate.**
**¡Te esperamos!**

title: Modelos Aditivos Generalizados en R
description:
**¡Se viene un nuevo Meetup VIRTUAL!** Organizado por **[R-Ladies Buenos Aires](https://rladiesba.netlify.app/) y [R-Ladies Ushuaia](https://twitter.com/RLadiesUshuaia).** En esta oportunidad **María Victoria Quiroga** nos acompaña para contarte todo lo que necesitas saber sobre **Modelos Aditivos Generalizados (GAM’s), u**na solución ideal para modelar datos que se generan mediante la suma de diferentes componentes (como descomponer series temporales, descomponer la variabilidad espacial en diferentes componentes, diferentes componentes que representan diferentes fuentes de variabilidad, entre otros).

Para sacarle provecho al meetup es conveniente tener un conocimiento básico de R y estadísticas descriptivas y necesitarás los siguientes paquetes: mgcv, ggplot2, itsadug, data.table, car, grid y lubridate.

Finalmente, te invitamos a compartir el link al meetup para quien quiera inscribirse. Por favor, para preservar el evento te pedimos que **no compartas el link de zoom por fuera de las asistentes inscriptas**.

**¡Te esperamos!**

title: Data Challenge Solution Showcase
description:
International Women’s Day took place on March 8th, 2024. The theme for this year was “Invest in women: Accelerate progress”.

In honour of this theme, R-Ladies Ottawa hosted **a month-long data challenge!** You can find the [full details](https://rladies-ottawa.github.io/events/2024/iwd) on our website.

title: 初探 Prompt Flow：開發生成式 AI 應用的利器！
description:
**[報名網址]**
麻煩移駕至此報名：
[https://www.accupass.com/go/rladies_promptflow](https://www.accupass.com/go/rladies_promptflow)

**[Agenda]**
19:00 \~ 19:30 開放入場
19:30 \~ 19:40 開場
19:40 \~ 20:40 主議程：初探 Prompt Flow：開發生成式 AI 應用的利器！
20:40 \~ 自由交流時間

**[Talk Information]**
生成式人工智慧的應用日益普及，其帶來的效益已是眾所周知。
然而，市面上的生成式 AI 通常傾向於通用應用。若要對特定領域或需求進行精準應用，則需要設計出適合的提示語。
提示語可視為我們應用程式的原始碼，需要持續優化。不同於程式碼的是，提示語是非確定性的。
換句話說，提示工程是一門玄學，這個和一般的應用開發完全不一樣。
因此，擁有一個優秀的框架或工具將極大提高效率與便利性。
讓我們一同來了解 Prompt Flow 吧！它是一個端到端旨在簡化提示工程的工具。

**[Speaker Information]**
**Alan Tsai**
熱愛學習、分享與教學，專精於 .NET (Core)、ASP.NET Core、Azure、架構設計、Container、DevOps、Bot Framework (chatbot) 等領域。
堅信教學是最佳的學習方式，喜歡將新知轉化為文章、影片、課程與演講，並在「Alan Tsai 的學習筆記」部落格分享。
同時，也熱衷於翻譯軟體/文章，目前已翻譯了兩本書。
榮幸擔任微軟最有價值專家 (MVP) - Azure和微軟認證講師 (MCT)，並擁有數十張與Azure相關的證照。
除了工作之外，我也非常喜歡參與和協助社群活動，是Study4.TW的成員之一。閒暇時，我喜歡閱讀小說和打打手遊。
如果有機會參加活動，歡迎找我聊聊哦！
期待與大家建立聯繫，一同學習和成長！

* 部落格：[https://blog.alantsai.net/](https://blog.alantsai.net/)
* Facebook：[http://fb.alantsai.net/](http://fb.alantsai.net/)

title: Venez nous rencontrer à Paris pour une soirée d’apprentissage et d’échange
description:
Nous sommes ravis de vous convier à notre prochain événement. Nous aurons le plaisir d’accueillir en personne María Paula Caldas et Julie Aubert, le 29 avril 2024 à partir de 19h dans les locaux de datacraft ([https://datacraft.paris/](https://datacraft.paris/)) situés au 3 Rue Rossini, 75009 Paris. Vous pouvez trouver l'adresse sur Google Maps à cette adresse : [https://maps.app.goo.gl/1VKXWyhF157xr5Qx6](https://maps.app.goo.gl/1VKXWyhF157xr5Qx6).

Le programme de l’événement est le suivant :

**19h - 19h05 :** Introduction de la communauté R-Ladies Paris
**19h05 - 19h35 :** « Création des packages dans R », María Paula Caldas
**19h35 - 20h05 :** « Développement pratique des modèles d’apprentissage statistique avec {Tidymodels} », Julie Aubert
**20h05 - 20h10 :** Mot de clôture
**20h10 - 20h30 :** Réception et échange entre la communauté avec distribution de goodies R

Vous trouverez plus de détails sur nos intervenantes et leurs présentations ci-dessous.

**Biographie de la première intervenante :**
María Paula Caldas est une data scientist à l'OCDE, où elle participe à des projets d'analyse des politiques publiques, de la recherche en économie, et à la production de statistiques internationales. Utilisatrice de R depuis presque 10 ans, elle s'intéresse dernièrement à l'analyse spatiale, au traitement des données avec Arrow, et à la création d'écosystèmes de paquets R pour faciliter le travail en équipe. Investie depuis plusieurs années dans la communauté R, María Paula co-anime actuellement le réseau d'utilisateurs de R/Python/Git de l'OCDE, et contribue (quand elle peut !) à des projets communautaires et open source tels que rOpenSci, R-Ladies, r4ds-es et RencontresR.
**Description de l’intervention :**
Suis-je prête à créer mon propre paquet ? Quel est l'intérêt si je travaille en solo ? Comment puis-je partager mon paquet avec les autres ? Nous essaierons d'aborder ces questions ensemble, en partageant nos expériences pendant que nous créons, documentons, testons et partageons un paquet en moins de 30 minutes. Tous les niveaux sont les bienvenus !

**Biographie de la deuxième intervenante :**
Julie Aubert est ingénieure de recherche INRAE en statistiques dans l'équipe SOLsTIS de l’unité MIA Paris-Saclay. Elle travaille dans le domaine de la modélisation et l’apprentissage statistique appliqués aux sciences du vivant avec une spécialisation pour les données omiques en écologie microbienne. Dans ce contexte, elle collabore avec des chercheurs issus de domaines aussi variés que l’agro-écologie, la génétique des plantes ou des bovins, la gestion des risques en agriculture ou l'alimentation
et développe des packages R pour diffuser les méthodes proposées. Elle anime également le réseau StateOftheR du département MathNum ([https://stateofther.netlify.app/](https://stateofther.netlify.app/)).

**Description de l’intervention :**
Tidymodels regroupe un ensemble de packages R facilitant l’utilisation de méthodes d’apprentissage statistique (telles que les forêts aléatoires, les modèles linéaires ...) dans un cadre unifié et “tidy”. Dans cette présentation, nous verrons comment utiliser ces packages pour prétraiter les données, construire, entraîner et évaluer un modèle, optimiser des hyperparamètres et tout ce qu'il est important de savoir pour mener de bout en bout un projet d’apprentissage statistique supervisé.

**Si vous prévoyez de participer à notre événement en distanciel,** veuillez vous inscrire via ce formulaire afin de recevoir le lien Zoom **🔗 [https://us06web.zoom.us/meeting/register/tZUrdOuuqD0qG9Vx_WPFSj_5VWVxCmbX9dqm](https://us06web.zoom.us/meeting/register/tZUrdOuuqD0qG9Vx_WPFSj_5VWVxCmbX9dqm).**

Cet événement est ouvert à toutes et à tous ! Assurez-vous de réserver votre place dès aujourd'hui.

Nous avons hâte de vous voir le 29 avril à Paris ! 🤩

title: Command Line Programming in R
description:
This workshop delves into the world of R programming from the command line interface. It empowers participants to leverage the direct interaction and control offered by the command line, enhancing their R workflow.
The focus will be on:

* **Core functionalities:** Mastering essential R commands for data manipulation, analysis, and visualization.
* **Scripting:** Crafting R scripts to automate repetitive tasks and improve reproducibility.
* **Efficiency:** Utilizing the command line for streamlined data exploration and analysis.

By the end of the workshop, attendees will be equipped to:

* Navigate the R command line with confidence.
* Execute core R operations for data management and analysis.
* Construct effective R scripts for automation and efficiency.
* Gain a deeper understanding of the R environment.

This workshop is ideal for those seeking to elevate their R proficiency and unlock the power of command-line programming for data science tasks.

title: Embracing open-source software to implement precision medicine
description:
We are so excited to announce our next virtual meetup, which will serve as a great opportunity to learn how to embrace open-source software to implement precision medicine.

Join us for an exciting talk with Guillaume Desachy - Head of Biometrics, Pierre Fabre, on April 24 at 7 pm CEST.

**Abstract:**
In recent years, the pharmaceutical industry has undergone two significant paradigm shifts, each transforming the landscape of drug development and collaboration.

The first paradigm shift revolves around the implementation of precision medicine, where the concept of biomarkers plays a pivotal role. The term biomarker being widely used, one could think that its meaning is well-understood. But it is not always the case. Actually, what is a biomarker? And why are biomarkers so important?

The second paradigm shift concerns the adoption of programming languages, notably the rise of R alongside traditional tools like SAS. Up until recently, one had to choose between R and SAS. Today, professionals with a quantitative background possess proficiency in at least two programming languages, reflecting the pharmaceutical industry's evolving technological landscape. Community-driven knowledge-sharing has emerged as a cornerstone in navigating these advancements.

With this in mind, I led the development of a community of R users within a big pharma. Over a span of three years, this community experienced exponential growth, expanding from 200 members to 1,600+ members.

In the development of this community, three pivotal questions emerged:

1\. What is the value of your community?
2\. What is a Key Performance Indicator \(KPI\) of your community?
3\. What is the Return On Investment \(ROI\) of your community?

Back in 2022, we were not able to answer these questions, but things changed drastically in 2023!

During this R-Ladies Paris MeetUp, you will hear what the value of a community is, its KPI and its ROI and why building a community is key to drive innovation to bring medicines to patients.

If you are up for joining us, please register for the meetup here 🔗 https://us06web.zoom.us/meeting/register/tZEtceCtqz8iHtN-5jDI7Hwi5tUqdKbyPc7u

We are very much looking forward to seeing many of you!

title: R-Ladies startup meeting! Basic ggplot2 and plotting flowers
description:
The room is S21!

Welcome to the first ever R-Ladies Trondheim event! Since this is our first meeting, and I don't know exatly the audience, I thought I will do a short tutorial for basic use of the R package ggplot2, which is a fantastic tool for data visualization and plotting. As an example of how flexible this package is, I will show how to use ggplot2 to create various flowers! Bring your computer with R and Rstudio installed, and get ready for some spring flowers!

For the workshop, it is an advantage if you are a bit familiar with R, but you do not need to be familiar with ggplot2 from before. If you are not familiar with R and want help installing it on your computer, feel free to come half an hour early, and I can help you with that.

Since this is our first ever meeting, I would also like to set aside some time at the end to chat and discuss any future wishes for what we could use this group for. I look forward to seeing some new faces and seeing what this group can become!

Follow-along code for the meeting: https://github.com/emmaSkarstein/R-Ladies-Trondheim

title: Is ShinyApp in R Production ready?
description:
In a previous Meetup our own Mari Plaza has presented an [introduction of Golem ](https://www.canva.com/design/DAF7twCZcLI/jRhZuTGeiVqHTuPamPV3iw/view?utm_content=DAF7twCZcLI&utm_campaign=designshare&utm_medium=link&utm_source=editor)which allows you for sure to write a ShinyApp with the production ready mind-set. That means you write a ShinyApp like a package, including all relevant components of Software Engineering such modules, tests, dependencies, git, code coverage and so on therefore your Web Application developed in R like a ShinyApp has the correct architecture to go to production. This time is not an introduction anymore, it is a **3 hours workshop**, starting with 1 hour presentation about how you plan your application and 2 hours practical experience on how you use Golem.

**Bring your computer** and be ready to ask questions and test by yourself what we are talking about.

Since the content is interesting and dense, you could join us in the preview activities starting at the end of February 2024, but you can always catch-up, even on the day of the event.
The preview activities includes:

* Join our SLACK dedicated channel for discussing the concepts and review related materials.
* Follow posts in our LinkedIn accounts
* Regular updates through the messages of this event on MeetUp.

We are really happy to host this event with care and willingness to learn more about the great world of software engineering.

title: R for geospatial data science and public health surveillance
description:
**💡Join us as we learn about Geospatial with R**👇🏻

Geospatial health data are essential to inform public health and policy. These data can be used to understand geographic and temporal patterns, identify risk factors, measure inequalities, and quickly detect outbreaks.

In this talk, **Dr. Moraga** will provide an overview of R packages and statistical methods for geospatial data analysis and health surveillance. She will discuss data biases and availability issues, and present health surveillance applications where R has been used to develop models that can integrate complex data from different sources and resolutions to predict disease risk and identify targets for intervention.

Finally, she will show how R can help with communication and dissemination which is essential to enable broad access to data and to develop and implement appropriate health policies and improve population health globally.

During this meeting, Gabe Winter (R-Ladies Cologne) will show us why testing your code is so essential, what unit tests are, and how you can incorporate it into your developing practices (based on chapter 12).

If you want to try it out, be sure to have RStudio with {rmarkdown} and {knitr} installed.

This will be an online event where you are welcome to code along and discuss any questions or problems that might arise. We are all learning this together!

The meeting will not be recorded.

title: Get to Know the Epidemiologist R Handbook – the R Code Reference Manual
description:
We are delighted to announce our next virtual meetup. Join us for an exciting talk with Paulina Jedynak, a Biostatistics and Bioinformatics Postdoctoral Researcher at the Barcelona Institute of Global Health ISGlobal, on April 11 at 7 pm CEST. Paulina will present The Epidemiologist R Handbook, a great resource for learning both R and epidemiology/stats concepts for both complete beginners and advanced R users.

**Description:**

**The Epidemiologist R Handbook** ([https://epirhandbook.com](https://epirhandbook.com)) is an open-access R reference manual for applied epidemiologists and public health practitioners, available in eight languages and used 2.1 million times by 650,000 people so far. It is a comprehensive resource designed to assist in using R for data analysis, visualization, and interpretation, suitable for people very new to R but also for experienced R users, looking for best practices and tips. In order to make it accessible and user-friendly, each of its 50 chapters is accompanied by clear explanations and step-by-step instructions, with brief examples that address common epidemiological problems. The Handbook covers a wide range of topics, including transitioning to R from other software, data import and export, cleaning and management, working with dates and ‘epiweeks’, generating epidemic curves, performing survival analysis, using basic statistical methods applied to epidemiology, etc.
The Epidemiologist R Handbook is produced by an independent collaboration of epidemiologists and it is hosted and curated by the [Applied Epi](https://www.appliedepi.org/) – a nonprofit organization and grassroots movement of frontline epis from around the world, aiming to strengthen epidemiological practice through training, tools, and support. The organization delivers practical R training courses to over 350 public health agencies worldwide and hosts the [Applied Epi Community](https://community.appliedepi.org/) with a Q&A forum for R code and public health practice.

If you are up for joining us, please register for the meetup here 🔗 https://us06web.zoom.us/meeting/register/tZwud-CgqD8rGdfm8rRU7Rq6pEvRNTv3_F16#/registration

title: Data Challenge 2024
description:
*Please note: You don't need to have attended the [kickoff event](https://www.meetup.com/rladies-ottawa/events/299173960/) to participate! Anyone can participate!*

International Women’s Day took place on March 8th, 2024. The theme for this year is “Invest in women: Accelerate progress”.

In honour of this theme, R-Ladies Ottawa is hosting **a month-long data challenge!**

You can find the [full details](https://rladies-ottawa.github.io/events/2024/iwd) on our website.

**Challenge**

The objective of this data challenge is to analyze *investing in women*. This is a very open-ended challenge, so be as creative as you’d like!

Some examples include:

* Analyze venture capital investment data to understand trends in funding for women-led startups.
* Analyze businesses or organizations that promote women’s empowerment.
* Analyze data on gender diversity in leadership positions.

You can use any data source, but the following are examples:

* [Measuring the value of women’s contribution to the Canadian economy: New insights based on recent work](https://www150.statcan.gc.ca/n1/pub/36-28-0001/2023002/article/00001-eng.htm)
* [Statistics Canada’s ](https://www.statcan.gc.ca/en/topics-start/gender_diversity_and_inclusion)*[Gender, Diversity and Inclusion Statistics Hub](https://www.statcan.gc.ca/en/topics-start/gender_diversity_and_inclusion)*
* [Statistics Canada’s ](https://www150.statcan.gc.ca/t1/tbl1/en/tv.action?pid=3310066001)*[Average percentage of women and men in management positions](https://www150.statcan.gc.ca/t1/tbl1/en/tv.action?pid=3310066001)*[ tables](https://www150.statcan.gc.ca/t1/tbl1/en/tv.action?pid=3310066001)

**Guidelines**

Although this challenge is hosted by *R-Ladies* Ottawa, you don’t have to use R to participate. You could use R, Python, a combination of the two, or anything you’re comfortable with!

No previous programming experience is required! If you’re interested in participating, but aren’t sure where to start, check out the recordings from our [Introduction to R workshops](https://rladies-ottawa.gitub.io/events/2024/intro_to_r.html) that we hosted in early 2024. The workshops will give you a solid foundation in data analysis and data visualization using R.

While anyone is welcome to participate in the challenge and submit an entry, **only submissions by women and gender minorities who reside in Canada will be eligible for the prizes.**

**Prizes**

You can participate individually, or in teams of up to 5 people!
Prize details will be released at a later date.

**How to submit an entry**

This will be a month-long data challenge, and you’ll have until **April 7th** to submit your entry. Your submission can be in any form you like – it can be a Quarto document, an image, a link to a webpage, or a link to a video, for example.

There are two options for submitting an entry:

* Option 1 (preferred option): If you have experience with GitHub and Quarto, you can create a pull request on [this repository](https://github.com/RLadies-Ottawa/rladies-ottawa.github.io) and add your submission to the [“Submissions”](https://rladies-ottawa.github.io/events/2024/iwd.html#submissions) part of [this page](https://rladies-ottawa.github.io/events/2024/iwd)
* Option 2: If you don’t have experience with GitHub, you can email [ottawa@rladies.org](mailto:ottawa@rladies.org) a link to your submission materials and we will add the link to the [“Submissions”](https://rladies-ottawa.github.io/events/2024/iwd.html#submissions) part of [this page](https://rladies-ottawa.github.io/events/2024/iwd)

**Submitting an entry**

After the submission deadline, R-Ladies Ottawa will host a virtual event where participants can showcase their work. This is a great way to learn from one another and to connect with other like-minded individuals!

The date and time of the showcase event will be announced soon.

fundamentals without needing to code.

title: ¿Tu código demora tres días en ejecutarse? ¡Es hora de paralelizar!
description:
¡Hola!

¿Tu código demora mucho tiempo en ejecutarse? Quizás sea la señal que estabas esperando para aprender acerca de paralelizar procesos con el paquete {furrr}. Con este paquete puedes combinar las capacidades de programación funcional de {purrr} con las de procesamiento paralelo de {futures} (**f**utures + p**urrr** = **furrr**).
La sesión estará a cargo de Stephanie Orellana, Científica de Datos.
¡Próximamente agregaremos detalles sobre cómo prepararte para el taller!

**Código de conducta**

¡Te esperamos!

title: Good (Enough) Practices for Scientific Reproducibility
description:
**Good Enough Practices for Scientific Reproducibility**

Computational tools are making it easier than ever to collect, analyze, and share data and results in transparent and reproducible ways. But… how do we achieve this desired reproducibility?

Join us at our next Meetup on **April 3 from 6:00 to 7:30 pm**, in person at NCEAS, where Colleen Smith and Camila Vargas Poulsen will share Good (Enough) Practices for Achieving Scientific Reproducibility. Through this presentation, the speakers will discuss tools and systems to help you organize and document your work for better reproducibility. We will discuss why aiming for reproducibility is important yet challenging and how these systems can help you make your work more robust and facilitate collaboration (including with yourself).

**About the speakers**
Colleen Smith is a biologist with the U.S. Fish and Wildlife Service, where she writes recovery plans for endangered species. Before that, she spent 9 years in academia, where she analyzed many different types of datasets to investigate how bee biodiversity is responding to global change. In her spare time she likes to run, hike and spend as much time outdoors with her dog Otto as she can.

Camila Vargas Poulsen is the Data Training Coordinator at the NCEAS Learning Hub, where she manages, maintains, and develops the NCEAS data science training portfolio. She also leads data science courses like coreR and a range of modular training for scientists who want to integrate data science tools into their analytical workflow. Outside work, you can find Camila in, next to, or on the ocean and cooking family-style dinners with friends.

venues.name: National Center for Ecological Analysis and Synthesis

title: RYouWithMe?
description:
Come join us for a **hands-on session on the basics of R and Rstudio**!

This month we keep going with the *"Clean it up"* modules of ***RYouWithMe,*** a series of online learning resources for using R - designed by us, for us.

The series is oriented towards R beginners and aims to provide a foundation for future RLadies Sydney activities.

You can start going through the Modules on your own and join us for questions, help and troubleshooting during the meetup.

Find it here: [https://rladiessydney.org/courses/ryouwithme/](https://rladiessydney.org/courses/ryouwithme/)

**How to get involved!**

As an R-Lady learner based in Sydney:

1. Join the R-Ladies Sydney Slack workspace ***[here](https://join.slack.com/t/rladies-sydney/shared_invite/zt-2czd4i8jq-qKuml1FeCHaJPuaYkRDXHQ)***;
2. Introduce yourself in the *#ryouwithme_intros_2024 channel*;
3. Keep an eye out in the *#ryouwithme_general_2024* channel for announcements;
4. Share your successes, ask questions, help out your peers in the *#ryouwithme_help_2024*.
5.

Bring your laptop!

***R-Ladies Sydney organisers***
***Thank you Nous Group for supporting the RLadies!***
*Find us on Twitter @rladiessydney for more information on news, presentations and code!*
\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*

By taking part in an R-Ladies Sydney event you grant the community organisers full rights to use the images resulting from the photography/video filming/media, and any reproductions or adaptations of the images for publicity, fund-raising or other purposes to help achieve the community’s aims. This might include (but is not limited to), the right to use them in their printed and online publicity, social media, press releases and funding applications. If you do not wish to be recorded in these media please inform a community organiser.\*
***Code of Conduct***\*: R-Ladies Sydney is dedicated to providing a welcoming and harassment-free event experience for everyone, regardless of gender, gender identity and expression, age, sexual orientation, disability, physical appearance, body size, race, ethnicity, or religion (or lack thereof). We do not tolerate harassment of event participants in any form, for any event venue, including talks, workshops, parties, Twitter and other online media.\*
***Community Policies***\*: Please note, cis men are welcome to join our meetups when they come as a guest of a woman participant or they bring a woman with them. We highly encourage speakers and teachers at R-Ladies Sydney events to be of minority genders.\*

title: [ONLINE] Capítulo 1: Visualização - Clube do Livro R for Data Science (2ed)
description:
• CONTEÚDO
Esse é o segundo encontro do clube do livro **R for Data Science** (2 edição)!
Esse livro foi escrito por Hadley Wickham (criador do ggplot2), Mine Çetinkaya-Rundel, e Garrett Grolemund.
Iremos usar a versão que está sendo traduzida para português, disponível **gratuitamente** e **online** em [https://cienciadedatos.github.io/pt-r4ds/](https://cienciadedatos.github.io/pt-r4ds/) .
Clube do livro organizado por R-Ladies São Paulo, liderado por Beatriz Milz e Luana Antunes.
Saiba mais em: [https://github.com/R-Ladies-Sao-Paulo/2024-clube-do-livro-r4ds](https://github.com/R-Ladies-Sao-Paulo/2024-clube-do-livro-r4ds)
O evento é aberto para TODAS as pessoas interessadas e será transmitido através do Youtube:[ https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=TaAog3K0toA](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=TaAog3K0toA)
• LEITURAS DA SEMANA
\- Capítulo 1 \- Visualização de Dados \- [https://cienciadedatos.github.io/pt-r4ds/](https://cienciadedatos.github.io/pt-r4ds/)

\*\*\*
Por favor leia todas as informações abaixo!
• LOCAL DO EVENTO:
Canal das R-Ladies São Paulo no Youtube
• CÓDIGO DE CONDUTA
Disponível aqui: [https://github.com/rladies/.github/blob/master/CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md#Portuguese](https://github.com/rladies/.github/blob/master/CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md#Portuguese)

\*\*\*
• SOBRE AS R-LADIES
R-Ladies é uma organização que promove a diversidade de gênero na comunidade da linguagem R. Nosso principal objetivo é promover a linguagem computacional estatística R compartilhando conhecimento, assim, quem tiver interesse na linguagem será bem-vinda, independente do nível de conhecimento. Nosso principal público-alvo são as pessoas que se identificam com o gênero feminino, portanto, mulheres cis, mulheres trans, bem como pessoas não-binárias e queer.

O evento é gratuito, e organizado com muito carinho, ajude a espalhar a palavra, compartilhe com outras ladies :)

title: Toronto R-Ladies is back: Lightning Talks!
description:

description:

title: Explorando a visualização de dados no R com tidyverse
description:
O evento '**Explorando a visualização de dados no R com *tidyverse***' será ministrado pela coorganizadora da comunidade R-Ladies Natal, **Jeanne Franco**.

A palestra ocorrerá no **dia 30/03/2024, às 19h, via Zoom**. O link do evento se encontra aqui na página do Meetup e nos comentários abaixo.

Os participantes devem ter o software RStudio instalado no computador e baixar o pacote *tidyverse* para acompanhar as atividades do evento.

O pacote *tidyverse* é considerado um 'metapacote' que contêm outros pacotes úteis para manipulação e visualização de dados.

**Principais pacotes que serão abordados na palestra:**

* *ggplot2*
* *dplyr*
* *readxl*
* *tibble*
* forcats

Acompanhe as atualizações sobre o evento e outras informações no [Instagram](https://www.instagram.com/rladiesnatal/) da comunidade R-Ladies Natal.

title: Evento de Planejamento dos Próximos Passos do R-Ladies BH
description:
**🌟Evento de Planejamento dos Próximos Passos do R-Ladies BH! 🌸**

Quer ajudar a moldar o futuro das nossas atividades? Venha se juntar a nós para uma sessão colaborativa de brainstorming e planejamento! Este é o momento perfeito para você compartilhar suas ideias, sugerir temas e contribuir para a criação de um calendário de eventos que atenda às necessidades e interesses da nossa comunidade.

**Pauta :**

* Recapitulação dos anos anteriores
* Brainstorm de Atividades (cursos, palestras, conteúdos nas redes sociais, etc),
* Recrutamento de Novas Organizadoras
* Indicação e/ou Voluntariado de Palestrantes e/ou Instrutores para Cursos, Eventos e Treinamentos
* Priorização e Seleção de Temas (temas para eventos, ideias de conteúdo, etc)
* Definição de Calendário
* Planos de Divulgação e Engajamento

**Data: 27 de março de 2024**
**Hora: 20:00 (horário de Brasília) , com duração de até 1 hora.**
**Local: Youtube , canal do RLadies BH (https://www.youtube.com/@RLadiesBeloHorizonte)**

Não importa se você é nova na comunidade ou uma participante frequente, sua voz é importante para nós! Juntas, podemos criar um ano cheio de aprendizado, crescimento e inspiração. Esperamos vê-la! 💻

**FAQs:**

**O que é R?**
R é uma linguagem de programação de computador e ambiente de software para computação estatística e gráficos. Confira aqui (https://www.r-project.org/). Ele é usado para análise e visualização de dados em uma ampla variedade de campos.

**O que é R-Ladies?**
A comunidade R-Ladies é uma organização global que promove a diversidade de gênero na comunidade da linguagem computacional estatística (R). Embora nossos eventos considerem pelo menos o interesse de nível básico em R (algum conhecimento anterior é útil, mas não obrigatório), você é mais que bem-vinde a participar de qualquer uma de nossas sessões para ver o que fazemos.

**Vai ter certificado?**
Não, pois é não será um curso e/ou treinamento

**O evento é gratuito?**
Sim

**Eu sou homem, posso me inscrever?**
Este grupo é principalmente voltado para o público feminino e LGBTQIA+, mas está aberto a todos que concordam com o Código de Conduta (https://rladies.org/code-of-conduct/). Dito isto, se você é um homem e quer participar dos nossos encontros, você está convidado a participar!

**Ficou alguma dúvida?**
É só enviar para belohorizonte@rladies.org

title: International Women’s Day 2024 | Impact the Future
description:
To all women in tech, come and celebrate with us the International Women's Day meetup PARTY! A collaboration of data science meetups in the Greater Seattle area.

To all our allies, this is a women event and is inclusive and everyone is welcome to attend.

Our vision is to build economic opportunity for women through technology. Our programs provide visibility, community, and resources for women in technology.

We all have the power and the responsibility to impact the future of technology and society, with AI as a major part of shaping our world. What will that future look like? And how will we build it? We can use our skills, creativity and passion to create positive change and solve the problems that matter to us and our communities. We can also inspire the next generation of women in tech who will continue to impact the future with their own ideas and innovations.

An event for our local tech community, with a strong focus on knowledge exchange, networking, and learning. Get together with [RLadies Seattle](https://www.meetup.com/rladies-seattle/), [Seattle Spark + AI](https://www.linkedin.com/company/seattle-spark-ai/), [PyLadies Seattle](https://www.linkedin.com/company/pyladies-seattle/), [GDG Bellevue](https://www.linkedin.com/company/gdg-bellevue), [PyData Seattle](https://www.linkedin.com/company/pydata-seattle/), and [Women Techmakers Seattle](https://www.linkedin.com/company/wtmseattle), [Seattle Artificial Intelligence Workshops](https://www.meetup.com/seattle-artificial-intelligence-workshops/)

Event program:

* 6:00 - 6:30 - Candy 🍭🍬 eat, beverages, swag and network - Donated by [Databricks](https://www.databricks.com/) and [Delta Lake](https://delta.io/) 🍿🍔 Hamburguers, Fried Chicken, Tamales, Cuban Sandwich!
* 6:30 - 6:40 - Announcements
* 6:40 - 7:10 - Women Speaker 1, 2, 3
* 7:10 - 7:40 - Women Speaker 4, 5, 6
* 7:40 - 8:20 - Networking + Pie bar! 🍭🍰🧁

We recommend to arrive early as seats are limited and are first come first serve.

2 Parking options:

* There is pay parking at the building.
* [Bellevue Place and Lincoln Square parking](https://bellevuecollection.com/visit-here/parking/) is complimentary with validation from Bellevue Place and Lincoln Square retail, restaurants and entertainment. After 8:00 p.m. – No validation is necessary.

title: R-Ladies Denver Relaunch: fusen, litr, and readme2pkg discussion
description:
Denver RLadies is rebooting! Our upcoming hybrid meeting will focus on new tools that take a ‘literate programming’ approach to package building.

As more people get into R package development, some have noticed that the ‘package story’ can get lost. R packages are composed of many files. But these multiple, un-ordered constituents may yield an unclear big-picture package narrative.

Our meeting will bring together like-minded thinkers and tool creators in this space, authors of the [{litr}](https://jacobbien.github.io/litr-project/index.html), [{fusen}](https://github.com/Thinkr-open/fusen/), and [{readme2pkg}](https://github.com/EvaMaeRey/readme2pkg.template) packages. Each of these tools is motivated by keeping the package creation narrative in one place (an Rmarkdown file). But implementations are different. Our meeting will allow for a comparative look at these approaches and illuminate similarities and differences.

Presenters:
Gina Reynolds: [{readme2pkg}](https://github.com/EvaMaeRey/readme2pkg.template)
Jacob Bien: [{litr}](https://jacobbien.github.io/litr-project/index.html)
Sébastien Rochette: [{fusen}](https://github.com/Thinkr-open/fusen/)

R-Ladies [Code of Conduct](https://rladies.org/coc/)

description:
Olá gurias!
Depois de algum tempo, estamos retornando! Com muita vontade de compartilhar, aprender e evoluir junto com vocês.
Contamos com a presença de vocês para retomarmos nosso ritmo de encontros com muita eneRgia!

Nosso encontro será mais uma vez na DB Server, na Tecnopuc.

Precisamos que nos encaminhem nome completo e CPF para conseguirem acesso ao prédio por isso solicitamos que se inscrevam via plataforma Sympla no link: [INSCRIÇÕES AQUI NO SYMPLA](https://www.sympla.com.br/evento/9-meetup-r-ladies-porto-alegre/2370619?_gl=1*4v8t4e*_ga*MjgxNDExMjI0LjE3MDk2ODAzNzU.*_ga_KXH10SQTZF*MTcwOTY4MDM3NC4xLjEuMTcwOTY4MTA1NC40Mi4wLjM3NjMyNjcwNg..)

**Local: Sala de Convivência, DBServer, 5º andar**
**Tecnopuc**
**Av. Ipiranga, 6681 - Partenon, Porto Alegre - RS, 90619-900**

\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-
**Programação:**

**Relatórios automatizados com RMarkdown**
O Markdown é uma ferramenta que possibilita a produção de análises de dados transparentes e replicáveis.
Ao integrar análise estatística e produção de relatórios em um único documento, o RMarkdown simplifica os fluxos de trabalho, aprimorando a qualidade e a confiabilidade das análises e promovendo uma comunicação eficaz de resultados e insights.

*Iara Passos* \- Socióloga e Cientista de Dados\. Possui graduação e mestrado em Sociologia pela UFRGS e é graduanda em Estatística na mesma universidade\. Atualmente é líder de pesquisa no Cappra Institute for Data Science\.

**Microdatasus**
A palestrante Jordana Henz Hammes irá apresentar sobre o pacote Microdatasus para explorar informações de saúde pública com R. Serão discutidos os seguintes tópicos:
\- Introdução ao Microdadosus: entendendo sua importância e funcionalidades;
\- Importação de dados: download e o pré\-processamento dos dados;
\- Exploração dos sistemas e estruturas de dados disponíveis
\- Interação da plataforma com outros pacotes para visualização de dados \(geobr\)

*Jordana Henz Hammes* \- Graduanda do 9° semestre de Fisioterapia pela PUCRS; ⁠Bolsista CNPq de Iniciação Científica do Laboratório de Fisiologia Respiratória \- Centro Infant da PUCRS; ⁠Auxilia na realização de estudos epidemiológicos utilizando Microdatasus \(Rstudio\); ⁠Idealizadora do grupo R for Health Data Science\.

title: CAREERS IN DATA SCIENCE AND STATISTICS
description:
Welcome to our first event of the year!

This event will showcase a panel of experts and discuss the different journeys that brought them to their current role in data science and statistics.

Doors will open at 5.30pm with pizza, drinks and networking. The panel discussion will start at 6pm.

**Professor Di Cook**
Dianne Cook is Professor of Business Analytics at Monash University in Melbourne, Australia. Her research is in the area of data visualisation, especially the visualisation of high-dimensional data using [tours](https://github.com/ggobi/tourr) with low-dimensional projections, and projection pursuit. A current focus is on bridging the gap between [exploratory graphics and statistical inference](http://dicook.github.io/nullabor/). Technology plays an important role in data visualisation for evaluating effectiveness and for public consumption. Di utilises technology such as virtual environments, Amazon’s Mechanical Turk, and eye-tracking in her work, and makes an effort to share her work with open source software. Di is a Fellow of the [American Statistical Association](https://www.amstat.org/), elected member of the [International Statistical Institute](https://isi-web.org/), past-editor of the [Journal of Computational and Graphical Statistics](http://amstat.tandfonline.com/toc/ucgs20/current), elected Member of the [R Foundation](https://www.r-project.org/foundation/), and current editor of the [R Journal](https://journal.r-project.org/). Education is an important part of her contributions, and mentoring graduate research is a significant activity. Several of her students have won the prestigious American Statistical Association John Chambers Software Award, including Hadley Wickham, Yihui Xie, Carson Sievert, and most recently, Monash student Earo Wang.

**Dr. Lauren Smith**
Lauren is a Postdoctoral Fellow in Infectious Disease Modelling at WEHI. Lauren has a broad range of interests spanning across One Health, human/veterinary/wildlife epidemiology, infectious disease and population dynamics modelling, and statistical analysis. Lauren currently works on projects relating to the control of infectious diseases in the Asia-Pacific region, including the development of diagnostic tools to identify individuals recently infected with Plasmodium vivax. In her previous research, she has addressed questions related to the management of animal populations and infectious diseases, and the welfare of domestic animals. This has included using statistical and mathematical models to assess the effectiveness of free-roaming dog population management, rabies control strategies, canine distemper virus dynamics, African swine fever in domestic pigs, and cetacean ecology and conservation.

**Dr. Mun Hua Tan**
Mun Hua is experienced in genomics and bioinformatics, with a hybrid repertoire of wet- and dry-lab skills. She is currently a postdoctoral researcher in the Day Lab at the University of Melbourne, focusing on malaria surveillance and antigenic diversity in natural parasite populations. Mun Hua holds a PhD in Life and Environmental Sciences and a Bachelor of Science in Biotechnology. Her areas of expertise include genome assemblies and annotation, variant calling, phylogenetics, genetics, data processing, and data visualisation. Mun Hua’s professional journey has included work experience in academic research, genomics centres, and industry, on a breadth of biological systems. She enjoys working in interdisciplinary collaborations, where she frequently navigates the intersection between biology and computational analysis.

**Evie Gizem**
Evie has extensive experience working in data science and analytics teams on various projects in the education sector, research, and government. Currently she is a Lead Data Analyst at RMIT, working in the Data & Analytics team. Evie holds a Master of Analytics degree from RMIT and a Bachelor of Science in Mathematics. Her areas of expertise include statistical modelling, machine learning techniques, data processing, programming, and data visualisation. Evie is passionate about effectively communicating data science concepts and provides guidance to both internal and external clients on data science strategies, utilising cutting-edge tools and approaches. She also evaluates the feasibility of data science projects to ensure practical outcomes are achieved.

**Dr. Göknur Giner**
Göknur Giner is a bioinformatician at WEHI. She has been using her statistical and machine learning skills to develop new bioinformatics methods needed for advancing genomic data analysis. In particular, she has been working to better understand the origins of the most invasive forms of breast cancer and to understand cancer metastasis. She has extensive experience working with and developing tools for genomic technologies, such as RNA sequencing, whole-exome sequencing, CRISPR/Cas9, microarray; and experiments conducted with circulating tumor cells, patient-derived xenografts.

See you there !

title: A Tutorial On Linear Regression
description:
Join us at our R- Ladies Chennai virtual meetup for a fun event of networking and learning R. Newbies are welcome too! During this meetup, we are excited to help you learn linear regression.

About the session: In this session, we will analyze a dataset using linear regression to explore the relationship between a response variable and explanatory variables which may be categorical or numerical. We will also quantify the fit of models and learn about outliers, leverage and influence.

title: From Complex to Clear: Understanding Your NLP Models
description:
**Abstract**
When training and evaluating machine learning models, it is important to understand why the model made its decision the way it did. This is what explainable machine learning is all about.
By providing an understanding of modern large language models and how they work, I will explain why explainable machine learning is an essential core part of any model development and evaluation pipeline.

This workshop aims to provide practitioners with practical insights, offering a toolbox of approaches to improve their understanding of models and lift the veil on these seemingly opaque "black box" models.

Throughout the workshop, we will be using an open-source model as a working example and accessing it through the popular framework huggingface. While the common frameworks are based on Python, I will show you how to use the best of both worlds and mix and match Python code chunks with R to learn more about your model.

title: Extending the data science workflow: {vetiver} and {pins}
description: Data practitioners possess a robust skill set in data analysis and model development. However, there's often a gap in understanding tasks beyond the conventional data science workflow, like deploying models as API endpoints or monitoring model decay. In this session, we'll delve into the fundamentals of machine learning operations (MLOps) tasks, leveraging the vetiver and pins packages. Our emphasis will be on exploring various real-world scenarios, good and bad, where these tools can be instrumental in solving practical challenges.

title: Database-ics: an introduction to SQL for R users
description:
As R users, we often read in data directly from files, such as .csv files. However, developing an understanding of SQL gives us an edge when working with big data, as we can connect to databases directly from R, pulling in only the bits that we need for analysis and saving memory. SQL can also be used to efficiently perform simple data manipulation tasks like sorting, filtering, and aggregation on large datasets, which would take significant computational resources in R.

In this RLadies workshop we will run through some basic SQL queries that can be used to retrieve and filter tables from databases, and learn a little bit about how relational databases work. We will then look at how to write and run SQL queries directly from R, and some use cases for this.

In order to fully participate in this workshop, please make sure you have R and RStudio installed on your laptop, and make sure you have the package ‘DBI’ installed by running \`install.packages("DBI")\` before the workshop. We will use this package to connect to a SQL database via R.

After the workshop, we will have ample time for networking and chatting over some snacks and drinks (courtesy of the Software Sustainability Institute).

Chloe Brook is a Data Science Applications Consultant at EPCC, University of Edinburgh. Having studied Biochemistry at university, she is keen to enthuse others who haven’t come directly from computer science backgrounds about the joys of coding and data. She is also an online tutor alongside her day job, and enjoys encouraging people to believe in their ability to learn and grow!

title: Statistics, Epidemiology, Team (Data) Science: Wastewater Surveillance (Virtual)
description:
## Details

R-Ladies Irvine ([https://www.rladiesirvine.org/](https://www.rladiesirvine.org/)) is very excited to host this virtual meeting on Tuesday, March 19, 2024 from 4:30-6 pm PDT!! The two speakers, Professor Katherine Ensor and Dr. Julia Schedler, are affiliated with Houston Wastewater Epidemiology (link [https://hou-wastewater-epi.org/](https://hou-wastewater-epi.org/)) and the Department of Statistics at Rice University. Professor Ensor will illustrate how Spatial-Temporal Modeling is applied to Public Health Surveillance through Wastewater and Dr. Schedler will share three stories about Reproducibility in Team Science.

**Spatial-Temporal Modeling is applied to Public Health Surveillance through Wastewater**
Wastewater surveillance has emerged as a strong tool for city, county, state, and national public health departments. By examining SARS-CoV-2 RNA viral load, wastewater provides a strong signal of the extent of the virus in a community. Measuring and modeling the evolution of the virus in a community is however fraught with many complexities. In this talk, I will explore the spatio-temporal modeling based on weekly observations of a mix of measurements across sewersheds of differing scales and localized lift stations. We will use adaptive temporal methods and the extended Hausdorff distance for spatial correlations of regions of vastly different shapes and serving different sized communities. Examining this case study in the context of statistical engineering and problem-solving, I will highlight some of the challenges and benefits of partnerships between universities and local governments.

**Three Stories About Reproducibility in Team Science**
Some statisticians and data scientists may imagine an ideal world where every scientific manuscript is accompanied by fully documented data sets and all code used to generate all included figures, tables, and numerical results. In the real world, there are many barriers to this ideal, for example, privacy concerns, time and staff constraints, differences in data science workflows, and statisticians getting excited about reproducibility and overwhelming their collaborators. In this talk, I will share three stories about reproducibility from my year working with the scientists and statisticians at Houston Wastewater Epidemiology. The first story will be our journey to learn Quarto to build a rendered vignette website to document the R code accompanying a recent paper and how we incorporated student research assistants into the publication process. The second story will tell the tale of two Shiny apps with the same functionality but drastically different implementations. Finally, I will share how we developed a reproducibility review process for providing feedback on R code written by our group's scientists, drawing on experiences from the business world.

\#\# Schedule
4:30 \- 4:45 pm PDT \| Announcements
4:45 \- 5:45 pm PDT \| Speakers
5:45 \- 6:00 pm PDT \| Questions

**Sobre o Projeto CasaNem**

O projeto **CasaNem** é um centro de acolhimento da cidade do Rio de Janeiro que abriga pessoas LGBTQI+ em situação de vulnerabilidade social, em sua maioria pessoas transexuais e travestis, e que desenvolve programas e atividades em diversas vertentes com atividades focadas na autonomia e cultura dos seus moradores, além de realizar atendimentos e oferecer cursos e oficinas. Em 2020, a CasaNem atingia direta e indiretamente mais de 100 pessoas.

title: Preparamiento de datos masivos en R
description:
¡Bievenidos! En este Meetup abordaremos el manejo y procesamiento de datos masivos utilizando R.

Al momento de recopilar datos experimentales reales, privados o públicos, para su análisis e interpretación, es común toparnos con una pared: los datos a usar no están formateados de la misma manera, tiene una agrupación compacta o presentan errores tipográficos que evitan su correcta lectura y manipulación. En este minitaller, aprenderemos algunas herramientas para leer y/o escribir grandes volúmenes de datos con formatos similares, identificar errores de formato y lectura de archivos de datos, leer información de forma eficiente, idear estrategias para limpiar y organizar datos, obtener algunos estadísticos básicos de los datos y realizar graficaciones automatizadas masivas.

**REQUERIMIENTOS DE SOFTWARE:**
Tener instalado R (versión >= 3.4) y RStudio (versión >= 1.4) y los paquetes "tidyverse", "readxl" y "writexl", "pals" y "scales"

**DESCARGA EL MATERIAL PARA LA SESIÓN**
https://github.com/RLadiesCuerna/meetup_marzo_2024

**CÓDIGO DE CONDUCTA**
Recuerda leer y atender nuestro código de conducta disponible en https://rladies.org/code-of-conduct/ o https://rladiesmx.netlify.app/codigo_de_conducta/

description:
```
Junte-se ao R-Ladies Niterói para um workshop emocionante sobre como podemos desenvolver nosso potencial profissional com auxílio da linguagem R.
```

Este encontro é uma retomada das atividades do grupo. Vamos compartilhar experiências e insights sobre desafios e sucessos no trabalho remoto até explorar estratégias de desenvolvimento pessoal, aproveitar a tecnologia para o crescimento profissional e promover uma comunidade de apoio para encorajamento mútuo.

Nota: Este evento está aberto a todos os sexos e níveis de habilidade. Conhecimento prévio de programação R é recomendado, mas não obrigatório.
O evento será conduzido pela profa. Luciane Alcoforado, pela turismóloga Noelle Camelo e pela profa. Vanessa Manhães.

O encontro contará com a participação do prof. Dra. João Paulo que nos trará reflexões e experiências com o tema "Diversidade nas Ciências: Potencial Transformador do R" e com a profa. Vanessa Manhães com o tema "Desafios do Novo Ensino Médio e a Educação Estatística".

We're eager to learn about your interests and what you hope to gain from our community, and will tailor future events on what we hear from you at this initial meeting.

This event is free and open to the public, up to the RSVP limit, and we welcome all levels of R software practitioners. Whether you're a seasoned professional or completely new to R, we hope to build this community to provide networking opportunities and useful insights. Don't miss the chance to join a supportive community dedicated to promoting gender diversity in the R community.

title: Workshop - Introduction to R - Part 2
description:
To complete topics that we did not manage to fit into the first workshop, we will host the second part of the Introduction to R Workshop in our next Meetup. This time we will cover:

* Reading in data
* Basic data visualization
* R packages
* R Markdown for writing reports

The workshop is **open to everyone**, in particular also if you did not make it to the first part! To catch up you can look at the first few sections of the workshop material prepared by Pia: [Intro to R](https://htmlpreview.github.io/?https://github.com/piapfeiffer/intro-to-R/blob/main/intro_r.html) (up to Data Structures) or you could read through the Getting Started section of [https://moderndive.netlify.app/1-getting-started.html](https://moderndive.netlify.app/1-getting-started.html)

As before, we recommend you **bring your own laptop** where you have R and RStudio installed ([https://cran.r-project.org/](https://cran.r-project.org/) and [https://www.rstudio.com/products/rstudio/download/](https://www.rstudio.com/products/rstudio/download/)). In case you want to install Rmarkdown you can follow the instructions at [https://bookdown.org/yihui/rmarkdown/installation.html](https://bookdown.org/yihui/rmarkdown/installation.html)

A: Yes, men are welcome at this event. Our mission is to encourage more women and non-binary individuals to get involved in the R community. However, we ask that cis men come as guests of women and gender minorities to our events to promote a sense support and encouragement for women and gender minorities in the R community. Please help us by spreading the word about R-Ladies. Please also take the time to review our code of conduct before you attend: https://github.com/rladies/starter-kit/wiki/Code-of-Conduct

The term "women and gender minorities" is inclusive of individuals who identify as female (trans and cis), trans-men, non-binary, genderqueer, genderfluid, agender, intersex, and all minority genders).

Important to know:

title: R-Stories: The Role of Gender in Data Collection and Analysis
description:
A day before International Women's Day, we dedicate a **joint event of R-Ladies Zürich and the [Zurich R User Group](https://www.meetup.com/zurich-r-user-group/)** to hearing about experiences and considerations for when collecting and analysing data on gender and diversity.

Join us for an exciting panel discussion moderated by Luisa Barbanti (Zurich Data Scientists), featuring some very interesting guests:

* Luisa Coppola (ABB Schweiz AG)
* Luca M. Leisten (ETH Zurich)
* Almut Scherer (SCQM)
* Christina Seyler (UZH),
* and others.

The event will take place on **March 7th, 2023, 18:30 - 20:00** in room **KOL-E-13 EV** in the main building of the university of Zürich and will be followed by a networking apero.

Program:
18:30 - 18:45 Welcome and introduction of panellists
18:45 - 19:30 Panel discussion
19:30 - 19:50 Open discussion with audience
19:50 - 20:00 Wrap up
20:00 < Apero, first round sponsored by the Zurich R User Group

We welcome people of all sexual orientations and gender identities and are looking forward to lively discussions!

Last but not least, we ask you to only sign up either through R-Ladies Zürich or the [Zurich R User Group](https://www.meetup.com/zurich-r-user-group/) so that we can avoid double-booking.

title: Reproducible pipelines book club #6
description:
The 6th meeting of our book club where we are going through the chapters of [“Building reproducible analytical pipelines with R”](https://raps-with-r.dev/) by Bruno Rodrigues.

During this meeting, Julia Romanowska (co-founder of R-Ladies Bergen) will guide us through [chapter 10](https://raps-with-r.dev/repro_intro.html), where the [{renv}](https://rstudio.github.io/renv/articles/renv.html) package is presented. This chapter teaches how to keep track of various versions of packages one uses during an analysis.

This will be an online event where you are welcome to code along and discuss any questions or problems that might arise. We are all learning this together!

The meeting will not be recorded.

Check the next meetings in this book club and sign up to present one of the chapters that need a presenter.

title: RYouWithMe?
description:
Come join us for a **hands-on session on the basics of R and Rstudio**!

This month we will kick off with the first 3 modules of ***RYouWithMe,*** a series of online learning resources for using R - designed by us, for us. The series is oriented towards R beginners and aims to provide a foundation for future RLadies Sydney activities.

Does opening RStudio make you just want to close it again? Don’t worry, we are going to start at the VERY beginning: where to find your files, what you might use the console for, and where your script get written. From there, we’ll move on to data wrangling, visualisation, R language, and R Markdown.

Find it here: [https://rladiessydney.org/courses/ryouwithme/](https://rladiessydney.org/courses/ryouwithme/)

**How to get involved!**

As an R-Lady learner based in Sydney:

1. Join the R-Ladies Sydney Slack workspace ***[here](https://join.slack.com/t/rladies-sydney/shared_invite/zt-2czd4i8jq-qKuml1FeCHaJPuaYkRDXHQ)***;
2. Introduce yourself in the *#ryouwithme_intros_2024 channel*;
3. Keep an eye out in the *#ryouwithme_general_2024* channel for announcements;
4. Share your successes, ask questions, help out your peers in the *#ryouwithme_help_2024*.

You can start going through the Modules ***BasicBasics 1,2*** and ***3*** on your own and join us for questions, help and troubleshooting during the meetup.

Bring your laptop!

***R-Ladies Sydney organisers***
***Thank you Nous Group for supporting the RLadies!***
*Find us on Twitter @rladiessydney for more information on news, presentations and code!*
\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*
***Photos, Films and all other media/recordings***\*: Photographs and/or video/other media will be taken at events held by this community. By taking part in an R-Ladies Sydney event you grant the community organisers full rights to use the images resulting from the photography/video filming/media, and any reproductions or adaptations of the images for publicity, fund-raising or other purposes to help achieve the community’s aims. This might include (but is not limited to), the right to use them in their printed and online publicity, social media, press releases and funding applications. If you do not wish to be recorded in these media please inform a community organiser.\*
***Code of Conduct***\*: R-Ladies Sydney is dedicated to providing a welcoming and harassment-free event experience for everyone, regardless of gender, gender identity and expression, age, sexual orientation, disability, physical appearance, body size, race, ethnicity, or religion (or lack thereof). We do not tolerate harassment of event participants in any form, for any event venue, including talks, workshops, parties, Twitter and other online media.\*
***Community Policies***\*: Please note, cis men are welcome to join our meetups when they come as a guest of a woman participant or they bring a woman with them. We highly encourage speakers and teachers at R-Ladies Sydney events to be of minority genders.\*
